keysToEntries.Attack.Whetstone.Name: Whetstone
keysToEntries.Attack.Whetstone.Desc: Allows crafting of whetstones at the Crafting Table, which can be used to sharpen melee weapons for increased damage!
keysToEntries.Construction.CreatureComforts.Name: Creature Comforts
keysToEntries.Construction.CreatureComforts.Desc: Unlocks an assortment of furniture and decorations.
keysToEntries.Construction.EyeOfOculus.Name: Eye Of Oculus
keysToEntries.Construction.EyeOfOculus.Desc: Become the mystical Eye of Oculus and build Structures easier and with a whole new perspective!
keysToEntries.Construction.FineDetails.Name: Fine Details
keysToEntries.Construction.MiddleGround.Name: Middle Ground
keysToEntries.Construction.Oak.Name: Mighty Oak
keysToEntries.Construction.Oak.Desc: Unlocks the next tier of building pieces.
keysToEntries.Construction.PersonalChest.Name: Personal Chest
keysToEntries.Construction.PersonalChest.Desc: Access your Personal Storage chest from anywhere.
keysToEntries.Construction.SacredGeometry.Name: Sacred Geometry
keysToEntries.Construction.SummonShelter.Name: Summon Shelter
keysToEntries.Construction.SummonShelter.Desc: Construct a temporary Shelter through the power of Astral Magic. It even has a Bed!
keysToEntries.Cooking.BonesToPeaches.Name: Bones to Peaches
keysToEntries.Cooking.BonesToPeaches.Desc: Transmute unwanted bones on the ground into fresh Peaches for your consumption. Inhumane? Yes... but delicious!
keysToEntries.Cooking.Gastronomy.Name: Gastronomy
keysToEntries.Cooking.Gastronomy.Desc: Increased chance of cooking Delicious Food
keysToEntries.Cooking.FancyCookware.Name: Fancy Cookware
keysToEntries.Cooking.FancyCookware.Desc: Unlocks furniture piece Pan Rack in the building menu.
keysToEntries.Cooking.InternalAlchemy.Name: Internal Alchemy
keysToEntries.Cooking.InternalAlchemy.Desc: Refreshes any active Food and Drink buffs with a spectacular display of culinary appreciation.
keysToEntries.Cooking.YesChef.Name: Yes, Chef!
keysToEntries.Cooking.YesChef.Desc: Unlocks recipe for Chef's Hat, craftable at the Crafting Station.
keysToEntries.Mining.PrecissionStrikes.Name: Precision Strikes
keysToEntries.Mining.CrumbledWalls.Name: Mine Crumbled Walls
keysToEntries.Mining.CrumbledWalls.Desc: Allows you to knock down crumbled walls with a pickaxe, granting access to hidden or blocked areas.
keysToEntries.Mining.DetectOre.Name: Summon Stone Spirits
keysToEntries.Mining.DetectOre.Desc: Call on the Stone Spirits to guide you to the nearest rocks you can mine!
keysToEntries.Mining.GemProspector.Name: Gem Prospector
keysToEntries.Mining.GemProspector.Desc: Mining ore now sometimes produces precious gemstones.
keysToEntries.Mining.OreWeight.Name: Not-So-Heavy Metal
keysToEntries.Mining.OreWeight.Desc: The weight of metal ores is reduced by 25%
keysToEntries.Mining.Rocksplosion.Name: Rocksplosion
keysToEntries.Mining.Rocksplosion.Desc: Conjure a powerful blast to break apart stone boulders and ore.
keysToEntries.Mining.StoneWeight.Name: Lift With Your Hips
keysToEntries.Mining.StoneWeight.Desc: The weight of stone, clay, and sand raw material is reduced by 25%
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.EarthRunes.Name: Earth Runes
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.EarthRunes.Desc: Unlocks recipe for Earth Runes, craftable at the Rune Altar.
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.FireRunes.Desc: Unlocks recipe for Fire Runes, craftable at the Rune Altar.
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.FireRunes.Name: Fire Runes
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.FireSpirit.Name: Fire Spirit
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.FireSpirit.Desc: Conjure forth a fire spirit to light your way.
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.LawRunes.Name: Law Runes
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.LawRunes.Desc: Unlocks recipe for Law Runes, craftable at the Rune Altar.
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.NatureRunes.Name: Nature Runes
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.NatureRunes.Desc: Unlocks recipe for Nature Runes, craftable at the Rune Altar.
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.EssentialStrength.Name: Essential Strength
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.EssentialStrength.Desc: The weight of rune essence is reduced by 50%
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.Windstep.Name: Windstep
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.Windstep.Desc: Summons the power of Air Magic to Jump higher and Descend slower.
keysToEntries.Woodcutting.AxtralProjection.Name: Axtral Projection
keysToEntries.Woodcutting.AxtralProjection.Desc: Conjures an Astral Axe and launches it forward, instantly chopping down trees in its path.
keysToEntries.Woodcutting.BirdNests.Name: Bird Nests
keysToEntries.Woodcutting.BirdNests.Desc: It is now possible to find bird nests while felling trees. Bird nests contain useful items.
keysToEntries.Woodcutting.ChopVines.Name: Chop Thorny Vines
keysToEntries.Woodcutting.ChopVines.Desc: Allows you to cut down thorny vines with a hatchet, granting access to hidden or blocked areas.
keysToEntries.Woodcutting.LumberStorage.Name: Lumber Storage
keysToEntries.Woodcutting.LumberStorage.Desc: You can now build the lumber storage, a very high capacity storage for logs and planks.
keysToEntries.Woodcutting.OverheadChop.Name: Overhead Chop
keysToEntries.Woodcutting.OverheadChop.Desc: You can now perform an overhead chop, which deals greater damage against fallen logs, but very little damage against trees.
keysToEntries.Woodcutting.Splinter.Name: Splinter
keysToEntries.Woodcutting.Splinter.Desc: A powerful spell that chops fallen trees into wood logs.
keysToEntries.Woodcutting.LumberjackStamina.Name: Lumberjack Stamina
keysToEntries.Cooking.MinorNode.Stat1: Delicious Food Chance at the Cooking Range
keysToEntries.Magic.Confuse.Name: Confuse
keysToEntries.Magic.Confuse.Desc: Twist the minds of lesser enemies, making them unable to distinguish between friends and foes for a limited time.
keysToEntries.Magic.ExtendCombo.Name: Arcane Expertise
keysToEntries.Magic.ExtendCombo.Desc: Unlocks an additional powerful magical attack at the end of staff magic combos.
keysToEntries.Magic.FireMagic.Name: Fire Magic
keysToEntries.Magic.FireMagic.Desc: Wield the destructive forces of fire. Equip Fire runes to burn your enemies to ash in combat.
keysToEntries.Magic.LesserPotionMagic.Name: Magic Potion
keysToEntries.Magic.LesserPotionMagic.Desc: Unlock the recipe for the Magic Potion at the Brewing Cauldron.
keysToEntries.Magic.SecondaryAttacks.Name: Secondary Attacks
keysToEntries.Magic.SecondaryAttacks.Desc: Charge up and release powerful secondary staff spells.
keysToEntries.Magic.Surge.Name: Surge
keysToEntries.Magic.Surge.Desc: Channel arcane power into your dodges, travelling further and evading longer as a result.
keysToEntries.Magic.Surge.Upgrade.Desc: Distract your enemies by leaving behind an illusionary image for a short time when you use your first Surge Charge.
keysToEntries.Magic.Surge.Upgrade.Name: Afterimage
keysToEntries.Magic.MagicFocus.Desc: Allows crafting of magic focus at the Crafting Table, which can be used to imbue magic staves to increase their damage!
keysToEntries.Magic.MagicFocus.Name: Magic Focus
keysToEntries.Magic.RuneRefund.Name: Runic Refund
keysToEntries.Magic.RuneRefund.Desc: Your greater understanding of magic gives you a 30% chance to preserve runes when attacking with magic.
keysToEntries.Magic.GreaterConfuse.Name: Greater Confuse
keysToEntries.Magic.GreaterConfuse.Desc: The Confuse spell now works on a wider variety of creatures.
keysToEntries.Magic.StaminaStat.Name: Magic Stamina Stat
keysToEntries.Ranged.CorruptionArrows.Name: Spectral Arrows
keysToEntries.Ranged.CorruptionArrows.Desc: Enchant your equipped bow with Astral Magic, causing arrows to penetrate through multiple targets.
keysToEntries.Ranged.ShadowVeil.Name: Shadow Veil
keysToEntries.Ranged.ShadowVeil.Desc: Make yourself invisible to enemies for a short period.
keysToEntries.Ranged.SnareTrap.Name: Snare
keysToEntries.Ranged.SnareTrap.Desc: Conjure Earth Magic at your feet and lay a trap for your Enemies! Those caught inside this trap become rooted in place for a short time.
keysToEntries.Ranged.VenomousTrapper.Name: Venomous Trapper
keysToEntries.Ranged.VenomousTrapper.Desc: The Snare trap now triggers a cloud of poison when it is activated, causing enemies stuck inside it to become poisoned over time.
keysToEntries.Ranged.CorruptionShot.Name: Corruption Shot
keysToEntries.Ranged.CorruptionShot.Desc: Fully-charged Spectral Arrows additionally Corrupt the target, granting extra damage to hits from Spectral Arrows for a short period.
keysToEntries.Ranged.RangedProjectileRefundChance.Name: Ammo Recovery Chance
keysToEntries.Ranged.RangedProjectileRefundChance.Desc: Adds a chance to be able to retrieve your arrows or bolts from your enemy's loot when they die.
keysToEntries.Ranged.HuntersSense.Name: Hunter's Sense
keysToEntries.Ranged.HuntersSense.Desc: Crouch for a short time to activate your Hunter Senses, allowing you to sense nearby Creatures and Enemies.
keysToEntries.Ranged.QuickAction.Name: Quick Action
keysToEntries.Ranged.QuickAction.Desc: Perform Quick Actions with Ranged Weapons using the Primary Action button.
keysToEntries.Ranged.ToxicBehaviour.Name: Toxic Behaviour
keysToEntries.Ranged.ToxicBehaviour.Desc: Unlocks the recipe for Weapon Poison, which can be used to imbue melee weapons with Poison or to craft Poisonous Arrows.
keysToEntries.Ranged.MakeEmBleed.Name: Make 'em Bleed
keysToEntries.Ranged.MakeEmBleed.Desc: Unlocks the recipe for Weapon Barbs, which can be used to imbue melee weapons with Bleed or to craft Barbed Arrows.
keysToEntries.Ranged.ArcheryTarget.Name: Archery Target
keysToEntries.Ranged.ArcheryTarget.Desc: Build an Archery Target to practice your Ranged accuracy against. Shoot the Bullseye for a surprise!
keysToEntries.Ranged.TrickShot.Name: Trick Shot
keysToEntries.Ranged.TrickShot.Desc: After firing a fully draw arrow, release follow up shots at the right time for increased damage!
keysToEntries.Ranged.TrickShot.Upgrade.Name: Crossbow Trick Shot
keysToEntries.Ranged.TrickShot.Upgrade.Desc: Trick Shot now applies to Crossbow Weapons. Fire at the right time to instantly finish reloading and ready your next shot!
keysToEntries.Ranged.EnchantedCrossbowBolts.Name: Enchanted Crossbow Bolts
keysToEntries.Ranged.EnchantedCrossbowBolts.Desc: Unlocks the ability to craft Enchanted Crossbow Bolts. Enchanted bolts have a chance of triggering unique magical effects when being fired!
keysToEntries.Ranged.FireStarter.Name: Fire Starter
keysToEntries.Ranged.FireStarter.Desc: Unlocks the Fire Oil and Fire Arrows Recipes, giving you new ways to burn your enemies to ash!
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.WaterRunes.Name: Water Runes
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.WaterRunes.Desc: Unlocks recipe for Water Runes, craftable at the Rune Altar.
keysToEntries.Cooking.GastronomicGains.Name: Gastronomic Gains
keysToEntries.Cooking.GastronomicGains.T1.Description: Reduces the chance to Burn Power Level 1 Ingredients
keysToEntries.Cooking.GastronomicGains.T1T2.Descriptio: Reduces the chance to Burn Power Level 1 & 2 Ingredients
keysToEntries.Cooking.GastronomicGains.T2.Description: Reduces the chance to Burn Power Level 2 Ingredients
keysToEntries.Cooking.GastronomicGains.T2T3.Descriptio: Reduces the chance to Burn Power Level 2 & 3 Ingredients
keysToEntries.Cooking.GastronomicGains.T3.Description: Reduces the chance to Burn Power Level 3 Ingredients
keysToEntries.Cooking.ChanceToBurn.Major.T2.Name: Culinary Confidence: Power Level 2
keysToEntries.Cooking.ChanceToBurn.Major.T3.Name: Culinary Confidence: Power Level 3
keysToEntries.Cooking.AdeptCulinaromancer.Name: Adept Culinaromancer
keysToEntries.Cooking.Teamonger.Name: Teamonger
keysToEntries.Cooking.AmateurCulinaromancer.Name: Amateur Culinaromancer
keysToEntries.Cooking.AmateurCulinaromancer.Descriptio: Unlocks a collection of Perk granting recipes at the Stone Range and Range.
keysToEntries.Farming.Harvest.Name: Uproot
keysToEntries.Farming.Harvest.Desc: Channeling the power of the earth, summon a spectral surge that gently coaxes ripe plants from the soil. Within a small radius, all fully grown crops and wild plants are instantly harvested and drawn towards you
keysToEntries.Cooking.GrillMaster.Name: Grill Master
keysToEntries.Farming.Harvest.Upgrade.Name: Harvester
keysToEntries.Farming.Harvest.Upgrade.Desc: With greater mastery over the land, the caster’s reach extends even further. Harvester expands Uproot’s range to a wider radius
keysToEntries.Farming.Humidify.Name: Humidify
keysToEntries.Farming.Humidify.Desc: Conjure Water magic to create a rain cloud that travels forward, instantly watering any Farming Plot that it passes over with Clean Water.
keysToEntries.Cooking.ChanceToBurn.Major.T4.Name: Culinary Confidence: Power Level 4
keysToEntries.Cooking.GastronomicGains.T4.Description: Reduces the chance to Burn Power Level 4 Ingredients
keysToEntries.Construction.AdvancedStone.Name: Bastion
keysToEntries.RuneCrafting.FreeSiphonChance.Name: Free Siphon Chance
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.FreeSiphonChance.Desc: Increases the chance of Siphoning runes without spending Rune essence
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.SiphonBoostAirRune.Name: Air Rune Siphon Boost
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.SiphonBoostNatureRune.Name: Nature Rune Siphon Boost
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.SiphonBoostAstralRune.Name: Astral Rune Siphon Boost
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.SiphonBoostEarthRune.Name: Earth Rune Siphon Boost
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.SiphonBoostFireRune.Name: Fire Rune Siphon Boost
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.SiphonBoostWater.Name: Water Rune Siphon Boost
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.SiphonBoostLaw.Name: Law Rune Siphon Boost
keysToEntries.Runecrfting.PureEssence.Name: Pure Essence
keysToEntries.Runecrfting.PureEssence.Desc: Unlocks a chance of the Pure Essence Stone eruption while siphoning. It's hard to catch... But the reward is a burst of runes...
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.SiphonBoostAirRune.Desc: Siphoning Air Runes yields increased rewards
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.SiphonBoostAstralRune.Desc: Siphoning Astral Runes yields increased rewards
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.SiphonBoostEarthRune.Desc: Siphoning Earth Runes yields increased rewards
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.SiphonBoostFireRune.Desc: Siphoning Fire Runes yields increased rewards
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.SiphonBoostLawRune.Desc: Siphoning Law Runes yields increased rewards
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.SiphonBoostNatureRune.Desc: Siphoning Nature Runes yields increased rewards
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.SiphonBoostWaterRune.Desc: Siphoning Water Runes yields increased rewards
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.RuneToRuneEssence.Name: Runes To Rune Essence
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.RuneToRuneEssence.Desc: Too many runes of the wrong type and no essence around? Throw your runes on the ground, and this spell will draw out their magic, leaving you with 40% of the rune essence.
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.DetectAnima.Name: Summon Elemental Spirits
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.DetectAnima.Desc: Call on the Wisps to guide you to nearby Anima Vents you can siphon!
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.DetectConcentratedAnima.Des: The Summon Elemental Spirits perk now also indicates which Anima Vents are Concentrated.
keysToEntries.RuneCrafting.LesserAnimaSiphoning.Name: Lesser Anima Siphoning
keysToEntries.RuneCrafting.LesserAnimaSiphoning.Desc: Each Anima Vent siphon consumes 1 rune essence to produce 2 runes.
keysToEntries.RuneCrafting.GreaterAnimaSiphoning.Desc: Each Anima Vent siphon consumes 1 rune essence to produce 3 runes.
keysToEntries.RuneCrafting.GreaterAnimaSiphoning.Name: Greater Anima Siphoning
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.RuneToRuneEssenceUpgrade.De: As your mastery grows, so does your yield. You can now extract 60% of the rune essence.
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.AirRunes.Desc: Unlocks recipe for Air Runes, craftable at the Rune Altar.
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.AstralRunes.Desc: Unlocks recipe for Astral Runes, craftable at the Rune Altar.

keysToEntries.MiningLevelInstruction: Mine ore, clay or stone, or gather stones from the floor to level this skill.
keysToEntries.WoodcuttingLevelInstruction: Chop down trees, tear up saplings or collect logs from the floor to level this skill.
keysToEntries.ArtisanLevelInstruction: Craft new weapons, armour or tools to level this skill.
keysToEntries.ConstructionLevelInstruction: Build new structures to level this skill.
keysToEntries.AttackLevelInstruction: Defeat enemies with melee weapons to level this skill.
keysToEntries.MagicLevelInstruction: Defeat enemies with magic to level this skill.
keysToEntries.RangedLevelInstruction: Defeat enemies with ranged weapons to level this skill.
keysToEntries.RunecraftingLevelInstruction: Gather Rune Essence and craft Runes to level this skill.
keysToEntries.CookingLevelInstruction: Cook meals to level this skill.
keysToEntries.FarmingLevelInstruction: Grow crops to level this skill.

keysToEntries.DifficultySetting.ThirstKills.Descriptio: You can die from not drinking

keysToEntries.DifficultySetting.ResetLastCompletedXPLe: Any XP you had between achieved Skill Levels is lost upon death

keysToEntries.DifficultySetting.AllCraftingRecipesUnlo: Full access to all Crafting recipes

keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.AccessBuildMenu: Press {BuildMenuButton} to open the build menu.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.Encumbered: You are carrying too much. Keep an eye on the weight of everything in your inventory.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.AccessJournal: You've discovered a new recipe. Press {JournalButton} to see it in the journal.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.ProcessingFuelText: A good fire is a great way to keep warm, but you'll need to keep it burning. A steady supply of kindling stops it from burning out.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.CraftingStationText: Interact with workbenches to create new tools and weapons, as well as repairing old ones.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.AccessMap: You can open/close the map at any time by pressing {MapButton}.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.AccessInventory: Press {InventoryButton} to interact with your inventory.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.HealthRegen: When your health isn't as good as it could be, a good meal will help restore you and bolster you for the journey ahead.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.BuildingMaterialsText: You can convert logs into wooden planks, which can be used to construct a shelter.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.BuildingBeamsText: Adding support beams to your building will improve stability.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.ExploreText: Ashenfall is home to many useful resources, exploration will give you access to different materials.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.AccessSpells: Press {SpellBookButton} to open the spell book
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.CastSpells: Select a spell to cast it.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.SpellsAndRunesText: Spells require runes to cast them. They can be crafted using rune essence.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.RepairingItemsText: When an item breaks you can repair it at a crafting station.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.BedsText: A bed will let you rest and gives you somewhere to recover if you're too badly hurt.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.FatigueText: Avoid exhaustion by getting a good night's sleep, if you're too tired you'll find exploring much more difficult.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.CombatVsGoblinsText: Goblins? Aggressive critters. Keep your wits about you, damage avoided is better than damage blocked. Choose your attacks carefully.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.DodgeBlockAndParryControls:
1. {BlockButton} to block.
2. {EvadeButton} to dodge.
3. Blocking an enemy attack at the right moment will parry an enemy attack.
4. Enemies that have been parried take more damage.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.StealthArcheryText: A bow is great for taking our targets from the shadows. Try sneaking to make it harder for others to spot you.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.UpgradingWorkstationsText: Upgrading a workstation increases its functionality, allowing you to craft better equipment.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.NeedStrongerAxeText: Yeah, your axe isn't going to cut it – pardon the pun – against a tree like that. You'll need a stronger axe.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.RespawningLocationNoBed: If you have claimed a bed you'll return there, otherwise you'll be taken to the temple.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.RespawningLocationBed: As you have claimed a bed you'll return there, otherwise you'll be taken to the temple.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.RespawningText: You'll be whisked to safety on death, but your items will be left behind. {DescribeRespawningLocation}
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.FriendlyCharacters: Approach friendly characters to speak with them.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.CraftingItems.Title: Crafting Items
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.CraftingItems.Text: Crafting benches allow you to create tools and other items.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.PowerLevels.Title: Power Levels
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.PowerLevels.Text: You’re entering an area with stronger creatures and enemies. Craft better weapons and armour to improve your chances of survival.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.FirstNightText: The cold will lower your stamina, and the temperature drops harshly at night. Build a campfire to keep yourself warm.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.AccessSkills: Certain actions will award you XP in one of several skills. As you gain XP your skills will gain levels which will give you access to new features and powerful spells.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.DurabilityDropText: As you use tools, armour and weapons, they will fall into disrepair as their durability falls. When something has no durability left it becomes broken.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.FirstArmorEquipText: Your power level can be viewed in your inventory next to your equipped armour. The higher your power level, the better your chance of survival.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.HealthRegen.Title: Health Regen
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.RuneEssenceText: Runes are crafted using rune essence, which can be mined at rune geysers all over the land.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.RuneCraftingText: Runes can be crafted from rune essence at a rune altar.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.BetterGearText: As you venture further you will come across new materials which will unlock new recipes. Crafting better equipment will increase your chances of survival.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.Cooking.Title: Cooking
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.Cooking.Text: Raw food is rarely edible, build a campfire to cook food on. A good meal will improve your health and stamina.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.FirstArmorEquip.Text2: Enemy power levels are indicated next to their health bar. The higher the power level, the more dangerous the foe. Make sure you are properly equipped to face them.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.Repair.Title: Repairing
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.Repair.Text: You can repair weapons, tools and armor at a workbench, using resources you've gathered.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.Building.Stability.Title: Stability
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.Building.Stability.Text: You can't build just anywhere. Make sure your structure is stable enough to support itself. Structures are more stable if build near the ground.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.Beds.Shelter.Text: Some structures require a roof overhead. Make sure your giving yourself sufficient shelter.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.Beds.Shelter.Title: Shelter from the night
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.Beds.Claim.Title: Shelter from the night
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.Beds.Claim.Text: Sleeping will allow you to skip the night and alleviate fatigue.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.Building.Placement.Title: Building
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.Building.Placement.Text: You can rotate and adjust the distance and height of a building piece before you place it.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.Building.Unlocked.2.Title: Building
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.Building.Unlocked.2.Text: You need a workbench to craft tools.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.BackpackText: Items and resources will be stored in your backpack. Try not to overburden yourself by carrying too much
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.FoodBenenfitsText: Eating food provides Sustenance, which improves health regeneration.
keysToEntries.UI.FTUE.AccessSkills.Title: Skills

keysToEntries.FlavourText_Seed_Potato:
A Potato Seed. Equip and use them to scatter these into a farming plot and start growing!

Best Planted in Brynmoor plots.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Tool_PlantCurePotion: Used to cure diseased plants and bring them back to a healthy state.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Seed_Redberry:
A redberry seed. Equip and use them to scatter these into a farming plot and start growing!

Best Planted in Brynmoor plots.

A dwellberry seed. Equip and use them to scatter these into a farming plot and start growing!

Best Planted in Ghornfell plots.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Perk_Waterbarrel: Unlocks the Water Barrel, which stores Clean or Dirty Water collected from Watering Cans for later use.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Perk_Composter: Unlocks the Compost Bin and Compost Bucket recipes. These allow for creating and using Normal Compost to boost the yield and disease resistance of your plants.

keysToEntries.FlavourText_Perk_Scarecrow: Unlocks the Scarecrow, a perfect companion for your crops!
keysToEntries.Description_RainCatcher: Collects and filters rain water into usable Clean Water over time.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Perk_PlantCure: Unlocks the ability to create Plant Cures at the Cooking Range! Use Plant Cures to heal your plants from disease.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Perk_Recovery_Chance: Increases the chance of finding seeds when you harvest plants from both your farms and the world.

keysToEntries.FlavourText_Bucket_Wood:
{HeldContainerDescription}

A wooden bucket used to carry a variety of Compost. Pour it's contents out over a Farming Plot to Fertilize your plants!
keysToEntries.FlavourText_WateringCan_Bronze:
{HeldContainerDescription}

A bronze watering can. Can be filled at any water source.Used for watering crops in Farming Plots.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_WateringCan_Steel:
{HeldContainerDescription}

A Steel watering can. Can be filled at any water source. Used for watering crops in Farming Plots.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Composter: Deposit all of your unwanted food or plant matter to turn it into compost over time.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Building_WaterBarrel: A barrel for storing either Clean or Dirty Water, filled using Watering Cans.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Seed_Tree_Ash:
Ash tree seeds. 

Plant this directly into the ground to grow your own Tree!
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Seed_Tree_Oak:
Oak Tree Seeds. 

Plant this directly into the ground to grow your own Tree!
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Seed_Tree_Willow:
Willow Tree Seeds. 

Plant this directly into the ground to grow your own Tree!
keysToEntries.SpellDescription_Humidify: Conjure Water magic to create a rain cloud that travels forward, instantly watering any Farming Plot that it passes over with Clean Water.
keysToEntries.SpellDescription_Humidify_Upgrade: Upgrades the Humidify Spell, causing the conjured rain cloud to cure any diseased plants it passes over alongside watering them.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Tool_Secateurs: A pair of gardening Secateurs that boost your harvesting yield while equipped.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Perk_SpeedyFarmer: You've become more adapt at watering and composting your plants. You can now do so with less pentalty to your movement speed!
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Perk_TreeFarming: You are skilled enough at farming to now grow Trees! Trees can be planted directly into the ground and seeds can be found while chopping down trees and from birds nests.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Building_Well: Unlocks the ability to build a Well! A Well provides a limited amount of Clean Water per day. Wells refill in the morning.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Tool_Shovel_Bronze:
A basic Spade made from Bronze. 

Used to clear farming plots.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Tool_Shovel_Steel:
A robust Spade made from Steel. 

Used to clear farming plots.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Tool_Shovel_Wood:
A rudimentary Spade made from Ash wood. 

Used to clear farming plots.
keysToEntries.Notification_InvalidPlotForSeed: The seed will not grow in this plot
keysToEntries.Notification_InvalidShovelForPlot: The Spade is not strong enough to dig in this plot.
keysToEntries.Notification_PlotIncorrectStateForPlanti: The plot has to be empty to plant a seed
keysToEntries.Notification_CannotDestroyContainerNotEm: Only empty containers can be destroyed.
keysToEntries.Description_Well: Allows you to draw a limited amount of Clean Water per day. Wells refill in the morning.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Perk_Waterbarrel_Large: Unlocks the Large Water Barrel, which stores even more Clean or Dirty Water collected from Watering Cans for later use.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Building_WaterBarrel_Large: A large barrel for storing greater amounts of Clean or Dirty Water, filled using Watering Cans.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_Perk_ComposterSuper: Unlocks the Super Compost Bin which produces Super Compost, for an even better yield boost and disease resistance of your plants.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_ComposterSuper: Deposit all of your unwanted nutrient rich food or plant matter to turn it into Super Compost over time.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_WateringCan_Wood:
{HeldContainerDescription}

A Watering Can made from Ash Wood. Can be filled at any water source. Used for watering crops in Farming Plots.

keysToEntries.Whetstone: Use this to hone your equipped melee weapon.
keysToEntries.MagicFocus: Imbue your staff to increase the damage of your magical attacks for a limited time.
keysToEntries.MagicPotion: Consume this potion to increase your critical chance with magical attacks.
keysToEntries.WeaponPoison:
A vial of Weapon Poison. 

Used in crafting Poison Arrows or to coat your equipped Melee Weapon with Poison for a short time.
keysToEntries.WeaponBarbs:
Pointy! 

Used to craft Barbed Arrows or to coat your equipped melee weapon with barbs, causing Bleed buildup for a short duration.
keysToEntries.EnchantedBronzeCrossbowBolts: Bronze crossbow bolts tipped with enchanted opal, that have a chance to summon a lightning strike around your target, dealing Air magic damage to all nearby enemies.
keysToEntries.EnchantedIronCrossbowBolts: Iron crossbow bolts tipped with enchanted Jade, that have a chance of dealing Water magic damage and slowing your target.
keysToEntries.EnchantedBluriteCrossbowBolts: Blurite crossbow bolts tipped with enchanted Jade, that have a chance of dealing Earth magic damage and knocking your target down.
keysToEntries.FireOil: Throw this at your enemies to set them on fire!
keysToEntries.BluriteCrossbow: A crossbow made from Blurite, thought to be a myth.
keysToEntries.BronzeLimbs: A pair of bronze crossbow limbs. Used in crafting a Bronze Crossbow.
keysToEntries.IronLimbs: A pair of iron crossbow limbs. Used in crafting an Iron Crossbow.
keysToEntries.BluriteLimbs: A pair of blurite crossbow limbs. Used in crafting a Blurite Crossbow.
keysToEntries.BluriteBar: A rare bar of refined Blurite, thought to be myth.
keysToEntries.BluriteOre: A chunk of rare and unrefined Blurite.
keysToEntries.PoisonBoneArrows: Arrows with tips carved from animal fangs coated in Weapon Poison.
keysToEntries.PoisonBronzeArrows: Arrows with tips forged from bronze coated in Weapon Poison.
keysToEntries.PoisonIronArrows: Arrows with tips forged from iron coated in Weapon Poison.
keysToEntries.BarbedBronzeArrows: Lacerating arrows with barbed tips forged from bronze.
keysToEntries.BarbedIronArrows: Lacerating arrows with barbed tips forged from iron.
keysToEntries.FireBronzeArrows: Burning arrows with tips forged from bronze coated in fire oil.
keysToEntries.FireIronArrows: Burning arrows with tips forged from iron coated in fire oil.
keysToEntries.FireOilNonThrowable:
Flammable and volatile, handle with care!

Used to craft Fire arrows.
keysToEntries.Shrapnel: A collection of various sharp objects.
keysToEntries.Airrune:
Equippable as ammo when using a Magic Weapon.

A symbol of blustering winds, essential for air-based magic and other spells. 

Runes are retained if you happen to die.
keysToEntries.AstralRune:
Astral runes are used for casting advanced magic spells and attacks. 

Runes are retained if you happen to die.
keysToEntries.EarthRune:
A symbol of the earth's might, essential for earth-based magic. 

Runes are retained if you happen to die.
keysToEntries.FireRune:
Equippable as ammo when using a Magic Weapon (provided the required Skill Perk is unlocked).

A symbol of blazing heat, essential for fire-based magic. 

Runes are retained if you happen to die.
keysToEntries.LawRune:
Resource needed for teleportation.

Runes are retained if you happen to die.
keysToEntries.NatureRune:
Nature runes are used for casting transmutation spells. 

Runes are retained if you happen to die.
keysToEntries.WaterRune:
A symbol of flowing waters, essential for water-based magic and other spells. 

Runes are retained if you happen to die.
keysToEntries.Vestige.DragonkinRobe: An ancient robe, tattered beyond repair, but perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.PaladinArmour: The remnants of armour worn long ago by Saradominst knights. Perhaps you can learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.WildArcher: Scraps of leather, embroidered with curious patterns. Perhaps you can learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.DragonkinRobeHood: A hood, worn to the barest of threads by the passage of time. Perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.PaladinHelm: Barely more than metal scraps and time-worn leather. Still, perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.BagofNoggin: An odd bundle of scratchy sackcloth. It's unsettling to hold. Still, perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.WildArcherHelm: A leathery coif, long worn away into barely more than memories. Still, perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.DragonkinRobeLegs: Tattered remnants of ancient robes. Perhaps you could learn something from them.
keysToEntries.Vestige.Bootsoflightness: Barely heavier than a feather, but worn away by time's cruel march. Still, perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.PaladinLegs: Nothing but scrap metal now. Still, perhaps you could learn something from them.
keysToEntries.Vestige.WildArcherLegs: Leathery scraps, victims of entropy's touch. Still, perhaps you could learn something from them.
keysToEntries.Vestige.ShatteredSkull: A skull, obliterated by a particularly nasty weapon. If you study it, perhaps you might learn something.
keysToEntries.Vestige.Swingslash: A rusted blade with a wicked edge. Barely usable now, but perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.BluriteCrossbow: A contraption made from strange blue metal. Perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.DragonDagger: Remants of a dragon's tooth, perhaps? Looks like it was carved. Perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.ThaneGreataxe: A powerful looking blade, broken beyond repair. Still, perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.AdventurersLongbow: The remains of a longbow, damaged in a dragon attack. Perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.HunterStagBow: Sinew, bound tightly enough to be dangerous. Useless now, but perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.ShortbowWildScouts: The last few splinters of an ancient shortbow. Perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.DragonkinStaff: Although damaged beyond repair, this staff seethes with ancient power. Perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.GrievingmoonStaff: What's left of a staff that feels soaked in sorrow. Perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.ChieftainsBlade: The rusted remains of a symbol of authority. Perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.TrainingBlade: A crude wooden blade and handle; what's left of a child's toy turned to weaponry. Perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.SteelDagger: A fine dagger made from high quality Steel.
keysToEntries.SteelSword: For fighting monsters and swearing Oaths.
keysToEntries.SteelScimitar: A vicious, curved sword of Eastern Origins forged from Steel.
keysToEntries.SteelMace: A brutal crush weapon forged from high quality Steel.
keysToEntries.SteelGreatsword: A two handed Steel Sword made famous by Highland Warriors.
keysToEntries.SteelGreataxe: A two handed Battleaxe forged from Steel.
keysToEntries.SteelWarhammer: A massive weapon made from Steel. Great for hammering really big nails.
keysToEntries.WillowLongbow: A precise Longbow crafted from Willow.
keysToEntries.WillowShortbow: An agile Shortbow crafted from Willow.
keysToEntries.SteelArrows: Arrows with tips forged from Steel.
keysToEntries.PoisonedSteelArrows: Arrows with tips forged from Steel coated in Weapon Poison.
keysToEntries.SteelBarbedArrows: Lacerating arrows with barbed tips forged from Steel.
keysToEntries.SteelFireArrows: Burning arrows with tips forged from Steel coated in fire oil.
keysToEntries.EnchantedSteelBolts: Steel crossbow bolts tipped with enchanted Red Topaz. Has a chance to create a necrotic bolt that heals your lifeward for a proportion of the damage dealt.
keysToEntries.SplitbarkStaff: A Mage's Weapon crafted from Wither rotted Hollow Bark. It has Strange Magical Properties.
keysToEntries.WolfbaneDagger: A silver dagger that brings fear to all those who howl in the night.
keysToEntries.ShadowSword: This Greatsword exsists between the realms of the living and the dead.
keysToEntries.DorgeshuunCrossbow: The signature weapon of the Dorgeshuun Goblin tribe. It fires Bone bolts and is surprisingly well made!
keysToEntries.BoneBolts: Lightweight, but very sharp. Can be fired using the Dorgeshuun Crossbow.
keysToEntries.UndeadRangersBow: This bow still fires with undeadly precision.
keysToEntries.NecromancersStaff: Necromantic essence still eminates from this staff.
keysToEntries.ImarusTalon: A Scimitar forged from the Visage of Imaru.
keysToEntries.SteelFullHelm: A full helm forged from Steel for Melee users.
keysToEntries.SteelPlatebody: A strong set of well crafted Steel Platemail for Melee users.
keysToEntries.SteelPlatelegs: Sturdy and well-made Steel leg armour for Melee users.
keysToEntries.GreenDragonhideCoif: Lightweight Ranger headgear crafted from Green Dragonhide for Ranged users
keysToEntries.GreenDragonhideBody: Ranged body armour crafted from Green Dragonhide. Form fitting yet intimidating.
keysToEntries.GreenDragonhideChaps: Agile Ranged leg armour crafted from Green Dragonhide.
keysToEntries.SplitbarkHelm: Magical head armour crafted from wither corrupted Hollow Bark.
keysToEntries.SplitbarkBody: Magical armour crafted from Fine Cloth and Hollow Bark. It has strange magical properties.
keysToEntries.SplitbarkLegs: Magical leg armour armour crafted from wither corrupted Hollow Bark.
keysToEntries.WhiteFullHelm: A helmet worn by a member of the Holy Order of White Knights. It is pure white
keysToEntries.WhitePlatebody: White platemail armour worn by a member of the Holy Order of White Knights.
keysToEntries.WhitePlatelegs: White Platelegs worn by a member of the Holy Order of White Knights.
keysToEntries.RangerHat: Ranger head gear. Wearing this gives you the urge to rob from the rich to help the poor for some reason.
keysToEntries.RangerTunic: Ranger armour that's as dangerous as it is dapper!
keysToEntries.RangerTights: Ranger leg gear that makes you feel both agile and slightly rebellious.
keysToEntries.NecromancersCrown: Magical headgear for those that rule over their undead minions.
keysToEntries.NecromancersRobeTop: Magical robes for those who believe that death is only the beginning.
keysToEntries.NecromancersRobeBottom: Magical robe bottoms for Sorcerer's that have no concept of ethics.
keysToEntries.FallenHoplitesAspis: The shield of an Undead Hoplite.
keysToEntries.SalveAmulet: A powerful amulet made from a crystal that has absorbed the magical blessings of the River Salve.
keysToEntries.ImaruShield: The Spirit of Imaru lives on within this shield.
keysToEntries.FallenHoplitesHelm: A Melee helmet once belonging to a long dead Saradomin Hoplite.
keysToEntries.FallenHoplitesChest: Melee chest armour once belonging to a long dead Saradomin Hoplite.
keysToEntries.FallenHoplitesTassets: Melee leg armour once belonging to a long dead Saradomin Hoplite.
keysToEntries.FellhollowCape: A cloak depicting the ethereal beauty of Fellhollow.
keysToEntries.ShadowscaleCape: A cape worn by those from the lands of death and decay.

keysToEntries.SteelLimbs: A pair of Steel crossbow limbs. Used in crafting a Steel Crossbow.
keysToEntries.DorgeshuunLimbs: Crossbow limbs made from powerful bone. Used in crafting the Dorgeshuun Crossbow.
keysToEntries.SteelLoggingAxe: A woodcutter's tool, forged from Steel.

keysToEntries.SteelShield: A reliable, durable shield crafted from Steel, perfect for tanking big hits.

keysToEntries.GarouPack: A brutal bag sewn from hide and sinew.
keysToEntries.GoblinPack: A cloth shoulder bag, grimy and poorly kept.

keysToEntries.ZombiePack: A moist and squishly bag of bits.
keysToEntries.ZogrePack: A foul smelling sack hewn from human skin.
keysToEntries.Vestige.HeadWhite: A battered helmet. Perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.UndeadBone: Dropped from the restless dead. Use as a crafting material, or process at a grindstone.

keysToEntries.Ectoplasm: Dropped from aggressive spirits or extracted from wither hearts. Used in crafting.

keysToEntries.WitheredHeart: Dropped from withered enemies. Used as a crafting ingredient.
keysToEntries.Pumpkin: More treat than trick. Can be cooked by itself or used as an ingredient in cooking meals.
keysToEntries.Cadavaberries: Can be eaten raw, but wouldn't recommend it. Can be cooked alone or as an ingredient in cooking meals.
keysToEntries.Irit: Havested from Thorny Irit Roots, used in potion making at the brewing cauldron.
keysToEntries.HollowBark: Found lying around Fellhollow, around long-dead trees. Used in the crafting.
keysToEntries.WillowLogs: A highly efficient fuel. Required for smelting more advanced metals.

keysToEntries.DragonBlood: Harvested from dragons and dragonwolves. Used as a tanning agent at the tannery.

keysToEntries.GreenDragonhideLeather: Made by tanning green dragonhide. A key crafting component for armour.

keysToEntries.DraconicLeather: A sturdy leather made from draconic hide, or from tanning hard leather with dragon blood.
keysToEntries.PrimordialHeart: The heart of a nature spirit, you hunted this for Vannaka. He wants three of them.
keysToEntries.Trinket.RingOfRecoil.Description: A PERCENTAGE of DAMAGE received is DEALT back to ATTACKERS
keysToEntries.Trinket.RingOfPursuit.Description: ANIMALS are less AWARE of your PRESENCE
keysToEntries.Trinket.RingOfLife.Description: When close to DEATH, TELEPORTS you to your current BED
keysToEntries.DriedCadavaberries: Probably not poisonous. A neat little snack.
keysToEntries.StoneBlock: A building material. Used for advanced construction.
keysToEntries.Cursefragment.Emberwood: A crystallised part of a curse, imbued with Imaru's grief.
keysToEntries.Cursefragment.Silverthorn: A crystallised part of a curse, imbued with Imaru's despair.

keysToEntries.SoulFragment: Someone may have use for this.
keysToEntries.RelentlesssInfusion: A tasty tipple that'll put a quiver in your quiver.
keysToEntries.WitherWater: A source of hydration, tainted by the realm of Imaru.

keysToEntries.DraconicVisage: The image of a fearsome dragon. Used in crafting powerful equipment.
keysToEntries.VegballSweet: A marvelous amalgamation of sweet and savoury.
keysToEntries.TomatoGrilled: Fire really brings out the flavour.
keysToEntries.PumpkinSpiceInfusion: A seasonal treat, popular with some.
keysToEntries.GenericBurnt: Too well done, but still edible.
keysToEntries.CadavaberryInfusion: A refreshing fruity drink. Excellent for hydration.
keysToEntries.SteakAndEggs: A marvelous way, to start your day.
keysToEntries.UndeadSteak: A technically edible steak. Morally? That's another question
keysToEntries.bread: Crusty brown outside, pillowy white inside.
keysToEntries.Potion.WeakHealing: A potion that restores a small amount of health.
keysToEntries.Potion.LesserHealing: A potion that restores a moderate amount of health.
keysToEntries.FuelPellet: A high temperature fuel. Needed to smelt more advanced metals.
keysToEntries.EggFried: Eggs, sunny side up, a nutritious snack.
keysToEntries.PungentOmelette: Tastes much better than it's name suggests.
keysToEntries.VeganFryup: All of the calories, none of the guilt.
keysToEntries.FruitPie: A fruit party, wrapped in golden pastry.
keysToEntries.Flour: Cultivated grains ground to a fine powder.
keysToEntries.ImarusHead: Proof of your triumph over the Soul-Eater. It's dripping ectoplasm all over, you should probably get rid of it...
keysToEntries.AnimalHorn: A cartilaginous extension of an animal skull.
keysToEntries.WintumberTree: A decorative tree with which to celebrate the festive season.

keysToEntries.Vestige.DeathsShopeGeneric.Other: The tattered pieces of a mysterious object. Perhaps you could learn something from them.

keysToEntries.Vestige.ZombieAxe: A very old and rusted axe, well beyond repair. Still, perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.UndeadShovel: The sinister remains of farming equipment. Perhaps you can learn something from it.

keysToEntries.Alric: The archmage and lead demon summoner of Dowdun Reach.

keysToEntries.AnglersHat: A rather dapper looking cap that also reduces the chance of gaining junk while fishing.
keysToEntries.AnimatedFeather: To a fish, this feather looks like a long-lost love.

keysToEntries.BlackKnightCowl: A ranged headpiece worn by the Children of Shadows.
keysToEntries.BlackKnightCossbow: A crossbow-wielding member of the Children of Shadows.
keysToEntries.BlackKnightGuardian: Guarding the Black Knights from people like you.

keysToEntries.BlackKnightHoundmaster: A black knight who has really gone to the dogs.

keysToEntries.BlackKnightMelee: A black knight who is tired of you farming them for their drops.

keysToEntries.DoubleMeatSandwich: Meat, meat and more meat. A sandwich with flank steak, rat roast and dried redberries.
keysToEntries.DowdunReachCape: There is no better display of mastery of the Dowdun Reach area.
keysToEntries.DreamShard: Used to craft magical equipment. Postie Pete also has a use for them.
keysToEntries.DriedKrill: Krills hunger with one bite. Grants the Sea Legs perk.
keysToEntries.EggsRoyale: Made by combining fried egg and fried salmon. Grants the Sea Life perk.
keysToEntries.EnchantedMithrilCrossbowBolts: These crossbow bolts offer a chance to create a toxic burst, poisoning all nearby enemies.
keysToEntries.FeatherBait: What is it about feathers that fascinates a fish? A great way to bait them.
keysToEntries.FeatheredLure: Entice fish with a knotted ball of bird knock-offs.
keysToEntries.Fido: Its staring eyes seem to be burrowing deep into your subconscious.
keysToEntries.FishFry: Made by combining roasted lobster, braised undead eel and fried cabbage.
keysToEntries.FriedSalmon: For when you feel like a hungry bear. Grants the Sea Life perk.
keysToEntries.FriedSardine: Delicious little fishies. Grants the Warding Fish perk.
keysToEntries.GiantKrill: Look at the size of that krill! Can be dried and eaten.
keysToEntries.GlassVessel: A glass item made by glassblowing.
keysToEntries.GlazedRoastPumpkinSandwich: A warming dish made with roast pumpkin, maple syrup and dried redberry.
keysToEntries.Gnasher: It seems to enjoy fighting you, going by the happy eyes and lolling tongue.
keysToEntries.GrilledTrout: Stave off hunger with this tasty bite. Grants the Sea Life perk.
keysToEntries.GrilledUndeadBass: Undead and makes you unhungry. Grants the Sea Life perk.
keysToEntries.GroundLimestone: Ground from limestone. With the proper tools, it can be made into glass at a furnace.
keysToEntries.HealingPotion: Recovers some lost health. Made with irit and redberries.
keysToEntries.Hellhound: A canine-shaped demon from the plane of Infernus.
keysToEntries.HerbBakedTrout: Made by combining grilled trout and a baked potato. Grants the Sea Life perk.
keysToEntries.Herring: A medium-sized fish. Its flavours emerge when smoked.
keysToEntries.HoundmasterRexiton: Has the complete trust of his hellhounds.
keysToEntries.InfernalEel: Slippery and sulphurous. Smoke it to make it edible.
keysToEntries.KrillBait: If I told you why this bait works, I'd have to krill you.
keysToEntries.LesserAshes: There seems to be something solid within the ash pile.
keysToEntries.LesserBlueDragon: Let's hope you remembered to bring your antifire potion.
keysToEntries.LesserDemon: Lesser only in name. Can carve noughts and crosses into your belly.
keysToEntries.LesserFocusedFishingPotion: Grants a boost to your Fishing XP for a short period of time.
keysToEntries.Limestone: A building material. It can also be ground into dust.
keysToEntries.LimestoneRock: Can be mined for limestone, a hardy building material.
keysToEntries.LinenNet: Made with two fine threads at a crafting table. An elegant little net.
keysToEntries.Lobster: You've just been lobstered. Delectable when roasted.
keysToEntries.LobsterBait: Scraps of lobster meat made into bait to draw in fish.
keysToEntries.LoyalHellhound: A hellhound that has been broken to the point that it will obey the word of Rexiton.
keysToEntries.MageOfZamorak: Just as likely to set themselves alight as you.
keysToEntries.MapleLogs: Cut from maple trees. Can be crafted into various equipment.
keysToEntries.MapleLongbow: A supple longbow carved from maple wood.
keysToEntries.MapleRod: Crafted from a maple log and some mystic thread. A bad-ass rod.
keysToEntries.MapleSap: Harvested from maple trees. An ingredient that can be cooked on a campfire.
keysToEntries.MapleShortbow: A limber shortbow carved from maple wood
keysToEntries.MapleStaff: A magical weapon carved from maple wood.
keysToEntries.MapleSyrup: A sweet and nutty syrup. An ingredient for various dishes.
keysToEntries.MapleToastie: A sweet lunch made with maple syrup and dried redberry.
keysToEntries.MapleTree: A versatile tree that supplies maple sap and maple logs.
keysToEntries.MeatSandwich: A carnivore's favourite, made with flank steak and rat roast.
keysToEntries.MithrilArrows: Arrows with tips forged from mithril.
keysToEntries.MithrilBar: A versatile metal used for crafting weapons and armour.
keysToEntries.MithrilCrossbow: A one-handed crossbow composed mainly from mithril.
keysToEntries.MithrilCrossbowLimbs: A pair of mithril crossbow limbs. Essential for the crafting of a mithril crossbow.
keysToEntries.MithrilDagger: A concealable weapon, perfect for backstabbing your enemies.
keysToEntries.MithrilDeposit: A rich seam that can be mined for mithril.
keysToEntries.MithrilGreataxe: A two-handed battleaxe forged from mithril.
keysToEntries.MithrilGreatsword: A sturdy two-handed sword, with strength to match its size.
keysToEntries.MithrilHelmet: Protect your head with this durable mithril headgear.
keysToEntries.MithrilLoggingAxe: A tool for chopping trees. Make sure to hit them with the sharp side.
keysToEntries.MithrilMace: Crush your foes with this heavy, spiked mithril mace.
keysToEntries.MithrilOre: Mined from Mithril deposits. It can be smelted into bars at a furnace.
keysToEntries.MithrilPickaxe: A tool for mining. Few rocks can withstand its strength.
keysToEntries.MithrilPlatebody: A heavy mithril plate, providing welcome protection in battle.
keysToEntries.MithrilScimitar: A curved sword of Eastern influence, forged from mithril.
keysToEntries.MithrilShield: A sturdy shield crafted from mithril. Perfect for holding your ground.
keysToEntries.MithrilSword: A well-forged mithril blade for trained combatants.
keysToEntries.MithrilWarhammer: An imposing two-handed weapon, capable of crushing any enemy.
keysToEntries.MoltenGlass: Raw material used in the art of glassblowing.
keysToEntries.MysticCloth: A magical textile, woven from mythic thread.
keysToEntries.MysticFibre: Dropped from Zamorak magi. Can be spun at a spinning wheel into mystic thread.
keysToEntries.MysticHood: A magically resonant hood, perfect for staying warm.
keysToEntries.MysticNet: Dropped from Zamorak magi. Can be spun at a spinning wheel into mystic thread.
keysToEntries.MysticRobe: The finest magical thread, spun into fabulous mage gear.
keysToEntries.MysticRobeLegs: Lightweight magical gear for the aspiring sorcerer.
keysToEntries.MysticThread: Spun from mystic fibre. Magic courses through every thread.
keysToEntries.Nosey: Believes that the amount of cologne you put on makes you MILDLY FLAMMABLE.
keysToEntries.OakRod: Crafted from an oak log and some wool thread. A favourite fishing rod.
keysToEntries.Patch: Patch would like you to AVOID LINGERING EYE CONTACT please.
keysToEntries.Pip: Is tired of hellrats taking the mickey out of her HIDEOUS HYPOCHONDRIA.
keysToEntries.RatMasterSkrexis: Skrexis has likely built up quite the immune system.
keysToEntries.Raven: One well-aimed crossbow bolt would take this spy down.
keysToEntries.RawAnchovies: Doesn't look like much now, but sear it on a bonfire and receive a delicious treat.
keysToEntries.RawArmouredCatfish: Should be de-shelled before being braised.
keysToEntries.RawGiantKrill: A colossal crustacean. A little soggy now, so best dry it.
keysToEntries.RawHerring: Cooked on an anvil. Which may or may not be a red herring.
keysToEntries.RawInfernalEel: Wriggly no more. Will need to be smoked before it goes near your palate.
keysToEntries.RawLobster: Don't worry, it's not alive anymore. For best results, roast.
keysToEntries.RawSalmon: While it might be edible now, you'll need to fry it in this game.
keysToEntries.RawSardine: A small fish that was brought into this world to be fried and eaten.
keysToEntries.RawShrimp: Schrodinger's shrimp: could end up burnt or fried.
keysToEntries.RawTrout: Grill this trout on a fire. You will thank us later.
keysToEntries.RawUndeadBass: It may not look edible, but trust us: grill it and you're in for a treat.
keysToEntries.RawUndeadEel: A dead undead eel. We suggest you braise it.
keysToEntries.Reek: Would like your UNFETTERED ATTENTION: could you give him a different name, please?
keysToEntries.RoastedLobster: Does wonders for your hunger bar. Grants the Sea Legs perk.
keysToEntries.Salmon: Caught while moving upstream. Delicious when fried.
keysToEntries.Sardine: A small fish that travels in shoals. Catch one or two and fry them.
keysToEntries.SauteedSardines: Made by combining fried sardine and dried redberry. Grants the Warding Fish perk.
keysToEntries.SauteedShrimp: A simple meal but one that will hold back the hunger. Grants the Sea Legs perk.
keysToEntries.SearedAnchovies: Hold hunger back until lunch. Grants the Sea Legs perk.
keysToEntries.SergeantBlister: A high-ranking Black Knight who would rather you scarpered quick-smart.
keysToEntries.Shrimp: The simplest of all creatures to angle. Catch it and sautee it.
keysToEntries.ShrimpBait: Scraps of shrimp dressed to be eaten by fish.
keysToEntries.ShrimpOmelette: Made by combining fried egg and sauteed shrimp. Grants the Sea Legs perk.
keysToEntries.SmallAshes: There seems to be something solid within the ash pile.
keysToEntries.SmokedHerring: Nothing beats hunger like a herring. Grants the Warding Fish perk.
keysToEntries.SmokedInfernalEel: An unusual meal, but a good one. Grants the Warding Fish perk.
keysToEntries.SpookyBait: Bait that gives fish the heeby-jeebies, but draws them in anyway.
keysToEntries.StandardArtisanPotion: Increases XP gain for the Artisan skill. Made with irit and necrotic bonemeal.
keysToEntries.StandardAttackPotion: Increases XP gain for the Attack skill. Made with irit and small animal fang.
keysToEntries.StandardConstructionPotion: Increases XP gain for the Construction skill. Made with irit and ground sandstone.
keysToEntries.StandardCookingPotion: Increases XP gain for the Cooking skill. Made with irit and cabbage.
keysToEntries.StandardMiningPotion: Increases XP gain for the Mining skill. Made with irit and undead bone.
keysToEntries.StandardRunecraftingPotion: Increases XP gain for the Runecrafting skill. Made with irit and coarse animal fur.
keysToEntries.StandardWoodcuttingPotion: Increases XP gain for the Woodcutting skill. Made with irit and feathers.
keysToEntries.SteakSandwich: A delectable treat made with flank steak, fried onions and grilled mushrooms.
keysToEntries.StirFry: Made by combining dried giant krill, dried redberry and smoked infernal eel.
keysToEntries.StuffedCatfish: Made by combining dried giant krill, fried cabbage and a braised catfish.
keysToEntries.SubjugationStaff: A powerful battlestaff, once owned and used by Archmage Alric.
keysToEntries.SummonerFlament: A demon summoner who has lost his mind to the Dreaming Stone.
keysToEntries.SuperLumberjackPotion: Applies the Lumberjack perk, which helps to decrease stamina lost from Woodcutting.
keysToEntries.SuperQuarrymasterPotion: Applies the Quarrymaster perk, which helps to decrease stamina lost from Mining.
keysToEntries.Thammarith: A rather imposing looking demon who seems distracted by something.
keysToEntries.TitansWrath: This sword is now thrice-owned: by you, the Black Knight Titan and Lord Rasmodel.
keysToEntries.Trout: A sizeable catch. Grill a trout and treat your belly.
keysToEntries.UndeadBass: A bass that was dropped and is now undead. Can be grilled.
keysToEntries.UndeadDelight: Made by combining fried grilled undead bass and grilled tomato. Grants the Sea Life perk.
keysToEntries.UndeadEel: Life isn't even sacred for a poor eel. An acquired taste when braised.
keysToEntries.UnholySymbol: A necklace that proudlty displays the Zamorak symbol. Don't wear it to church.
keysToEntries.VeggieSandwich: A hunger-reducing snack made with fried cabbages and grilled mushrooms.
keysToEntries.VileAshes: Remains of a demonic creature. A crafting material used to make demonic oil.
keysToEntries.WeakFocusedFishingPotion: Grants a boost to your Fishing XP for a short period of time.
keysToEntries.WillowRod: Crafted from a willow log and some fine thread. A professional-looking fishing rod.
keysToEntries.WoolNet: Made with two wool threads at a crafting table. Unwieldy but does a job.
keysToEntries.ZamorakCape: A cape that proudly displays obeisance to the god Zamorak.
keysToEntries.ZamorakianAcolyte: A personal servant to Alric and a zealot of Zamorak.
keysToEntries.ZamorakMageHood: A hood styled after the Zamorakian magi of Dowdun Reach.
keysToEntries.ZamorakMagePack: A sack holding the personal effects of a Zamorakian.
keysToEntries.ZamorakMageRobe: Robes styled after the Zamorakian magi of Dowdun Reach.
keysToEntries.ZamorakMageRobeLegs: Robe legs styled after the Zamorakian magi of Dowdun Reach.
keysToEntries.ZamorakStaff: Standard issue for any mage in Dowdun Reach.
keysToEntries.ZamorakSword: A ceremonial sword intended as a gift to Zamorak.
keysToEntries.ZamorakWarriorPack: A sack holding the personal effects of a Zamorakian.
keysToEntries.UlvsLongbow: Once the bow of a silent hunter. Deals additional damage to unaware targets.
keysToEntries.AbraxusRing: A ring that once belonged to a powerful necromancer.
keysToEntries.DreamingStonePiece: A piece of the broken Dreaming Stone in Dowdun Reach's Nexus. It whispers if you listen closely.
keysToEntries.BeastmasterNotes: Notes, from the beastmasters of Dowdun Reach, on how to train various creatures.
keysToEntries.CorruptedEssenceSample: A strange sickly essence, linked to the corruption in Dowdun Reach. It writhes as you look at it.
keysToEntries.UnholyWater: Water blessed by priests of Zamorak.
keysToEntries.TwilightLily: A strange, glowing lily, with magical properties.
keysToEntries.GlassOwl: A handcrafted owl made of glass. It seems to have great sentimental value to someone.
keysToEntries.DragonsTooth: A dragon's tooth. Extracted from lesser dragons or mined from a dragon's skull found in the Highlands.
keysToEntries.Vestige.BlackSword: The remnants of a Zamorakian weapon. Perhaps you could learn something from it.

keysToEntries.Vestige.BlackShield: The remnants of a Zamorakian shield. Perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.DowdunReachCape: A piece of cloth torn from one of Dowdun Reach's tapestries.

keysToEntries.SalvagePileBlack: Scraps that can be searched through. Little bits of Black Knight are in there.

keysToEntries.DowdunRaven: Never trust a raven. They are a diabolical breed.

keysToEntries.Trinket.RingOfRecoil.FlavourText: A magical ring to punish enemy aggression.

keysToEntries.Vestige.TrinketRingofLife: Endlessly burning and reconstituting itself. Perhaps you could learn from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.TrinketAmuletofStrength: You can feel it pulling away from you. Perhaps you could learn from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.TrinketAmuletofDefence: Nothing is breaking this. Perhaps you could learn from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.TrinketAmuletofAccuracy: Still sharp. Perhaps you could learn from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.TrinketAmuletofMagic: A sphere that seems to be playing Menu Chill. Perhaps you could learn from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.TrinketRingofPursuit: Seems eager to be elsewhere. Perhaps you could learn from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.TrinketRingofRecoil: Shows the worst possible side of yourself. Perhaps you could learn from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.GreatswordShadow: The smoke seems to be roiling inside. Perhaps you could learn from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.ShieldHoplite: Splattered with blood. Perhaps you could learn from it.
keysToEntries.Vestige.ShroudofNoggin: A ghostly bundle of scratchy sackcloth. It's unsettling to hold. Still, perhaps you could learn something from it.
keysToEntries.AdamantiteBar: A sturdy metal used for crafting weapons and armour.
keysToEntries.AdamantiteOre: Mined from Adamantite deposits. It can be smelted into bars at a furnace.

keysToEntries.RedberryFlavourText: Gathered from bushes, eat them to gain a small amount of sustenance.

keysToEntries.BuildingPiece_Description: A lovely red postbox. Perfect for those who like to get letters.

keysToEntries.WoM_DS_NotFinishedTutorial1: Don't get ahead of yourself young whippersnapper, go finish up what the others are asking you to do. Come see me after all that.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_Intro1: Good, you've got the basics under your belt, but now the guide ropes are off. Taking down a dragon won't be easy, but you've got the skills you need to do it. Well... almost.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_Intro2: We need to start thinking long term. Bramblemead village is an excellent place for an outpost, even if it's a little overrun with goblins right now.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_Intro3: We should build our new base of operations here. By we, I mean you -- bad back.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_Intro4: Doesn't need to be fancy -- though style is always worth pursuing -- but it needs to be functional. Make a shelter, some foundations, walls, and a roof, and then build and claim a bed in it.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_Intro5: You can go bigger, of course, let your inner architect run wild, but the bed's the bare minimum we need to get started. Go on, hop to it. Get!
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_NotClaimedABed1: Not finished yet? The longer you leave things, the more likely you are to become a dragon's dinner.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_MadeBed1: Good, you've built a shelter. I mean... it could do with some work, we're gonna be here a while. Have you considered the decor at all?
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_MadeBed2: Maybe some better lighting? Curtains? A single potted plant? *Sigh* no, no, you're right. It's not important. Why live it up when there's so much squalor to enjoy?
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_MadeBed3: Anyway, look, we're set up here, but there's one of our number missing. Young Cathan, an adventurer in training that came along with us.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_MadeBed4: He disappeared after we got the lodestone working, vanished before you arrived. I'd like to track him down. To do that I need to cast a locator spell.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_MadeBed5: I'm just missing one ingredient, which I'll need from you. Antlers. You can get them from deer, hunt one and bring me the antlers. Then we'll get started. But first, here, take this.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_NotFoundCathanJournal_Castle: Not found young Cathan yet? Last we heard he'd been captured by the goblins up at the Brynmoor Castle. I'll need you to storm in there and rescue him.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_FoundCathanJournal1: Any sign of young Cathan? What -- he escaped, even managed to turn a few goblins to his cause? Interesting lad that, going to go places.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_FoundCathanJournal2: Speaking of going places, you mention he's exploring a dragonkin vault in the swamps? I think you probably want to go and take a look. No?
keysToEntries.Goblin_DS_VaultConvo1: Oh no, not another human. You gonna just leave me to die too? Like the last one did?
keysToEntries.Goblin_DS_VaultConvo2: Got us all riled up. Promised us power and glory. Said he knews how to get rid of the big scaly war king.
keysToEntries.Goblin_DS_VaultConvo3: Well he got what he wanted, some weird dragonkin stuff, then he said he were heading into Ghornfell and that I'd just slow us down. The others agreed, gits.
keysToEntries.Goblin_DS_VaultConvo4: So I bet you're just like him, so I aint gonna talk to yous no more. Yeah, that'll teach yas!
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_SpokenToGoblin1: So, young Cathan's pushing his way into Ghornfell, eh? Reckless, as always, that boy's gonna get himself killed.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_SpokenToGoblin2: But, if he's braving the garou, he must know a way of dealing with Velgar. There's no other reason he'd do something quite so stupid.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_SpokenToGoblin3: Look, I hate to ask this, but I need you to go after him. You need to fight your way into Ghornfell and pick up his trail.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_SpokenToGoblin4: Ghornfell's no joke though, that's where the garou live and if you thought goblins were trouble you're in for a shock.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_SpokenToGoblin5: Horrible creatures, the garou. Deadly beastmen with no concept of mercy. You'll need to make sure you're as prepared as you can be, before you risk facing them in battle.
keysToEntries.Cathan_DS_NotGotAmulet2: The spirit gestures emphatically at the backpack on the floor.
keysToEntries.Cathan_DS_DoesNotHaveHorn1: So, first off, death sucks. Let's just get that out of the way. I figured I might at least stand a chance against Velgar, I had all the plans, and I am sure I could have beaten him.
keysToEntries.Cathan_DS_DoesNotHaveHorn2: But then his garou minions mobbed me and dragged me here. They held me down as Velgar watched, laughing as they sacrificed me!
keysToEntries.Cathan_DS_DoesNotHaveHorn3: As death's go it could have been more heroic. Not exactly what I wanted on my epitaph. But, hey, silver cloud, you can avenge me now!
keysToEntries.Cathan_DS_DoesNotHaveHorn4: Here, let me teach you a recipe. It's a dragonkin horn that will force Velgar to battle. It's got to be used here, in an altar on the tower, but Velgar can't refuse it.
keysToEntries.Cathan_DS_DoesNotHaveHorn5: Make the horn, summon the dragon and then kick his ass for me. I'm counting on you, hero, we all are.
keysToEntries.Cathan_DS_HasHorn1: You've made the horn! Now you can finally avenge me. Use it on the altar in the tower over there.
keysToEntries.Cathan_DS_HasHorn2: But be ready, once you summon Velgar, he'll not give you a chance to change your mind.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_SpokenToCathan_NotKilledVelgar1: Cathan's dead? To shreds you say? Poor lad, not the end he deserved. But Velgar's made a mistake here, he's made this personal.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_SpokenToCathan_NotKilledVelgar2: I generally discourage violence, but this time I think you need to gather as much of it as possible and serve up a dragon sized portion.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_SpokenToCathan_NotKilledVelgar3: Build the horn. Summon Velgar. Avenge Cathan, save us all. Punch a dragon in the face!
keysToEntries.Cathan_DS_KilledVelgar1: You did it! Velgar got what he deserved. Thank you, hero, now I can rest in peace.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_HasVelgarHead1: The dragon's dead, that's quite the victory. No, really, you should be proud of yourself!
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_HasVelgarHead2: It's not every day you take out a draconic general infused with the elemental power of the earth. But you managed it, well done!
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_HasVelgarHead3: Here, I'll take that rapidly decomposing head off you and give you something much better in return.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_HasVelgarHead4: We can relax for a little while, but not forever. Velgar was one tough cookie, but he's just the foot soldier. Queen Kuldra is out there somewhere and she won't sit quietly for long.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_NoSpaceInInventory: I've got something to give you, but you've got your hands full. Go clear some space and then come back to me.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice_AboutDragons: Tell me more about these dragons.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutDragons1: When my comrades and I set foot on Ashenfall, we thought it would be paradise. Lush green fields, tall and resilient trees – a world unsullied.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutDragons3: They swooped down from the skies and decimated the landing party. Whole teams of people, gone, just like that.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutDragons4: "Queen Kuldra bids you welcome," they laughed, as our comrades drew their final breaths.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutDragons5: So here's the deal: I need you to kill them all. Show these dragons and their Queen, that we're not just going to roll over and die.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutDragons6: We fight back, and we fight back hard.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice_AboutVelgar: Can you tell me about Velgar?
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutVelgar1: General Velgar, let that sink in. A title, on a dragon, not a good sign.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutVelgar2: We don't know much at the moment, since everyone who's seen him is dead.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutVelgar3: But he's big, scary and he wants to make all of us wyrm food. So, diplomacy's out.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice_AboutKuldra: Can you tell me about Queen Kuldra?
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutKuldra1: I know very little about this Kuldra character, except that Velgar pledged his loyalty to her.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutKuldra2: From what I can gather, she's something like royalty to the dragons here; never a good sign.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutKuldra3: She's spoken of not with the reverence due a monarch, but with the devotion due a deity.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutKuldra4: I don't like that. Not one itty little bit.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice_AboutCathan: Can you tell me about the previous adventurer?
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutCathan1: Cathan, now there was a pain in the neck.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutCathan2: Young noble sprog, low down the pecking order. Fifth son, I think. Wanted to be a hero, you know how it goes.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutCathan3: Got into scraps as a boy. Lost his leg to a wolf. Say what you want, he had guts that kid.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutCathan4: The "legendary" swordsman Vannaka taught him the art of swordplay, while I instructed him in the arcane.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutCathan5: He was a constant annoyance—loud, irritating, overflowing with relentless optimism. You know the type. Even so, his absence leaves a void.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice_AboutDragonkin: Can you tell me about the Dragonkin?
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutDragonkin1: Relatively little is known about the Dragonkin, less still the ones who set up shop here.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutDragonkin2: We know that they're ancient, dangerous and constantly angry. They were cursed by the elder gods a long, long, long time ago.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutDragonkin3: They have a penchant for two things that interest us. First, they make dragons, which is the problem we're facing right now.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutDragonkin4: Secondly, they hoard secrets like they're more valuable than gold... or party hats.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutDragonkin5: Their vaults will be guarded, but well worth exploring. You never know what you might find in there.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice_AboutGarou: Can you tell me about the Garou?
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutGarou1: I don't have much for you there, Vannaka's the one investigating them. He's pushing his way into Ghornfell, you should look for him there.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutGarou2: What I know is that the garou are merciless. They'll cut you down without a chance to speak.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_AboutGarou3: Beastmen from another world, living only for the hunt. Best avoided if you can, but be prepared for a tough fight where you can't.
keysToEntries.CathanJournal_Castle_Content:
Just waiting for Spitfoot to bring me the keys, then next stop is the blue-flamed door in the swamps. It holds the secret to Velgar’s destruction, I just know it. For now I’ll bide my time, enjoying the spectacle of goblin hospitality. 

I enjoy being a goblin’s prisoner, there’s a kind of dance to the whole thing. They threaten, they shout, they exclaim all the horrible things they intend to do to you, and then they never go through with it. 

Goblin culture is like that. The strong hold power, but it’s mostly performative -- because if you make yourself too much of a threat, someone’s gonna challenge you to make you prove you deserve it. 

The dragon hasn’t learned that lesson about goblin faith. 

Bandos was a nutjob, Zanik will tell you, but he got his people to behave exactly how he wanted. Conflict for conflict’s sake, everyone just itching to beat up their boss. Which is how I’ve got a few of them wrapped round my finger – because I’ve let it slip that I’m gunning for their god. 

Velgar isn’t a god, he’s just a dragon, but he’s the toughest guy around and so they treat him as such. But I’ve worked out that path to taking him down and enough of these guys are thinking that I can do what I say. So they’re coming with me, we’re making a coup, and big old green-and-scaley’s getting an axe to the face. 

Just as soon as Spitfoot gets here, we’ll make for that blue-flamed swamp door, where there’s a piece of the puzzle that’ll set my plan in motion.
keysToEntries.CathanJournal_Ghornfell_Content:
The goblins warn me about Ghornfell and the creatures within it. They call them the garou, and they shiver when they say it. I’ve heard them mention them before, in the stories, myths and legends of this place – beastial warriors, that Bandos came here to destroy. He failed. 

The god of war, failed to destroy the Garou and their goddess. That should tell you something. 

I’ll be careful. I’ll sneak past their fortifications, I need to get through to the tower that rises in the heard of these Fractured Plains. That’s where Velgar rests. 

That’s where I’ll destroy him.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_SpokeToGoblin6: Here, this'll help get you started.
keysToEntries.Goblin_DS_PostDialogue: I still ain't talkin' to no stinky human. Get lost!
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_HeadIntoGhornfell1: I need you to gear yourself up as best you can, then push into Ghornfel to look for young Cathan.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_HeadIntoGhornfell2: Ghornfell is a dangerous place, and home to the garou beastmen. Keep your wits about you.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_NotFoundCathanGhost1: You've made it into Ghornfel, I'm impressed. No, honestly, I expected you to drown in the swamp or something.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_NotFoundCathanGhost2: But if the entrance to Ghornfel is what you say, it sounds like Cathan has been captured...again... so I need you to go and rescue him.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_NotFoundCathanGhost3: I imagine the garou will be taking him to Velgar. Make your way to the tower, and pray you're not too late.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_NeedVelgarHead1: What's that? Velgar's dead? Are you sure? I'll need to see his head, dragons can be tricky like that.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_NeedVelgarHead2: Bring me his head and I'll make sure you're rewarded.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_RefuseToHandOverHead: OK... not sure why you want to keep a decomposing dragon's head around, but you do you I guess.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_HasManyHeads1: But, we'll worry about that later. Here, let me take that slimey, smelly dragon he... why do you have so many of them?
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_HasManyHeads2: Do you just carry multiple heads around with you? Is that normal to you?
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_HasManyHeads3: Eh, whatever, I've seen weirder. Hand them over, I'll turn them into something more useful.
keysToEntries.Altar_DS_HasRecipe: There is an indention in the altar where something would be placed. It looks like it would fit the shape of Cathan's summoning horn design.
keysToEntries.Altar_DS_HasHorn: There is an indentation that would fit the summoning horn, if you are ready to summon Velgar.
keysToEntries.Altar_DS_DoesNotHaveRecipe: There is an indentation in the altar where something would be placed, but you are not sure what it might be.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice_GiveWoMHeads: <Hand over all of your Velgar heads>
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice_DenyWoMHeads: <Refuse to hand over your Velgar heads>
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_NotGotDeerHorn1: Not got the ingredients yet? I need you to hunt a deer and bring me one of their antlers. Deers are flighty creatures, and easily spooked.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_NotGotDeerHorn2: You'll find it easier to hunt one if you craft yourself a bow of some kind. The deer themselves are all over Bramblemead, finding them shouldn't be a problem.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_NotGotDeerHorn3: Just hunt one down, and bring me the antlers. You can keep all the other useful materials, maybe use them to craft better gear? You're looking a bit shabby.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_NotGotDeerHorn_Struggling1: You're really struggling with this, aren't you? I'll walk you through exactly what to do, but I can't be holding your hand all the time.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_NotGotDeerHorn_Struggling2: Discover, learn and improve. That's going to need to be your mantra on Ashenfall if you're going to survive. So find new materials, discover new recipes and then make them.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_NotGotDeerHorn_Struggling3: To hunt deer you'll want a bow. Find some flax and some wood, this should be enough to help you build a spinning wheel. There you'll make thread and with that you can craft a bow.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_NotGotDeerHorn_Struggling4: Find feathers to make arrows, and then you're good to go. While you're at it, look for recipes that will let you make stronger gear. You're going to need it.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_GotHorn1: Ah excellent, you've got one, we can begin. This ritual is a bit old school, proper druidic guff, but it's handy in a pinch.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_GotHorn2: It homes in on the spiritual residue of our subject, in this case young Cathan, using this old shoe of his as a spiritual connection. Vile, but useful. Just don't sniff it.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_GotHorn3: We'll then just point the antler at the map, and it'll point straight to where we'll find him... or at least where he's been most the last couple of days.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_GotHorn4: Now then, we just need to spread the rune essence like so. Add a dash of this weird unguent stuff Zanik made from snails, and then...
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_GotHorn5: Ah... the castle, not ideal. That place is a veritable goblin stronghold. Go see if you can find him, but make sure you're geared up for a fight.
keysToEntries.WoM_DS_DeerCheck_Intro: Hrmm? How are you getting on? Got me any antlers yet?
keysToEntries.Description: Recruited with promises of glory, your first challenge is to defeat General Velgar.
keysToEntries.Title: Dragon Slayer
keysToEntries.CathanJournal_Vannaka_Content:
