Old Vannaka’s an odd chap. Loyal to a fault, but not one to show affection. I still remember how he trained me, instilling me with a hatred for him that pushed me right past the pain. He was merciless, he was relentless, his training made me ache every day. So I took months of resentment and I balled them into a fist and with all of my loathing rammed it right into his face.  

He was so proud. So proud that I punched him. So pleased with me for bruising his jaw. Because that was the first time I had walked on my new leg, without having to think through every painful motion.

He had given me something else to turn my loathing on, that wasn’t myself.

That lummox took a broken kid, ready to give up on the world, and turned him into a warrior.

He’s a good man, Vannaka, even if he won’t always admit it. So of course, I said I’d join him on this new adventure.

I feel like there’s more than just a curiosity here for him. Something deeper. Something hidden. A pain behind the eyes. So, yeah, I’ll help him find what he’s looking for. It’s what a good friend does.
keysToEntries.DragonkinKnowledgeStatuesActivate: Your mind fills with the knowledge of a new crafting recipe... but at what cost?
keysToEntries.Statue.AbyssalWhip.RecipeNotUnlocked: The statue's shadow flickers strangely, as if it has a will of its own. It promises power, but only for those worthy of overcoming a creature of true darkness.
keysToEntries.Statue.AbyssalWhip.PlayerChoice.GiveSpin: Show the Abyssal Spine to the statue.
keysToEntries.Statue.AbyssalWhip.GiveWhip: The statue's shadow seems to shiver as though giggling with wicked malice. A tendril of night touches you and your mind fills with the knowledge of how to craft a weapon of unnatural power.
keysToEntries.Statue.AbyssalWhip.NotGotSpine: You lack the trophy the shadow desires. It waits silently for you to bring it what it seeks. Your mind fills with images of flickering purple lightning, and arms like blades.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice.Generic: Is there anything I can help with?

keysToEntries.Objective.VisitWoM: Speak to the Wise Old Man near Ashien's Watch.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.WitherRot.5: That is the curse that afflicts Fellhollow. Raising the dead as mindless creatures, and forcing the souls to wander the world unable to pass on. Doomed to lose themselves and go mad from the lonely horror of it all.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.WitherRot.3: Yet, with each step, you lose more and more of that grasp on life. Your soul is tugged further and further from your body until finally... SNAP! Your tether breaks and your soul is lost to the twilight.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.WitherRot.4: Yet, it is not your time and you did not pass naturally. Your body, while dead, still wanders because it is now driven not by your will, but by instinct, hunger and a need to live again.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.WitherRot.2: You start to see the world as a spirit would. As everything around you drains of life, colour and vibrancy, you see the hidden things that lie beneath the glamour of living.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.Death.6: Still, when Gielinor itself dies and I can retire, I suspect there will still be more forms to fill in than there are stars in the skies. Only two things in life are guaranteed, death and paperwork... and the former is debatable.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.WitherRot.1: Imagine, for a moment, that your soul is on a string and that string gets snagged round a tree. As your body walks, the essence that is you gets pulled further and further away.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.Death.5: It's a lonely old job, sometimes, and the paperwork will drive you to madness. But, I have Muncher to help with the lonliness, and all the time in the world to make a decent headstart on the paperwork.
keysToEntries.Cathan.SecondSealObtained.2: Being sacrificed to a dragon somewhat ruins that. Still, I'm going to try my best at the ghostly sidekick gig. Maybe I can be a footnote in one of your adventures somewhere?
keysToEntries.Cathan.SecondSealObtained.1: Two down, one to go. You've really got the hang of this adventurer gig. I'm kinda jealous, I'd always wanted to be one of the heroic types. Great adventures, derring-dos and all that.
keysToEntries.Cathan.FirstSealObtained.1: Great work on that first seal! You're already looking stronger, spiritually speaking. Get the other two and we should be able to deal with Imaru. By we, I mean you, obviously, but I'll be cheering from the sides... very far away sides.
keysToEntries.Death.FoundStones.5: I feel sorry for her. This was not her fault, nor her fate, but the world is cruel and there is no way to undo what was done without her destruction. So gear up, get yourself cursed, and take her down.
keysToEntries.Death.FoundStones.4: Fortunately, curses like this can be modified. They're not the more fairytale affair, none of that true love nonsense. Instead there will be a number of seals, special shrines dedicated to holding onto some aspect of the curse. You can take a piece of the curse for yourself, from each of these shrines. Get enough of them and you'll be strong enough to challenge Imaru directly.
keysToEntries.Death.FoundStones.3: Those wandering dead are stuck. Trapped. Unable to move on whilst Imaru still guards the region. Furthermore, you cannot just destroy Imaru, because she is empowered by a foolish necromantic curse.
keysToEntries.Cathan.StandingStones.Intro.3: All these shambling corpses causing trouble, that's her doing. Their spirits ripped out of them, leaving just enough to keep them wandering.
keysToEntries.Cathan.StandingStones.Intro.2: There are some lingering ghostly echoes around the place, and I got talking to them. Seems like there was this big friendly dragon called Imaru, who turned somewhat unfriendly after being killed by some other, bigger, less-friendly dragon. Now she's stuck in the afterlife, eating any unfortunate souls that come her way.
keysToEntries.Cathan.StandingStones.Intro.1: Hey there friend, good to see you again. The Wise Old Man was getting on my nerves a bit, so I thought I might try and give you a hand. Been looking into everything and you're not gonna want to hear this, but there's another ruddy dragon at play. Only, get this, she's already dead.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.FailedFiveTimes: Struggling a bit, I take it? I get it, I can be such a numb-skull sometimes. Here, let me help you out. You need to get Soulrifted, which means you need to go and stand in that horrible red nastiness over there until you can see dead-people-things...like the door outta here.
keysToEntries.Death.FoundStones.2: Yes, I already knew, but, as per my writing group, I feel showing is often better than telling. You've now seen the state of Fellhollow for yourself, it's not a good sight I'm sure you'll agree.
keysToEntries.Death.FoundStones.1: Good, you understand the situation now. Imaru, a soul-devouring dragon, cursed with an unending hunger and afflicted with an eternal rage. Not a good combination.
keysToEntries.Cathan.StandingStones.FoundStones: Pieces of Imaru's curse, just up for grabs? Feels like someone's laid a trap for big, mean and scaly. I'll leave it to you to go and collect them, I don't think I'm going to do much against a dragon that eats wandering souls.
keysToEntries.Cathan.StandingStones.NotFoundStones.Fai: There's something strange about these stones, they're a focus point for a whole load of necromantic energy. Maybe try reading them?
keysToEntries.Cathan.StandingStones.NotFoundStones.2: If I wasn't a misty cloud of regret in a vaguely human shape, I'd try and work out what those stones are pointing to, and then deal with whatever's there.
keysToEntries.Cathan.StandingStones.NotFoundStones.1: So, you think those stones mean something? Feels like they're pointing to something important. There's a lot of necromantic magic whizzing around here, and these things seem to be the focus of it all.
keysToEntries.Cathan.StandingStones.Intro.5: I'm not exactly knowledgeable about curses, but I can feel a strange sort of necromantic power in the air. It mostly focuses over the big tower over there, like a ward keeping us out, but it also converges here by these stones. Something odd about them. Worth a look?
keysToEntries.Cathan.StandingStones.Intro.4: Now, normally I'd say you should go and give her hell, but get this - she's not just a dragon, she's a CURSED dragon! And this curse, it makes her bigger, nastier and generally unstoppable. Someone did some proper dark magic here to get that going, but if you're going to take this dragon out, you need to deal with that somehow.
keysToEntries.WoM.AshiensWatchLocation.IntroCompletedD: Well? Go on, get! I don't have all day to hold your hand. There's something weird and sinister, go poke it with a stick like you always do!
Obviously, I'm not going to open it, so I'm giving the words to you. You need to go up to the door and say "Mors, introitum quaero!" and it should let you in.

See this creepy little passageway? There's a door at the bottom wreathed in magic, and the spooky stuff at that -- necromancy. I've been studying it for a while and I've learned the words to allow access.
Now then, an adventurer's work is never finished. Trust me, I know.
keysToEntries.WoM.DragonSlayerLocation.GoToAshiensWatc: Now then, an adventurer's work is never finished. Trust me, I know. There's a strange door to the north, just past Ashien's Watch and it's sending chills down my spine. Meet me there, we'll talk more.
keysToEntries.Death.ImaruSlain.HasHead.NoSpace.1: Hrmm, you appear to be a little overburdened. Shed yourself some of thy worldly trappings and then come speak to me again.
keysToEntries.Death.ImaruSlain.HasHead.HasSpace.2: Now, I have an enormously unpleasant cleanup to do. So you'll forgive me if I get back to work.
keysToEntries.Death.ImaruSlain.HasHead.HasSpace.1: A foul thing, really. Corrupted beyond repair. I almost pity her. Still, you're not interested in that, I feel you'll enjoy this much more.
keysToEntries.Death.ImaruSlain.HasHead.1: Here let me take that dragon head from you.
keysToEntries.Death.ImaruSlain.NoHead: If you bring me her head, then I'll turn it into something useful. Grim, yes, but one must have something to put in the filing cabinet.
keysToEntries.Death.ImaruSlain.2: I knew one of you would luck out eventually, I'm glad it was you. Oh, yes, sorry, no chosen ones here. But ignore that, you did good, better than the rest, so you deserve what you came for.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.Intro.3: See that horrible red stuff over there? I know it looks nasty, I certainly wouldn't have the stomach for it, but you're going to need to dip your toes in for a minute or two to get nice and Soulrifted. That'll let you see things you living types never get to see.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.Intro.2: Still, if you're determined to head on through, you'll need to shed a few pounds. You need to get Soulrifted, my friend. Need to get that spirit of yours dragged a little into the world of the dead. It's the only way to get through this here gate.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.Intro.1: Hey there, bit lost? No-body to point you in the right direction for a good tailor?... Because, frankly, your look could use some work. I assume you're trying to get into Fellhollow? Nasty place. I wouldn't be seen dead there.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.Underworld.4: But, death isn't really something you need to worry about for a while. I've decided to keep you from passing on until you've cleared up a few problems for me. Just don't get too carried away.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.Underworld.3: The Menaphite god Icthlarin, guards souls as they pass through the gate. Time was that Amascut would offer some souls rebirth, but things have been... tricky, on that front for a while.
keysToEntries.Death.AllSealsObtained.2: When she's slain, bring me her head. Gruesome, I know, but I will need it to give her spirit peace at least. Also it helps with the paperwork.
keysToEntries.Death.AllSealsObtained.1: Now that you're borrowing the power of her curse, Imaru is finally vulnerable. She's had centuries of souls to feast on, and she's still one of Kuldra's draconic generals, so she won't go down easily. Make sure you're prepared.
keysToEntries.Death.FirstSealObtained.1: Good. I can feel the necromantic energy fluctuating, Once you've empowered yourself at all three seals then you should be able to face Imaru in her tower. Then you can put an end to her suffering, and start Fellhollow back on the slow path to healing.
keysToEntries.StandingStone.SilverthornKeep: To the east stands a fortress that once housed the Wild Knights, brave soldiers of Guthix. They fought bravely against the endless hordes of the dead, but, like all things, fell in the end. I have left a shard of the curse there, guarded by their last living protector.
keysToEntries.Cathan.ThirdSealObtained.2: Ha! Wish I'd learned that before I died, it would have made things a whole lot easier, and I'd probably still be alive today. Still, no point dwelling over spilled arterial fluid.
keysToEntries.Cathan.ThirdSealObtained.1: That's all of the seals. Wow, you're really good at this. I thought I was cut out to be an adventurer, but compared to you I'm a joke.
keysToEntries.StandingStone.Vault: The dragonkin knew cruelty well, creating creatures whose lives were songs of suffering. Beneath the remains of Imaru's lover, behind a waterfall tainted by her sorrow, lies an ancient structure. Within it I hid a piece of the curse, guarded by the cruel masters that chained her.
keysToEntries.StandingStone.Emberwood: There was a village once, west of the tower, that I made my home. The people lived together, peacefully, happily, under the guardianship of dragons. Imaru's grief and rage has made it a prison, all joy turned to fear. I placed a piece of the curse within its heart, an anchor made of suffering.
keysToEntries.Cathan.ThirdSealObtained.3: Now that you've got yourself nice and cursed, you can breach the tower right? Time to take on Imaru and destroy her once and for all! I wish I could join you, but I'll be there in spirit. Well... not literally, cos she eats those, but you know what I mean.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.Death.2: Or, you can call me Harold, though few ever do. I was once like you, just a normal man, living a normal life -- a farmer, I think, though it's difficult to remember so long ago.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.Death.1: Ah, but you know me already, don't you? I am the shadow over your shoulder, inching ever closer until you take that final breath. You have always known me, because you await me each day.
keysToEntries.Seal.Emberwood.NotActivated.1: Grief, sorrow and fear unravel at your fingertips. A curse woven from the despair of the villagers finally stills. You take a piece of that sorrow for yourself.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.Underworld.2: In the Underworld, a great river, the river Noumenon, carries souls down to mighty gates that lead to what lies beyond. What happens next is a mystery, even to me.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.Underworld.1: There is a life after death, that's one great mystery answered for you - you're welcome. When a person dies I cut the cord connecting their soul to their body and allow them to pass into the Underworld.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.Fellhollow.4: The land is cursed and dangerous. The perfect place for an adventurer like yourself to cut their teeth, no?
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.Fellhollow.2: Whatever is happening prevents souls from reaching the Afterlife. More than that, it seems to prevent my ability to reap them. They are all off my list, and that's just not proper.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.Fellhollow.1: The waters of the River Noumenon flow into this region, and to be clear they shouldn't. Something about this place has caused damage enough to the Underworld to harm the world of the living as well.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.Fellhollow.3: This isn't just people either. Animals, even the plants, have had some of their vitality drawn out of them. Even the water is tainted with the essence of the river Noumenon -- meaning you need to be careful trying to drink it.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.Death.3: I died, that's what got me this job. I was the very first person to die on Gielinor. Hardly the most glamorous of accolades, but someone had to. In so dying I became Death, capital D, the reaper of souls.
keysToEntries.Cathan.PostQuest.2: As for me, nothing new really. Death's not come to reap me yet, think he might even like me. Maybe if I keep working along side you I can stave off the ever-after for a little while longer.
keysToEntries.Cathan.PostQuest.1: Another dragon down, eh? The mainland must be missing you! I bet you'd have solved half the world's problems by now.
keysToEntries.Death.PostQuest.NoHeads.1: Forgive me, but I must focus my attention elsewhere. There is an enormous backlog in Fellhollow that I need to clear up.
keysToEntries.Objective.RemoveSoulRift: Remove the Soul Rift at a Purification Pool.
keysToEntries.Objective.InvestigateStones: Find Cathan and look into what he was investigating.
keysToEntries.Objective.GatherCurseFragments.2: Gather two more curse fragments from the locations described in the standing stones.
keysToEntries.Objective.GatherCurseFragments.1: Gather three curse fragments from the locations described in the standing stones.
keysToEntries.Objective.GatherCurseFragments.3: Find the last curse fragment from the locations described in the standing stones.
keysToEntries.Objective.DefeatImaru: Enter the Shattered Spire and defeat Imaru.
keysToEntries.Death.QuestNotStarted.Intro.1: Oh, cease your cowering, you're not dead. You still have at least twenty minutes before I reap your soul.
keysToEntries.Death.QuestNotStarted.Intro.3: Welcome to Fellhollow. The whole region is infected with a terrible withering. Some dread curse that untethers the soul and drags it to the river Noumenon without ever sailing upon it, preventing the dead from moving on. This cannot stand.
keysToEntries.Death.QuestNotStarted.Intro.2: Haha, just a little joke... Mostly. You'll probably die a few times, but I'll bring you back like I have been doing. I still have work for you after all. I know why you're here, little bit of a problem with the dead not actually dying.
keysToEntries.Death.QuestNotStarted.Intro.5: I would start by seeking out an old friend of yours, young Cathan. He's been doing some work for me, investigating the site of some necromantic ritual or other. Before you do that, though, you'll need to get into Fellhollow. A colleague of mine, Postie Pete, can help you with that.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.Intro.4: You'll want to find a Purification Pool, once you've found your way past that wall. There's one down the path outta here, I think, but I'm getting a-head of myself there. Get to it quick, the longer you're soul rifted, the more likely *she* is to spot you, and the worse things will get. If that dragon finds you, you're dead, no two ways about it.
keysToEntries.Death.QuestNotStarted.Intro.4: I need you to investigate this. Find out what went on. Resolve it if you can and I shall reward you for your efforts. As well as continue this little resurrection situation. A fair bargain, I'm sure you agree.
keysToEntries.Death.ImaruSlain.1: Imaru the devourer is dead. Good! She was a potential problem that I did not want to have to deal with. You've done well, adventurer, better than the last few I sent anyway.
keysToEntries.Cathan.ImaruSlain.1: Dragons don't stand a chance against you! Best go speak to big tall and boney, so he can confirm she's dead and gone. I'll wait here, I guess.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.WitherRot.6: In short, it's a bad thing. Probably want to deal with it quickly.
keysToEntries.Death.FirstTimeTalkingAboutStore.StoreEx: If you come across any of these soul fragments, please bring them to me and I will happily exchange them for special items you cannot get anywhere else. Here, let me show you some of the items available to you.
keysToEntries.Death.QuestNotStarted.RunPastDeath: No. You can't outrun death, no matter how hard you try. Come back and speak to me, or you're not going to get very far.
keysToEntries.FellhollowEntrance.DefaultInteraction: Strange magic radiates off the door. It seems like it requires a phrase of some kind to access.
keysToEntries.Death.FirstTimeTalkingAboutStore.StoreEx: Now, before you go anywhere, there's something I'd like to discuss. Soul fragments. These little stones are of no use to you, but very useful to me. They contain the last traces of the poor lost souls of Fellhollow, and I would like to help them move on.
keysToEntries.Death.AskAbout.Death.4: Guthix himself gave me the scythe and robes I use to this day. It is my honour and my duty to cut the tether that traps souls to this world, allowing the dead to move on to the Underworld.
keysToEntries.Cathan.PostQuest.3: I've spotted this great keep up on the mountains. Feels like there might be someone alive there. What do you say? Meet you there? One last great adventure or something like that?
keysToEntries.Seal.Vault.NotActivated.1: Rage unlike any other, fueled by grief and terrible loss, wraps around your heart like a vice, before its grip finally relents as you take a piece of the curse for yourself.
keysToEntries.Seal.SilverthornKeep.NotActivated.1: A terrible despair works its way into your bones, making you shiver from the coldness of it all. As you take the power for yourself some warmth returns.
keysToEntries.Seal.Activated.1: You already have this shard of the curse, there is nothing more to do here .
keysToEntries.ShatteredTower.NotGotSeals: The door refuses to open. Powerful necromantic seals bar your entry. You will need the full power of Imaru's curse to break through them.
keysToEntries.EmberwoodPuzzle.GateSaradominStatue.Inac: Saradomin shields this village from the relentless dead. Seek his power on high, surrounded by a ring of water. Obtain the blessings of all the gods to destroy the barrier.
keysToEntries.EmberwoodPuzzle.GateZamorakStatue.Inacti: The power of chaos wards this village form harm. Hail Zamorak! Seek his altar near by, where the suffering is strongest. Obtain the blessings of all the gods to destroy the barrier.
keysToEntries.EmberwoodPuzzle.GateGuthuxStatue.Inactiv: The lord of balance leaves his blessing in a harmonious circle, guarded by a dread beast. Obtain the blessings of all the gods to destroy the barrier.
keysToEntries.EmberwoodPuzzle.GateArmadylStatue.Inacti: Justice must be maintained, even in times of great strife. Seek the wisdom of Armadyl on a nearby lake, where three spirits await judgement.  Obtain the blessings of all the gods to destroy the barrier.
keysToEntries.EmberwoodPuzzle.SaradominStatue.MainStat: The shrine is damaged, a piece missing. Until it is repaired the magic cannot flow to the village barrier.
You repair the broken shrine. Magic thrums into life, feeding energy back to the village barrier.
The shrine hums with magical power, weakening the barrier enchantment around Emberwood village.
keysToEntries.EmberwoodPuzzle.SaradominStatue.HeadPiec: You collect the broken statue piece.
You have no room in your inventory to pick up the statue piece.
keysToEntries.EmberwoodPuzzle.ZamorakStatue.Inactive: You offer a prayer to the shrine and it hums with magical energy. The barrier around the village weakens.
keysToEntries.EmberwoodPuzzle.ZamorakStatue.Active: The shrine hums with magical power, weakening the barrier around Emberwood Village.
keysToEntries.EmberwoodPuzzle.GuthixStatue.DragonStill: The dragon's power prevents you from using this.
keysToEntries.EmberwoodPuzzle.GuthixStatue.DragonDefea: You offer a prayer to the shrine, and it hums with magical power. The barrier around Emberwood village weakens as a result.
The shrine hums with magical power, weakening the barrier around Emberwood Village.
keysToEntries.EmberwoodPuzzle.ArmadylStatue.MainStatue: Justice must prevail, for the barrier to rise. Such is the will of Armadyl. Long ago a terrible act was committed. What was the crime? Who is responsible and why did they do it? Name these and unlock the blessing of Armadyl.
Speak to the ghosts to learn their take on the events. Look for the discrepancies and the emotions at play, the guilty often trap themselves with their own lies.
Cast your verdict arbiter. What crime was committed?
To spread fear among the population.
Because they thought it was funny.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice.ArmadylPuzzle.Suspect.Shady: There are no rabbits in Falador.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice.ArmadylPuzzle.Suspect.Suspi: They were drunk, but their story was too detailed.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice.ArmadylPuzzle.Suspect.Snick: They have a very annoying laugh.
They found it too funny. Matching the motive.
keysToEntries.EmberwoodPuzzle.ArmadylStatue.MainStatue: Incorrect. An arbiter must not be so blind to the crimes committed. Speak to the spirits again to learn the true crime committed.
Indeed, a brutal and terrible crime. Many lives lost in an instant, their families deprived of their loved ones. Tell me, why was such an act committed?
Incorrect. An arbiter must not be so blind to the crimes committed. Speak to the spirits again to learn the true crime committed.

Yes. Imagine, an act of such violence, such tragedy and loss. Families ruined. Destinies upended. Futures shattered. All because someone found it amusing. As though the lives of others carry no more weight than an ant beneath ones boot. But, tell me, who committed the crime?
An interesting deduction, but an incorrect one. Speak to the spirits once more and look for the discrepancies in their tales.
Yes, the guilty often miss the smaller details as they try to pretend their innocence. This spirit was responsible for the destruction and will pay the price in the afterlife.

Yes. Sometimes the guilty lack shame, or remorse, unable to keep their strange sense of pride hidden. To them, this massacre was an amusement, a bit of a laugh. To others, it was the worst day of their lives... or their last.
The shrine hums with magical power, weakening the barrier.
keysToEntries.EmberwoodPuzzle.ArmadylStatue.ShadyGhost: I shouldn't be here. I deserve to move on! I was there, sure, when the bodies piled up, but I had nothing to do with it. It was a sunny day, the light was in my eyes and there were rabbits everywhere and I just thought, why not? You know? There's good eating on one of those.

So, I took a shot, even though the glare made it hard to see and, well, next thing I know there's screaming. I ran, I admit it. I ran from the city gate, all the way to the party room. I figured music and revelry would keep me safe, right?

When the balloons started popping, I just assumed it was another drop party, you know? I figured there was always a chance I could get something from one, like a party hat or something. But they weren't popping because of parties, they were popping because the arrows went flying.
So many dead. Falador lined with bodies. But, it wasn't me, honest. I had nothing to do with it. I was just hunting rabbits.
keysToEntries.EmberwoodPuzzle.ArmadylStatue.Suspicious: I never get invited to parties, but this house party was something special, you know? A great achievement, deserving of celebration, how could I turn it down? We laughed, we drank, we danced the night away, it was wonderful. Partying till morning, in the glare of the sun.
We thought we might end celebrations at the Party Room, you know? Balloons, prizes, everyone having the time of their lives? Sure, we were a bit drunk, but who wouldn't be after an all night rager?
I think it was the laughing that came first. Loud and obnoxious, a guffawing cackle that we heard over the screaming. They thought it was funny, as they tore the city apart. Laughing as they went.
Sure, in the chaos, I stole a couple of gemstones someone left as they ran. But, i mean, come on, it was a drop party after all! Fair game. I shouldn't have to suffer because of that.
keysToEntries.EmberwoodPuzzle.ArmadylStatue.Snickering: It was a lovely house party, really it was. But, the weather was cold and grey, and I just wanted to go for a walk, you know? So that's why I was there, in the park, when  the trouble started.

I'd just been wandering over by the party room, the sound of balloons popping is soothing, you know? I had my bow out, because, well, I thought I could shoot a couple of them that floated away. Balloons, obviously.
Ha! Then the screaming happened, man it was funny!

What? Oh, come on, you'd have been laughing too. Balloons exploding as people fell over them running, you can't not laugh at that? It was all "Aaargh *POP* Noooo! *POP* My precious spleen!" how is that not funny?
keysToEntries.EmberwoodPuzzle.InactiveStatue: The statue no longer maintains the barrier to the village.
keysToEntries.ShadyGhost.Name: Shady Ghost
keysToEntries.SuspciciousGhost.Name: Suspicious Ghost
keysToEntries.SnickieringGhost.Name: Snickering Ghost
keysToEntries.WoM.DragonSlayerLocation.Generic: You've dealt with the problem for now, but who knows what danger waits around the corner? If I were you, I'd get yourself ready for whatever horribleness is coming next.
keysToEntries.WoM.AshiensWatchLocation.NotDoneDragonSl: Thought I'd do some exploring, it gets boring sitting in a ruined little village all the time... so I found a new ruined area, but this time with angry dog-people. Anyway, they leave me alone, I leave them alone.
The thing that really interests me though is down this little passageway. There's a door, and it's sealed by magic. Strange stuff too, old and powerful. It'll take me some time to work it out.
But, anyway, aren't you busy? Shouldn't you be off looking at how best to stab old Velgar to death? Feels like you should be focusing your attention on that, hrmm? Go on, get!
keysToEntries.WoM.AshiensWatch.NotFinishedQuest.Generi: I don't know what you're talking to me for. Aren't you busy doing stuff through that creepy door down this tunnel? Go on, get!
keysToEntries.Death.Name: Death
keysToEntries.PostiePete.Name: Postie Pete
I need you to investigate Fellhollow. Find out what went on. Resolve it if you can and I shall reward you for your efforts. As well as continue this little resurrection situation. A fair bargain, I'm sure you agree.
keysToEntries.Death.OptionalChoices.Generic: Now then, what do you need?
keysToEntries.Death.OptionalChoice.Hub: What would you like to know?
keysToEntries.PostiePete.QuestComplete: Nice work in Fellhollow! You're really a step a-head of the mainland adventurers. Now that things have calmed down a bit, I've got decades worth of letters to deliver. So I'm just gonna rest here a bit before I start.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.QuestInProgress: Yeah, Fellhollow's a bit of a nightmare, isn't it? You're absolutely doomed mate. Haha, just ribbing you! I'm sure you've got this all under control.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.Soulrifted.1: Ah, feel a little light in the soul there? That's Soul Rift for you. On the plus side, you'll see things you couldn't see before. On the downside you're a dragon's dinner waiting to happen.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.Soulrifted.2: You don't want to stay soul rifted for too long. As time goes on you'll attract the hungry dead to you, eventually luring Imaru herself and... well... that won't end well for you.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.Soulrifted.3: So, if I were you -- and I had feet -- I'd get my skates on and make a beeline straight for a purification pool. You can't miss them, Death's made them extra glowy, just for you!
keysToEntries.PostiePete.GoSeeDeath.NotRifted: Oh, ho, ho. Getting waay a-head of yourself here. I think you should go speak to tall, dark and spooky back there, huh?
keysToEntries.PostiePete.GoSeeDeath.Rifted: Oh dear, look at you running a-head of yourself. You're all soul rifted, and that's gonna get painful quick. You should go speak to Death before you do yourself a mischief.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.QuestInProgress.2: Enjoying yourself in Fellhollow? Yeah, I wouldn't take a holiday there either. Don't do too many holidays anyway, I don't like spending them on my own and, well, I've just got no-body to go with.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.QuestInProgress.SoulRifted: Oh dear, looking a bit peaky there. You should find yourself a rejuvination pool before Imaru spots you. Trust me.
keysToEntries.QuestDesc: Put an end to the horror of Fellhollow, by defeating Imaru, the soul-devourer.
keysToEntries.TwoSealsRemain: You feel the curse begin to build within you. Two shards still remain.
keysToEntries.OneSealRemains: The curse echoes within your soul. One curse shard remains.
keysToEntries.FinalSeal: Your soul resonates with Imaru's grief. You are ready to bring the fight to her.
keysToEntries.UnleashCurse: Unleash the curse, destroying the fragments, and face Imaru?
keysToEntries.Death.NotFoundStones.1: Hrmm, what are you doing here? Shouldn't you be investigating Fellhollow?
keysToEntries.SaradominSymbolDescription: It looks as if it is meant to sit on top of something.

keysToEntries.Doric.NotStarted.Intro.1: You know what, we make a good team, you and I. You find me things and I record them for future crafters to enjoy.
keysToEntries.Doric.NotStarted.Intro.2: It may not sound like much, but sharing knowledge is what turns a gathering of people into a thriving community.
keysToEntries.Doric.NotStarted.Intro.3: So, what do you say to helping me out a bit more?
keysToEntries.Doric.NotStarted.SpokenToDoric.1: Ah, you're back. Fancy helping me find a few things?
keysToEntries.Doric.NotStarted.QuestRejected.1: Ah, yes, you must be quite busy. Well, I won't keep you. If you're bored later, come say hi.
keysToEntries.Doric.QuestAccepted.NoProgress.NotSpoken: Wonderful. Now then, I once told me lad about this strange warrior from far away. Fought great evil, undead beasties and the like.
Sounds familiar, doesn't it? A land full of the undead, with deadly monsters roaming around. I'm now wondering if the story came from here somehow.
As the tale goes, the warrior first had to face off against a deadly archer. All bone and dark magic. Had this skeletal bow that was described in eerie detail.
If you can find and make me that undead ranger's bow, I can compare it to the description and see if it lines up.
Oh, yes, like that one you have there!
I'm looking for an undead ranger's bow. Perhaps it's another weapon in one of those dragonkin vaults?
That's it! That's the bow! It looks just like the story described. I wonder...
Here, a little something for your troubles.
OK, let's look at the next part of the story. There was a necromancer, nasty piece of work, with a powerful magic staff.
If it's anything like the bow, it'll be stored in a vault somewhere. Can you bring it to me? It'll really help with my research.
Like that staff there! Wow, you're really good at this.
I've got a little something to thank you for your efforts so far, but you seem a bit overburdened. I'll hold on to it til you've got your hands a bit freer.
keysToEntries.Doric.QuestAccepted.GivenBow.NotGotStaff: I'm looking for a necromancer's staff. Perhaps it's another weapon in one of those dragonkin vaults?
keysToEntries.Doric.QuestAccepted.GivenBow.GotStaff.1: Amazing, you've got the staff! That's... that's exactly like the story... this is... hrmm. Why would this story be so accurate, unless...
keysToEntries.Doric.QuestAccepted.GivenBow.GotStaff.No: I've got a little something to thank you for your efforts so far, but you seem a bit overburdened. I'll hold on to it til you've got your hands a bit freer.
keysToEntries.Doric.QuestAccepted.GivenBow.GotStaff.Go: Here, my little way of saying thank you.
Alright, last piece of the puzzle. There's this special sword that I need. Made of shadow, or smoke or something. Nasty thing, wielded by something proper wicked.

The good news, is that I'm pretty sure it's not in a vault. The bad news is that I'm pretty sure it's in the possession of one of them enormous zogre thingies. You might need to kill quite a few of them to find it.
So...erm... good luck? Stock up well!
Ha, of course you already have it. I never doubted you for a minute. Ok... maybe a minute, but probably no more than a few of em.
keysToEntries.Doric.QuestAccepted.GivenStaff.NotGotSwo: We're looking for a shadow sword. When I was investigating the area the clues I found pointed to one of those zogre things. You might need to kill quite a few of them.
Well now, there you have it! A shadow sword!
It's all like the story. You know, I used to tell my boy this story, good old Boric. He was so excited to hear the tale of heroic warriors and undead monsters.
To think, I'm standing in the very land this story took place. Boric would love to hear this... if... if I ever get a chance to see him again of course.
Ah don't mind me. Just a melancholic old man worried about his fully grown and perfectly capable boy. I just don't tell him enough how proud of him I am.
Anyway, here, have this. My little way of saying thank you for helping an old codger like me connect to happy memories.
I... I'm just gonna sit here with me memories for a bit. You go on now, don't let an old git like me sour your mood. Thank you again, friend.
Oh, a little overburdened I see. I'll hold onto this for you, come back and see me when you've got some space free.
keysToEntries.Doric.CompletedVisibleQuest.Note.Name: Doric's Note
keysToEntries.Doric.CompletedVisibleQuest.Note.Text: My boy was always afraid of monsters, poor lad. Scared of werewolves in the cupboard and all that.   I'll find him something to keep the beasts at bay. Bring him back something to make him as proud of his old dad as his dad is of him.
keysToEntries.Doric.InVault.Intro.1: Oh, er, hello there. I appear to have gotten myself into a bit of a pickle.
keysToEntries.Doric.InVault.Intro.2: I'd heard rumours of a special weapon here and figured, if I could get it, I could give it to Boric as a gift when I got home. Try and make up for being such a rubbish dad lately.
keysToEntries.Doric.InVault.Intro.NotGotDagger.1: It seems like I overestimated these old bones and the vault guardians here did me an injury. Don't suppose you've got a potion spare I can have?
keysToEntries.Doric.InVault.Intro.GotDagger.1: Ha, course, you already have the dagger recipe I was looking for. Shoulda guessed. Stupid Doric, never think things through.
keysToEntries.Doric.SpokenTo.DontGivePotion.1: Oh, don't worry about it. I'm sure these kidneys will fix themselves up soon enough.
keysToEntries.Doric.SpokenTo.GivePotion.1: Thanks, you really a hero, don't think enough people tell you that.
keysToEntries.Doric.SpokenTo.GivePotion.2: Here, tell you what, don't tell anyone else about this momentary lapse in judgement and I'll give you something special.
keysToEntries.Doric.SpokenTo.GivePotion.NoInventorySpa: Oh, you're a bit full up there I think. Chuck something away and then we can talk.
keysToEntries.Doric.SpokenTo.GivePotion.HasInventorySp: Thanks again for rescuing a silly old man who's still letting himself worry about his fully grown son. I guess I miss being the hero in my little boy's eyes.
Kids grow up so fast. Just a few decades, that's all you have. I wish I'd spent them better.
keysToEntries.Objective.FindSkeletonBow: Craft an Undead Ranger's Bow and bring it to Doric in Fellhollow. Doric believes the recipe is in a vault somewhere.
keysToEntries.Objective.NecromancerStaff: Craft a Necromancer's Staff and bring it to Doric in Fellhollow. Doric believes the recipe is in a vault somewhere.
keysToEntries.Objective.ShadowSword: Craft a Shadow Sword and bring it to Doric in Fellhollow. Doric believes a zogre has the recipe.
keysToEntries.Quest.Name: Doric's Quest
keysToEntries.Quest.Description: Doric is looking for rare weapons in Fellhollow.
keysToEntries.Doric.PostQuest: Thanks for all your help. Ashenfall really is home to some remarkable weapons. I wonder what else we'll uncover? My boy, Boric, would love this place.
keysToEntries.Doric.CollectorsCalling.Reward.FlavourTe: A cloth shoulder back, contains useful smithing materials.

keysToEntries.Zanik.QuestNotStarted.Intro.1: Strange place this, isn't it? Not just the zombies and the horrible red pools of awfulness, there's a sadness to it too. A misery, like a chasm stretching across everything.
keysToEntries.Zanik.QuestNotStarted.Intro.2: The dead don't pass on here, not really. The tethers that hold them to their bodies are stretched and snap against Imaru's hunger. Then they're devoured like evening snacks.
keysToEntries.Zanik.QuestNotStarted.Intro.3: Still, there are some souls yet to pass on. Holding tight against Imaru's gluttony. I want to help them in these final moments, take the burdens from them so they can pass on once the dragon's power has fully waned.
keysToEntries.Zanik.QuestNotStarted.Intro.4: As the ambassador to the underworld, I feel it's my duty to help these people out. Will you help me?
keysToEntries.Zanik.QuestNotStarted.Intro.NoGhostspeak: Oh, you'll need an amulet of ghostspeak first. I think there was one in Brynmoor, all the way back. Lost in the village somewhere. You'll need to find it.
keysToEntries.Zanik.QuestNotStarted.Intro.GotGhostSpea: Thank you. There are three lost souls all over Fellhollow, hidden in the remnants of human civilisation. I feel them, like a warm fire in this cold dark place.
I understand. If you change your mind, or if you find one of them on your journey, please come back and see me.
keysToEntries.Zanik.AskAbout.GhostLocations.1: I couldn't tell you where exactly, but I've got an inkling. Like an itch on the inside of my nose, pointing the way -- yeah, it's horrible, please find them.
keysToEntries.Zanik.AskAbout.GhostLocations.2: There's a village, long since fallen to ruin. There waits a spirit shivering against the wind and rain.
keysToEntries.Zanik.AskAbout.GhostLocations.3: North of here, where the world rots away from the inside, a spirit clings to the world with memories of a loved one.
keysToEntries.Zanik.AskAbout.GhostLocations.4: Crumbling against decaying ruins, near a waterfall of grief, a ghost finds herself trapped with memories of a loved one. She needs something returned to her that has been buried in shadow.
keysToEntries.Zanik.AskAbout.Underworld.1: It's not as bad as it sounds, the Underworld. A bit grim, sure, but manageable. Imagine a city, full of the dead, waiting to pass on. That's what it is really.
keysToEntries.Zanik.AskAbout.Underworld.2: Everyone just gets on with it. Similar to what it's like in the living world, only much less adventure.
keysToEntries.Zanik.AskAbout.Underworld.3: There's a great big river, the river Noumenon, that you can sail down on to whatever afterlife waits for you -- yeah, there's more than one. Each depending on your own beliefs. Or, if you're unlucky enough to be like me, the belief of the god's soul that's infected you.
keysToEntries.Zanik.AskAbout.Underworld.4: I didn't enjoy Bandos' afterlife. I wouldn't recommend it.
keysToEntries.Zanik.AskAbout.Afterlife.1: I had a bit of a god's soul in me when I died, so my afterlife didn't go very well. Bandos was, after all, pretty upset with me.
keysToEntries.Zanik.AskAbout.Afterlife.2: His afterlife was, unsurprisingly, a world of constant battle. The strong crushed the weak on an endless cycle. I... I tried to protect them. I failed... a lot.
keysToEntries.Zanik.AskAbout.Afterlife.3: It was to be an eternity of failure. Watching the innocent slain for no reason other than some brute's entertainment. Thankfully, a friend rescued me and I got a reprieve.
keysToEntries.Zanik.AskAbout.Ghosts.1: Mostly, the dead are more than happy to cross over. This world can make you weary, and it's nice to finally have an excuse to rest. But a ghost doesn't get that.
keysToEntries.Zanik.AskAbout.Ghosts.2: Some unfinished business keeps them trapped in place. Whether it be a mother desperately needing to know that her children are safe. Or some scholar who needs that elusive answer before they can let themselves sleep.
keysToEntries.Zanik.AskAbout.Ghosts.3: Some things are so important that they form a tether to our souls, that even Death's scythes can't cut. That's what a ghost is, a prisoner of ungiven closure.
keysToEntries.Zanik.QuestStarted.NoGhosts.1: There are three lost souls all over Fellhollow, hidden in the remnants of human civilisation. Each one has something stopping them from finding peace.
keysToEntries.Zanik.QuestStarted.SomeGhostsFound.1: Thank you for helping some of the spirits, but there are more still that need help finding peace. Please keep searching.
keysToEntries.Zanik.QuestStarted.GhostsFound.1: Thank you, I can feel them at peace, it's beautiful. The dead can get trapped in their issues, locked into a vicious cycle. You've stopped that.
keysToEntries.Zanik.QuestStarted.GhostsFound.2: Once all this Imaru business is dealt with, then Death will be able to help them move on properly. In the mean time, here, something to say thank you.
keysToEntries.Zanik.QuestStarted.GhostsFound.HasInvent: It's not much, I know, but I hope it helps.
keysToEntries.Zanik.QuestStarted.GhostsFound.DoesNotHa: Oh, you appear to be overburdened. I'll hold onto this for you until you've got some space free.
keysToEntries.Zanik.PostQuest.1: Thank you, I can feel them at peace, it's beautiful. The dead can get trapped in their issues, locked into a vicious cycle. You've stopped that.
keysToEntries.Zanik.PostQuest.2: Once Imaru's influence has vanished completely, the restless spirits here can rest properly.
keysToEntries.VillageGhost.NotSheltered.1: It's cold, don't you feel it? The wind feels like it's rushing through me, blowing away all the years I spent building this house.
keysToEntries.VillageGhost.NotSheltered.2: I poured my life and soul into these foundations. Created a home for myself that I can be proud of. But now it's gone, and I feel like I'm lost without it.
keysToEntries.VillageGhost.NotSheltered.3: I just want four walls and a roof? Is that so much to ask? A little shelter against the cold?
keysToEntries.VillageGhost.SpokenToNotSheltered.1: I just want four walls and a roof? Is that so much to ask? A little shelter against the cold?
keysToEntries.VillageGhost.Sheltered.1: I...I...I feel warm. I feel cosy. I feel like I'm home. Thank you, thank you friend. Now I can rest and move on once the dragon has been dealt with.
Thank you for the shelter, even if it was short-lived. It was nice to be out of the cold, even for a little while.
keysToEntries.ArtistGhost.NotGotBook.1: The afterlife is so dull, they don't tell you that in the books. Endless sameyness. Nothing changes. I want to yawn and yawn, but I can't even do that. No breath you see.
keysToEntries.ArtistGhost.NotGotBook.2: I just want to draw! Is that so much to ask? But my sketchbook is missing. My husband took it to work with him in the coal mine to the east. He likes to look at the pictures between swings.
keysToEntries.ArtistGhost.NotGotBook.3: But he never came back. I would have gone to look for him, but I got incinerated by a dragon. Which, you know, isn't ideal.
keysToEntries.ArtistGhost.NotGotBook.4: I miss him, sure, but I really miss my sketchbook. I've had decades to get over my grief, but the boredom lasts forever!
keysToEntries.ArtistGhost.SpokenTo.NoBook.1: I just want to draw! Is that so much to ask? But my sketchbook is missing. My husband took it to work with him in the coal mine to the east. He likes to look at the pictures between swings.
keysToEntries.ArtistGhost.SpokenTo.GotBook.1: My sketchbook! You found it! Oh, I can't wait to draw in it again.
keysToEntries.ArtistGhost.SpokenTo.GotBook.3: Oh, yeah, husband. Err... he OK? Dead you say? Into a fine paste you say? Oh dear, how tragic. I'll... err... I'll go cry now I guess?
keysToEntries.ArtistGhost.PostQuest.1: Oh, I'm so glad to have my sketch book back. I can't wait to draw everything around me. Now... where did I put a pencil?
keysToEntries.GrannyGhost.NotSpokenTo.1: I wonder how she's doing. Whether she managed to pass on safely. My granddaughter, Edra. She used to visit me often, always a smile on her face that could warm the coldest of winters.
keysToEntries.GrannyGhost.NotSpokenTo.2: Every time she visited, I would give her a clay doll. A little figurine that she could paint and display with the others, like a little village of clay friends.
keysToEntries.GrannyGhost.NotSpokenTo.3: I wish I had one of those clay dolls to remember her by now. It would help me find peace in these turbulent times.
keysToEntries.GrannyGhost.SpokenTo.NoDoll.1: I wish I had one of those clay dolls to remember her by now. It would help me find peace in these turbulent times.
keysToEntries.GrannyGhost.SpokenTo.HasDoll.1: That doll! That clay figurine! It's just like the ones I made for my granddaughter. Would... would you be so kind as to give it to me? It would help me find peace.
keysToEntries.GrannyGhost.SpokenTo.HasDoll.GiveDoll.1: Thank you. Thank you so much. I can rest now. Well... once the devouring force has gone at least. Thank you again, my friend. You have made this old fool very happy.
keysToEntries.GrannyGhost.SpokenTo.HasDoll.DontGiveDol: I understand, hard to let go of these things. They are delightfully pretty, my granddaughter loves... no... loved them.
keysToEntries.GrannyGhost.PostQuest.1: Thank you, again. I just know that Edra would have loved it.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice.DontGiveDecoration: [Don't give the ghost a clay decoration.]
keysToEntries.Quest.Name: Even More Restless Ghosts
keysToEntries.Quest.Description: Help Zanik lay some spirits to rest by finding them and completing their unfinished business.
keysToEntries.Objective.FindGhosts.1: Find ghosts and complete their unfinished business. (0/3)
keysToEntries.Objective.FindGhosts.2: Find ghosts and complete their unfinished business. (1/3)
keysToEntries.Objective.FindGhosts.3: Find ghosts and complete their unfinished business. (2/3)
keysToEntries.Ghost.Name: Ghost
keysToEntries.VillageGhost.Name: Windswept Ghost
keysToEntries.ArtistGhost.Name: Paint-spattered Ghost
keysToEntries.GrannyGhost.Name: Reminiscing Ghost
keysToEntries.SketchBook.AlreadyFound: Nothing interesting remains, just dust and old memories.
keysToEntries.SketchBook.Name: Dust-covered Sketchbook
keysToEntries.SketchBook.AlreadyFound.NotFinished: You already have this. You should return it to its owner.
keysToEntries.SketchBook.NotCollected.NoSpace: You find and old sketchbook, but your inventory is too full to collect it.
keysToEntries.GrannyGhost.SpokenTo.Generic: Oh, if only I had a little doll to remember my darling Edra by. They were such lovely decorative things, made out of clay as I recall.
keysToEntries.Sketchbook.Description: A ruined notebook, buried beneath the earth.
keysToEntries.Zanik.NotSpokenTo.AlreadyStarted: Oh, you're already looking into that? I knew I could count on you!

keysToEntries.PostiePete.LinkFromMainDialogue.FirstTim: Hey, while you're just being all bone idle here, mind helping me with something?
keysToEntries.PostiePete.Intro.NotRightNowReply.1: Oh, not to worry. When you've got a moment just... head on back and see me.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.Intro.SureReply.1: Great, knew you were the reliable sort. I've had a slight issue, by which I mean a pretty big issue by R.P.D.T standards... some post has gone missing.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.Intro.SureReply.2: I was just looking through my bag and then --WHOOSH! -- someone nabbed three letters and escaped into Fellhollow.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.Intro.SureReply.3: I did a quick look, but only thing I could see was someone shifty heading off to that little ruined house just outside the Fellhollow entrance. Give it a look would you?
keysToEntries.Objective.FindAndDeliver.1: Find and deliver letters for Postie Pete. 0/3
keysToEntries.Objective.FindAndDeliver.2: Find and deliver letters for Postie Pete. 1/3
keysToEntries.Objective.FindAndDeliver.3: Find and deliver letters for Postie Pete. 2/3
keysToEntries.PostiePete.SpokenTo.NotGotAnyLetters: Can you track down and deliver those lost letters for me? I saw something weird over by a little ruined house outside this entrance into Fellhollow, maybe start there?
keysToEntries.PostiePete.SpokeTo.GotFirstLetter.NotDel: Fantastic! You've gone one. No-body is as skilled at this quest malarkey as you are. If you could do me a favour and post that, maybe it'll help you find the next one?
keysToEntries.PostiePete.SpokeTo.GotFirstLetter.Delive: First letter sorted, wonderful! You're well a-head of the game here! Now then, any clues on the next one?
A weird leaf, half of it falling into the underworld? Sounds a bit like that door there, the one that leads into Fellhollow.
Hrmm, I think there's another door like this, just past the entrance, near that house you visited earlier.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.SpokenTo.GotSecondLetter.NotD: Fantastic! You've gone one. No-body is as skilled at this quest malarkey as you are. If you could do me a favour and post that, maybe it'll help you find the next one?
keysToEntries.PostiePete.SpokenTo.GotSecondLetter.Deli: Second letter sorted? Not an idle bone in your body is there! Now then, you found some spooky fluff? Let me take a look...
Oh dear... that's wool that is. Unspun wool of a ghostly sheep. If I know the dev--er, if I know the "gods" like I do, then you'll have some hidden around the place.
One of 'em will be right, the others will be some terrible joke that will fill you with well deserved rage. I'm betting puns are in your future. I'm so sorry.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.SpokenTo.GotThirdLetter.NotDe: Fantastic! You've gone one. No-body is as skilled at this quest malarkey as you are. If you could do me a favour and post that, maybe it'll help you find the next one?
keysToEntries.PostiePete.SpokenTo.GotThirdLetter.Deliv: Thanks for delivering those letters, here's the numb-skull that caused the problem in the first place.
Meet Parcel Pete, my far less handsome sibling and a junior employee of the R.P.D.T. Who seems to have gotten it into their incredible dense skull to try and "help"...

I've got him on report now, filling in his PIP as we speak. In the meantime let me give you this to say thanks. It's a decoration for the moment, but keep an eye on it and I might have some more letters to deliver, in exchange for some choice rewards.
keysToEntries.ParcelPete.QuestNotComplete: Sorry, bit distracted here trying to fill in this HR form, you should talk to old Postie here first.
keysToEntries.ParcelPete.QuestComplete.1: Sorry about messing you around there. I was just trying to help out my brother here, and, well, things got out of hand...
keysToEntries.ParcelPete.QuestComplete.2: Well, as out of hand as you can get when you don't have hands. No hard feelings, eh?
keysToEntries.ParcelPete.QuestComplete.3: I thought my skeleton disguise was pretty good there, don't you think? I bet I had you fooled! Haha!
keysToEntries.PostiePete.Name: Postie Pete
keysToEntries.ParcelPete.Name: Parcel Pete
keysToEntries.SuspiciousSkeleton.Name: Suspicious Skeleton
keysToEntries.SuspiciousSkeleton.QuestNotStarted.1: Don't mind me. I'm just being bone-idle for a bit. Haha! Get it? No, me neither, I hate puns.
keysToEntries.SuspiciousSkeleton.QuestStarted.Intro.1: Don't mind me. I'm just being bone-idle for a bit. Haha! Get it? No, me neither, I hate puns. What do you want?
keysToEntries.SuspiciousSkeleton.QuestStarted.ImLookin: I don't know what letter you're talking about!
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice.IJustWantToSolveThisQuest: I just want to solve this quest!
keysToEntries.SuspiciousSkeleton.ImLookingForLoveRespo: Well don't look at me. You're not my type!
keysToEntries.SuspcioousSkeleton.IJustWantToFinishThis: I don't know what letter you're talking about!
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice.ItsALetterFromPostiePete: It's a letter from Postie Pete.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice.WaitINeverMentionedALetter: Wait - I never mentioned a letter!
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice.DoYouKnowAnyoneThatMight: Do you know anyone that might?
keysToEntries.ParcelPete.ItsALetterFromPostiePeteRespo: Not a clue who that is. Sounds like a right twerp though. Sorry, can't help you.
keysToEntries.ParcelPete.WaitINeverMentionedALetterRes: Err... yes you did. Of course you did.
keysToEntries.ParcelPete.DoYouKnowAnyoneThatMightRespo: You could ask one of the zombies around here, they might have your letter. Not very talkative though.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice.YouveGotTheLetterDontYou: You've got the letter, don't you?
keysToEntries.ParcelPete.AlrightWhatDidISayReponse.1: You said... err... something about dragons probably? I dunno. Urgh, fine! You win.
keysToEntries.ParcelPete.OhYesYouDid.LoopedSolution.1: Oh, for Pete's sake! What are you, six? Urgh, fine! You win.
keysToEntries.ParcelPete.HereTakeYourStupidLetter: Here, take your stupid letter and go away!
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice.GiveItMeOrIllSmashYou: Give me it or I'll smash you!
keysToEntries.ParcelPete.SoIProveEverythingResponse: I... wait... what? Urgh, grammar is so hard! Urgh, fine! You win!
keysToEntries.ParcelPete.GiveItMeOrIllSmashYouResponse: I ain't scared of you! Besides, I don't have a hitbar, you can't hurt me!
keysToEntries.ParcelPete.IKnowYouHaveItResponse: No, you only THINK I have it, and now you should just go away and think about how bad you are at this!
keysToEntries.Postbox.WrongOne.Generic: You have no letters for this address.
keysToEntries.Postbox.Letter1.Correct: When you post the letter, you find a strange leaf that appears to partially fade into the spirit world. It reminds you strongly of one of those spectral doors, perhaps there's one nearby?
keysToEntries.Postbox.Letter2.Correct: You notice a small patch of ghostly looking fluff, looks almost like un-spun wool. Perhaps you should seek out its former owner.
keysToEntries.Sheep.Generic: Ewe have no idea why you'd be bothered with this right now.
keysToEntries.Sheep.Shaun.Name: Shaun
keysToEntries.Sheep.Simon.Name: Simon
keysToEntries.Sheep.Steve.Name: Steve
keysToEntries.Sheep.Stuart.Name: Stuart
keysToEntries.Sheep.Shaun.2: The ghostly sheep is chewing on a letter, you easily take it from its mouth.
keysToEntries.Sheep.Simon: On closer inspection this is actually a great big ball of spider web, filled with more spiders than your heart can handle. Best run away, very very fast!
keysToEntries.Sheep.Steve: On closer inspection this is just a cloud that's afraid of heights.
keysToEntries.Sheep.Stuart: On closer inspection this is two penguins in a sheep costume who shoo you away.
keysToEntries.SpectralDoorLetter.Name: Spectral Letter
keysToEntries.SpectralDoorLetter: You find one of the missing letters!
keysToEntries.Address.FH.PostiePete: Deliver to: Postie Pete - Death's Temple - Fellhollow
keysToEntries.Address.FH.KeepEntrance: Deliver to: Keep Entrance - Silverthorn Keep - Fellhollow
keysToEntries.Address.FH.FountainRuins: Deliver to: Fountain Ruins - Witchwillow Range - Fellhollow
keysToEntries.Address.FH.MinersCottage: Deliver to: Miner's Cottage Ruins - Coalridge Pass - Fellhollow
keysToEntries.Address.FH.WitheredRuins: Deliver to: Withered Ruins - Dragon's Run - Fellhollow
keysToEntries.Address.FH.ImarusTower: Deliver to: Imaru's Tower - Hope's Fall - Fellhollow
keysToEntries.Address.FH.Gatehouse: Deliver to: Gate House - Silverthorn Keep - Fellhollow
keysToEntries.Address.FH.FallenGates: Deliver to: Fallen Gates - Lake of Souls - Fellhollow
keysToEntries.Address.FH.EmberwoodCrossroads: Deliver to: Crossroads - Emberwood Village - Fellhollow
keysToEntries.Address.FH.WillowgroveRuins: Deliver to: Willowgrove Ruins - Silverthorn Keep - Fellhollow
keysToEntries.Address.FH.StandingStones: Deliver to: Standing Stones - Bleakfields Valley - Fellhollow
keysToEntries.Objective.FindAndDeliver.Generic: Find and deliver the three letters for Postie Pete.
keysToEntries.Quest.Address.FH.WitheredVillage: Deliver to: Withered Village - Bleakfields Valley - Fellhollow
keysToEntries.Item.Name: Letter
keysToEntries.Sheep.Shaun.NotGotSpace: The ghostly sheep is chewing on a letter, you could  easily take it from its mouth, if you had any space free.
keysToEntries.Skeleton.NotGotInventory: Here, take your stupid letter and go away! .., Oh come on? You've got your hands full anyway, why did you bother me this much when you can't even carry it?
keysToEntries.PostiePete.GotSomeLetters: Great, you've delivered some of those letters. Keep it up and you'll be well a-head of the rest of the delivery department.
keysToEntries.Quest.Name: Letters For The Dead
keysToEntries.Quest.Desc: Postie Pete needs some help finding and delivering some stolen letters around Bleakfields Valley in Fellhollow.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.PlayerHasLetterInInventory: Fantastic! You've got one of the letters. No-body is as skilled at this quest malarkey as you are. If you could do me a favour and post that, you can read the address by examining the letter.
keysToEntries.Quest.Address.Graveyard: Deliver to: Graveyard - Bleakfields Valley - Fellhollow
keysToEntries.quest.address.willowgrove: Deliver to: Willow Grove - Bleakfields Valley - Fellhollow
keysToEntries.Death.PostQuestLinkToPete: While you're around and if you have time to spare - which you do, I've checked my list - go and speak to Postie Pete. He has something he could use your help with.
keysToEntries.PostiePete.LinkFromFTUE: Oh! When you've done all of that, come back and see me, I've got something I could use a hand with. An actual hand, since I don't have them.
keysToEntries.PostBoxImaru.NoOthersLettersDelivered: Postie Pete suggested that something suspicious was waiting at the ruined house, outside the entrance to Fellhollow.
keysToEntries.Objective.SpeakToPete: Visit Postie Pete near the entrance to Fellhollow to begin searching for lost letters.
keysToEntries.SuspicousSkeleton.QuestStarted.ImLooking: Well, go look for something somewhere else!
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice.Doyouhavesomething: Do you have something you shouldn't?
keysToEntries.SuspciousSkeleton.Doyouhavesomethingyous: I haven't got any letters, that's for sure!
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice.TalkingAboutPete: Are you talking about Postie Pete's letters?

keysToEntries.IntroDialogue1: Wait! A person? An actual, real life person and not some shambling nightmare picking rubbish out of its teeth? Hello! Hello! Oh, also, help?
keysToEntries.IntroDialogue2: A... horrible necromancer trapped me in this mirror decades ago. Punishment for all my mistakes. I'm not saying I didn't deserve it, but it's been... well, an eternity, and I think I've served my time.
keysToEntries.IntroDialogue3: She even gave me the ritual to release myself to...err... taunt me, yes, taunt me. It's quite simple to resolve, really, it shouldn't take long.
keysToEntries.ReturnToMirrorNotAllCandlesLit1: I'll need you to rekindle my memories, quite literally.
keysToEntries.ObjectiveOne: Gather dragon blood, spectral silver thread and a ritual spellbook and bring them back to the mirror.
keysToEntries.ReturnNoItemsDialogue1: I just need you to grab a few things, so I can get myself out of this mirror.
keysToEntries.ReturnNoItemsDialogue2: I need dragon blood, it's the blood of the enemy and that always has mystical significance. I read about it in a book once.
keysToEntries.ReturnNoItemsDialogue3: Spectral silver thread will help me stitch the mirror back together. My seamstress, Edna, can help you there. You can find her little cottage in the centre of Dragon's Run. She's...err... quite thin these days.
keysToEntries.ReturnNoItemsDialogue4: Finally I need a copy of the spell itself. Using morse code and a very clever pigeon, I managed to order a copy from Agatha's mail-order magic -- but it got sent back to my shop, in my windmill in Emberwood village.
keysToEntries.GotAllIngredients1: Great, that's all the bits I need! Thank you so much... I'll take those off your hands for now, just press them to the glass. It's weird, I know, but it won't hurt.
keysToEntries.GotAllIngredients2: Wonderful, I can start getting the ritual ready on my end. I just need something to tether myself back to your side of reality. I'll need you to rekindle my memories, quite literally.
keysToEntries.PlayerDoesntHandOverIngredient_Response: Well, there's nothing I can do until you give me the ingredients. Don't worry, I'll be right here until you change your mind... right...here...
keysToEntries.GotAllIngredients3: When one lives as long as I have, one's memories get a little overcrowded and sometimes you just need to put them somewhere for safekeeping. I've left a few of mine as "memorial candles" in each region I've visited.
keysToEntries.GotAllIngredients4: For each candle you light, you'll rekindle a memory which will help me reconnect myself to this world and pull myself back through. I'll give you a spark to reignite them, just a small piece of my soul so the candles will ignite when you find them.
keysToEntries.GotAllIngrdients5: *A small greenish flame passes from the mirror and into the palm of your hand. It feels full of sorrow, rage and boundless grief.*
keysToEntries.ObjectiveTwo: Rekindle the memorial candles in Brynmoor, the Whispering Swamp, the Fractured Planes, the Stormtouched Highlands, Bloodblight Swap, and Fellhollow.
keysToEntries.ObjectiveTwoOne: Rekindle the memorial candles across Ashenfall.
keysToEntries.Candle_BeforeLit: No flame seems to touch this wick, as though it burns from something else.
keysToEntries.CandleBrynmoor: The soul ignites the candle. A memory that is not yours seeps into your thoughts. Distrustful eyes fall upon you. Scorn and derision from every corner. The priests shake their head at you. Only one face smiles back beneath a mask of bone.
keysToEntries.CandleWhisperingSwamps: The soul ignites the candle. A memory that is not yours seeps into your thoughts. A shared meal, tougher than it should be. A monster avoided through quick wit and guile. A silent conversation worth more than a thousand words.
keysToEntries.CandleFracturedPlains: The soul ignites the candle. A memory that is not yours seeps into your thoughts. Your fingers are warm, though his body grows colder with each second. There is no breath in his lungs and once bright eyes are cold and empty. A sorrow like no other grasps icy fingers around your heart and mercy dies to its frigid touch.
keysToEntries.CandleStormtouchedHighlands: The soul ignites the candle. A memory that is not yours seeps into your thoughts. Your pour your rage into the heavens, corrupting the veil a god placed there to protect. Dark magic turns clouds into weapons, blasting settlements with spears of lightning. You laugh, a cold and bitter sound, at the nightmare you've wrought.
keysToEntries.CandleBloodblightSwamp: The soul ignites the candle. A memory that is not yours seeps into your thoughts. You've grown to like the antipoison's taste, a pick-me-up first thing in the morning. The cave is damp, but oddly comforting. The bodies of seven garou keep you warm  as they burn. You wonder if you have a future in potions.
keysToEntries.CandleFellhollow: The soul ignites the candle. A memory that is not yours seeps into your thoughts. You stare into the mirror, guilt finally quelling the rage that always burns in your chest. The nightmare all around you, your fault, but who will punish you, if not yourself?
keysToEntries.QuestComplete1: Brilliant stuff! I can get the spell going now. I should be out of here in... what... a week or so.
keysToEntries.QuestComplete3: What? You don't need to stick around for that, I'm good now. I can stitch this mirror back together and then escape this reflective prison. You've done everything I need.
keysToEntries.QuestComplete4: Here, take this as a thank you. Who knows, we might see each other on the other side sometime. Your side, I mean, not this one... that wasn't a threat, we're cool.
keysToEntries.Goodbye: Thanks for your help, it'll be so nice to escape this mirror realm nightmare prison. Don't worry, I'm not going to go on some sinister evil rampage or anything. Honest.
keysToEntries.QuestDescription: Help free a mysterious figure from their mirror prison.
keysToEntries.NoSpaceForBook: You have no room in your inventory for this book, you'll need to make some space and come back to it later.
keysToEntries.SpellBookDesc: An ancient book of spells by Agatha's mail-order magic service. "Guaranteed to put warts on your nose, or your money back!"
keysToEntries.SpectralSilverThreadDesc: A spooky silver thread. Used to repair mirrors from the inside.
keysToEntries.IntroDialogue4.1: I just need you to grab a few things. Dragon blood, which should be plentiful. A copy of the spell from my old shop in Emberwood's windmill, and some spectral silver thread - my seamstress, Edna, can help you there.
keysToEntries.ReturnToMirrorNotAllCandlesLit2: When one lives as long as I have, one's memories get a little overcrowded and sometimes you just need to put them somewhere for safekeeping. I've left a few of mine as "memorial candles" in each region I've visited.
keysToEntries.ReturnToMirrorNotAllCandlesLit3: For each candle you light, you'll rekindle a memory which will help me reconnect myself to this world and pull myself back through. I'll give you a spark to reignite them, just a small piece of my soul so the candles will ignite when you find them.
keysToEntries.seamstressdontgive: The skeleton returns to rest, leaving you without your thread.
keysToEntries.Seamstressnospace: You have no room to take thisss thread. Return to me when space is made among your belongings.
keysToEntries.QuestFinished: Thanks for your help, it'll be so nice to escape this mirror realm nightmare prison. Don't worry, I'm not going to go on some sinister evil rampage or anything. Honest.
keysToEntries.SeamstressHasQuest: A mote of light runs between yourself and the skeleton and she turns her skull to face you and opens her arms out wide, expectantly.
keysToEntries.SeamstressNoIngredients: The mistress needssss...bring me a bar of silver, a spool of fine thread, a bottle of sacred oil, and six soul fragments and I shall weave what she needsssss.
keysToEntries.seamstresshasspace: The skeleton performs a series of gestures, too quick and strange for your eyes to follow and then hands you a spool of spectral silver thread. The skeleton returns to rest.
keysToEntries.Spellbook.Intro: A mysterious spellbook, from Agatha's Magical Delivery Service.

keysToEntries.Vannaka.WildHuntCompleted.NotSpokenTo.In: Young pup, it seems like the spirits here are in dire trouble and I'll need a little more of your help to put things right.
The land is angry. Enraged at the corruption forced upon it and in turn it fights back. Dangerous spirits of destruction roam this land in the fluffiest of forms.
Chinchompas. Ghostly little critters with explosive personalities. They threaten our efforts here, as the land does not understand we work in its interest.
It is the vet's hand that the dog bites most, after all. I need you to find these critters and hunt down as many as you can, before they explode and rip the land apart in the process.
If you bring me ten of their skulls, I can do what I can to help lay their spirits to rest. Perhaps we can curb some of this land's fury.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.QuestStarted.SpokenTo.NoProgress: Find those chinchompa spirits and put an end to them before they can put an end to you. If they explode, their essence will just reform somewhere else, so strike them before they can.
If you bring me their skulls, I can do what I can to help lay their spirits to rest. Perhaps we can curb some of this land's fury.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.GotHeads.1: Good, that looks like enough of their skulls to send a message. I'm not good with magic, but there are old rituals, primal and primordial, that the land will listen to.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.GotHeads.2: In time, thanks to this, and with Imaru's destruction, we may see the land return to the lush land it once was. In a few decades perhaps.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.GotHeads.NoInventoryFree.1: I've got something for you, a thank you of sorts, but you're a little overburdened. Clear out some of that backpack and then come back and see me.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.GotHeads.InventoryFree.1: Here, something for your hard work. It's not much, young pup, but it'll be a reminder of the good work you've done for this place.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.PostQuest.1: It'll take some time for the land to heal, but when it does it'll remember the good work you've done for it. The land always remembers.
Hrmm, what do you have there young pup? The spectral skulls of vengeful little spirits? I know what this means.
Hrmm, what do you have there young pup? The spectral skulls of vengeful little spirits? I know what this means.
You've got enough there for me to sort this mess out. An old ritual, primal and primordial nonsense -- not my favourite thing to work with, but needs must.
keysToEntries.Quest.Name: Things That Go Boom In The Night
keysToEntries.Quest.Description: Gather spectral chinchompa skulls so that Vannaka can help the land heal.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.QuestComplete.HasMoreSkulls.1: I see you've been hunting more of those spiritual pests. Nasty creatures those, best to get rid of them. Here, I'll take those skulls off your hands and give you something for your efforts.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.QuestComplete.HasMoreSkulls.Fini: If you find more of them, bring them to me. I'll take ten at a time, clear the problem up faster that way.
Or I would, if you weren't so overburdened. Clear some space, young pup, then come find me.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.LinkFromWitherBeast.1: You've earned a rest, but if you're looking for work come back and see me. I have another task I could use your help with.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.GenericGreeting: Hello there, young pup, what can I help you with?
keysToEntries.Objective.GatherSkulls: Gather 10 spectral chinchompa skulls and bring them to Vannaka in Fellhollow.

keysToEntries.BookInteraction: You find one of Abraxus' books.
keysToEntries.AbraxusNotWearingRobes_Intro: What do you want? I have little time for the likes of you. Only a member of my own order can assist me and you certainly don't look the part.
keysToEntries.AbraxusIntroDialogue_1: Ah, apprentice... I assume you're an apprentice, with robes that shabby what else could you be? Still, needs must. Apprentice, I require your assistance.
keysToEntries.AbraxusIntroDialogue_2: Yes, yes, I know, I know. You stand in the presence of Abraxus the Eternal, master of necromancy. Keeper of secrets both occult and arcane.
keysToEntries.AbraxusIntroDialogue_3: You are very blessed, I am sure. Now, I need you to assist me. My library has been ransacked by the locals and my most precious tomes spread across the eastern parts of this region.
keysToEntries.AbraxusIntroDialogue_4: Find them, all five of them, bring them to me and -- this is crucial -- do not read them. Your mind is too young, too limited to contain the dread secrets within! Well... hop to it!
keysToEntries.ObjectiveFindBooks: Search the eastern areas of Fellhollow and find the books for Abraxus.
keysToEntries.AbraxusNotWearingRobes_ReturnNoBooks1: In disguise are we? Blending in with the riff raff and the yobs? Not sure I agree with such a plan, but you do you.
keysToEntries.AbraxusNotWearingRobes_ReturnNoBooks2: I need you to find my books. Find them, all five of them, bring them to me and -- this is crucial -- do not read them. Your feeble mind cannot handle their secrets.
keysToEntries.AbraxusWearingRobes_ReturnNoBooks: I need you to find my books. Find them, all five of them, bring them to me and -- this is crucial -- do not read them. Your feeble mind cannot handle their secrets.
keysToEntries.AbraxusReturnWearingRobes_SomeBooks: Good, you've found some of my books, I need you to find the rest.
keysToEntries.ReturnWithAllBooks_1: Excellent, excellent, you've found my books. I will trust you were not fool enough to peak.
keysToEntries.ReturnWithAllBooks_2: However, your assistance is still required, apprentice. Three of my secret scrolls have been stolen and you must reclaim them for me.
keysToEntries.ReturnWithAllBooks_3: The first was stolen by a wretched green dragon known as Blightscale. Arrogant creature, you must make a lesson of her hubris.
keysToEntries.ReturnWithAllBooks_4: The second scroll was taken by a zogre known as Ulgo. Foul creature, stinks beyond comprehension, but I'm sure you can handle it.
keysToEntries.ReturnWithAllBooks_5: The last was taken by a rival of mine, Razlem - the deranged. A far less skilled necromancer than myself, but dangerous none the less.
keysToEntries.ObjectiveFindScrolls: Defeat Abraxus' enemies to retrieve the scrolls.
keysToEntries.ReturnSomeScrolls_1: Three of my secret scrolls have been stolen and you must reclaim them for me.
keysToEntries.ReturnSomeScrolls_2: The first was stolen by a wretched green dragon known as Blightscale. Arrogant creature, you must make a lesson of her hubris.
keysToEntries.ReturnSomeScrolls_3: The second scroll was taken by a zogre known as Ulgo. Foul creature, stinks beyond comprehension, but I'm sure you can handle it.
keysToEntries.ReturnSomeScrolls_4: The last was taken by a rival of mine, Razlem - the deranged. A far less skilled necromancer than myself, but dangerous none the less.
keysToEntries.ReturnAllScrolls_1: You have done well my apprentice and earned something no one else has earned in centuries -- my respect.
keysToEntries.ReturnAllScrolls_2: But I am not fool enough to believe you would find such a thing sufficient for your efforts, so, here, take this instead. May it serve you well.
keysToEntries.ReturnAllScrolls_3: Now, if you'll excuse me, I need to mail a pebble to my... to an old friend of mine. See if that will let me haunt them for a while. I am so bored of this view.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_1: Ah, hello apprentice. I'm sorry, I have no arrogant quips for you today... fate has been a most cruel mistress.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_2: Valerius here, he used to be a shining thunderstorm of genius. A wizard who could have made the world tremble if he could turn himself away from his books, just for a time.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_3: But, now... now he barely remembers his name...
keysToEntries.Dowdun_4: Still, what good is eternity if you cannot look after the ones you lo... those that matter most? So, I'll keep an eye on him for now.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_5: Maybe, one day, he'll even remember me.
keysToEntries.Book1_Desc: A study on elemental forces and their interactions with runic energies.
keysToEntries.Book1_Dialogue_1: Among all the technical jargon, a note falls out.
keysToEntries.Book1_Dialogue_2:
On summer days, with bluer skies,
I am oft reminded of your eyes. 
In winter nights, by the fire we roast,
And I think about what I love the most.
keysToEntries.Book1_Dialogue_3:
It's your sparkling wit, your wicked smile.
With your every word, you do beguile.
keysToEntries.Book1_Dialogue_4: *Great scratch marks of pencil obscure the rest of the page.*
keysToEntries.Book2_Desc: A badly burned book wrapped in leather hide... at least you hope it's leather.
keysToEntries.Book2_Dialogue_1: The book is written in the language of Infernus and impossible to read. However, a perfumed note is stuffed between the pages...
keysToEntries.Book2_Dialogue_2: My dearest Abraxus, this keep is full of madmen and morons. People to whom power means strength and might, rather than unraveling the secrets of the universe.
keysToEntries.Book2_Dialogue_3: We are looking to create a demonic portal, that we might summon beings from another world. Imagine! We could learn so much!
