keysToEntries.Book2_Dialogue_4: Yet they will just try and raise an army. Urgh! How I wish you were here with me. We could show them what a real wizard can do.
keysToEntries.Book3_Desc: A sinister book of truly nasty curses.
keysToEntries.Book3_Dialogue_1: This book appears to be a grimoire of terrible curses. When you open it a crumpled note falls out.
keysToEntries.Book3_Dialogue_2: You make me so angry Abraxus. A fire I cannot put out. I want to shout at you. I want to kick you down the stairs. I want to wrap my arms around you and scream into your chest.
keysToEntries.Book3_Dialogue_3: You make me a very undignified wizard, Abraxus. Very undignified indeed.
keysToEntries.Book4_Desc: A religious tome discussing the god of rebellion.
keysToEntries.Book4_Dialogue_1: This book appears to be a religious dissertation on the merits of the Zamorak faith. When you open it a letter falls out.
keysToEntries.Book4_Dialogue_2: Dear Abraxus, I hope this letter finds you well. Dowdun Reach is proving both fascinating and infuriating in equal measure.
keysToEntries.Book4_Dialogue_3: Whilst we are not limited by the prattling sensibilities of the village wizards, they replace it with a sort of blind aggression and anger against everyone.
keysToEntries.Book4_Dialogue_4: Still, I do wish you would join me. I think you might find there are wonderful secrets to uncover when one is not shackled by pointless morality.
keysToEntries.Book5_Desc: This book squirms in your backpack, as though trying to escape.
keysToEntries.Book5_Dialogue_1: This tome writhes beneath your fingertips, as if infused with some dread spirit. It practically spits a letter out at you.
keysToEntries.Book5_Dialogue_2: Dear Valerius, I am too cowardly to send this letter. Too afraid that you might burn it, or laugh at it, or worse still, ignore it. But if I do not say it now, I never will.
keysToEntries.Book5_Dialogue_3: *The letter is torn in half before any more can be said.*
keysToEntries.Scroll1_Desc: A sealed scroll containing dark secrets.
keysToEntries.Scroll2_Desc: A sealed scroll full of forbidden magics.
keysToEntries.Scroll3_Desc: A sealed scroll full of the worst things imaginable.
keysToEntries.QuestDesc: Find stolen knowledge and return it to its owner. I'm sure nothing bad will happen because of it.
keysToEntries.ObjectiveFindScrollsAndReturn: Defeat Abraxus' enemies to retrieve the scrolls. Then return them to Abraxus.
keysToEntries.ObjectiveFindBooksAndReturn: Find five of Abraxus’ missing books in eastern Fellhollow and return them to him.
keysToEntries.IntroAlreadyHasBooks_1: Oh, but you already have my books. Are you in league with the thief, perhaps? Or are you simply more efficient than expected. No matter, your work is appreciated.
keysToEntries.Scroll1_Text: You find one of Abraxus' scrolls. The writing crawls along the page making it impossible to read without strange arcane knowledge.
keysToEntries.Scroll2_Text: You find one of Abraxus' scrolls. Each time you look at the scroll, you could swear the text is different to the last time you looked.
keysToEntries.Scroll3_Text: You find one of Abraxus' scrolls. It's too awful, you cannot bring yourself to read it.
keysToEntries.Objective.FoundBookOrScrollBeforeAbraxus: Find the owner of the sinister text.

keysToEntries.Vannaka.Intro.1: Not a pleasant landscape this, ey, young pup? Life is fading, drained away by a dragon's hunger. Rotten creatures, dragons.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.Intro.2: Even this willow grove, once home to a proud and magnificent nature spirit, is now corrupted by wither and rot. That poor spirit has withered away to nothing.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.Intro.3: If this land is ever going to heal, we need to save the rest of those spirits and the only way to save them, is to hunt them and kill them.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.Intro.4: Don't worry, they're meant to be hunted. That's the spirit way, a spirit of nature should be hunted by a predator, not slowly drained away by a monster, and you're the apex predator here.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.Intro.5: You'll be doing this place a favour by hunting them, trust me. Look for willow groves, like this one, those are places where nature spirits thrive. Bring me three of their hearts and we can find a way to keep them safe from this withering hunger.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.QuestStarted.NotProgressed.1: Look for willow groves, like this one, those are places where nature spirits thrive. There's a few to the west of here. Bring me three of their hearts and we can find a way to keep them safe from this withering hunger.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.QuestStarted.NotGotHearts.1: Majestic creatures, aren't they? I'm sure it feels strange to have to hunt them, but trust me we're doing them a favour. You're part way there, but I'll need more hearts.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.QuestStarted.NotGotHearts.2: Look for willow groves, those are places where nature spirits thrive. There's a few to the west of here. Bring me the spirits' hearts and we can find a way to keep them safe from this withering hunger.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.QuestStarted.GotHearts.1: You did it, well done. Now, the tricky part. I asked you to get these hearts because of a garou ritual I learned. An old one, from their time before Kuldra.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.QuestStarted.GotHearts.2: It's a way to liberate the spirit from this place. Allow it to run free and wild, bound to a new anchor, one able to give them freedom.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.QuestStarted.GotHearts.3: That anchor is you. Now, one moment and... here... that should do it. With a little magic, that spirit will carry you across Ashenfall. Give it a good run, won't you?
keysToEntries.Vannaka.PostQuest.NoHearts.1: How are you and your new friend getting on? Running wild and free, I hope. See, you did them a favour by hunting them here. Now they're safe from this land's blighted curse.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.PostQuest.LessThanThreeHearts.1: You and your spirit friend seem happy together, young pup. Always good to see that sort of bond between man and beast.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.PostQuest.LessThanThreeHearts.2: Ah, but I see you've been liberating more nature spirits. Good work. It may feel strange, but you're doing them a favour, trust me. Tell you what, bring me three of them and I'll give you some of those shiny stones Death seems so interested in.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.PostQuest.GotHearts.1: You and your spirit friend seem happy together, young pup. Always good to see that sort of bond between man and beast.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.PostQuest.GotHearts.2: You've gathered another three hearts for me from the nature spirits? Good, very good. You're a skilled hunter, young pup, you should be proud.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.PostQuest.GotHearts.3: I'll take them off your hands and you can take these in their place. Shiny little stones that old Harold loves so much.
keysToEntries.Quest.Name: The Wild Hunt
keysToEntries.Quest.Description: Help Vannaka safeguard Fellhollow's nature spirits, by hunting them down, in willow groves, and collecting their hearts.
keysToEntries.Objective.HuntBeast: Hunt down Nature Spirits near Fellhollow's willow groves and bring three of their hearts to Vannaka.
keysToEntries.Objective.BringHeartNotStarted: Bring the Primordial Heart to someone with an interest in the wild hunt.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.Name: Vannaka
keysToEntries.Vannaka.NotStarted.GatheredAllThreeHeart: Ah... but your a skilled hunter, young pup, I see you've already gathered them.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.NotStarted.GatheredAtLeastOneHea: I see you're part way there already. Just need to get the rest and we can help this spirit escape Imaru's foul curse.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.NoInventorySpace: Ah, or I would give them to you if you had any room. Make some space and then come back and talk to me.
keysToEntries.Vannaka.PostQuest.Info.1: If you find another three hearts on your travels, bring them to me and I'll exchange them for some of those shiny soul fragments that Harold seems so fond of.

keysToEntries.Doric.Intro.1: I don't like this place, it's creepy and weird. Had to whack several zombies with my hammer and now it's got this weird purple ooze on it that won't wash off.
keysToEntries.Doric.Intro.2: Still, I'm betting there's something interesting waiting around here. There are vaults in this place and they must be loaded with dragonkin secrets.
keysToEntries.Doric.Intro.3: Who knows what you might find? I discovered one vault, and I was all geared up to visit, but it's locked down tight. Hey, I know, if you get a chance why don't you take a look? Let me know what you find inside.
keysToEntries.Doric.QuestStarted.NoProgress.1: Taken a look in that locked vault yet? There must be a trick to it. Those dragonkin were a smart lot, secretive... and arrogant as heck.
keysToEntries.Doric.QuestStarted.NoProgress.2: If you can find your way inside, let me know if you discover anything interesting.
keysToEntries.Doric.FoundStatue.1: Well, will you look at that, another dwarven tablet. Why were the dragonkin out here stealing dwarven secrets?
keysToEntries.Doric.FoundStatue.2: I guess we'll never know. Here, let me translate this for you, hopefully it'll help you on your travels.
keysToEntries.Doric.FoundStatue.3: Well now, that is unexpected. This is the Salve amulet... why is this here? It requires a gemstone only found by the river Salve back in Misthalin. I mean, it's handy against the undead, so it'll be real useful here.
keysToEntries.Doric.FoundStatue.4: Oh! Ha, you're gonna laugh at this, but I actually have one of them river Salve gemstones. I found one a few years back and I just keep it with me as a good luck charm. Here, you should take it, make yourself one of those salve amulets.
keysToEntries.Doric.FoundStatue.HasInventorySpace.1: Hope it helps in your travels.
keysToEntries.Doric.FoundStatue.DoesNotHaveInventorySp: Oh, you don't appear to have the space for it. Come back and see me when you do.
keysToEntries.Quest.Name: Seeking Salvation
keysToEntries.Quest.Description: Find the statues to gain entrance to the vault and uncover the secrets that lie within.
keysToEntries.Objective.FindRecipe: Access the vault and find the hidden secret within.
keysToEntries.Objective.ReturnToDoric: Bring the dwarven writing to Doric.
keysToEntries.Objective.LookForDoric: Find someone in Fellhollow that can translate dwarvish text.
keysToEntries.Doric.Name: Doric
keysToEntries.Doric.PostQuest.1: Hope that amulet helps you out. There are a lot of them shamblin'orribles out there and anything to keep 'em off you feels like a good thing.
keysToEntries.Statue.Discover.1: You find strange dwarven writings that you cannot read. Perhaps Doric can translate for you.
keysToEntries.Statue.Discover.NotStarted.1: You find strange dwarven writings that you cannot read. Perhaps someone can translate for you.
keysToEntries.Door.SealBroken: You hear the sound of a dragonkin lock releasing.
keysToEntries.Crystal.Name: Salve Crystal
keysToEntries.Crystal.Description: A crystal that Doric obtained from a cave near the river Salve, back in Misthalin. Used in creating the Salve amulet.

keysToEntries.Journal2:
I have chosen here to rest, to close my eyes and never see the dawn. I am tired, so tired, and will welcome the longest, deepest sleep.

I hope my companions found rest in their final moments. Between us all, we will keep the secret.
keysToEntries.Journal3:
A bunch of backstabbers, each of them, cowards and fools. The wounds we gave to one another guaranteeing no one will ever find our treasures.

A shared plunder, between us all it lays buried deep.
keysToEntries.Journal4:
I wonder if we shouldn't have left it buried. If we should have brought it back to the village for all to see. What wonders, what secrets, could we reveal? Answers to questions we never knew we had. 

Now, between the four of us, that secret lays buried. I wonder if that is our greatest mistake?
keysToEntries.Journal1:
We have plundered the very depths of Fellhollow and perhaps we have dug too deep? What lays buried should stay buried.

Each of us turned from the other, back to back and walked away. Not one of us alone knows the location, only together will the secret be revealed. 

And we are no longer talking.
keysToEntries.IntroJournal:
They say you cannot take it with you, yet people certainly try. Kings, pharaohs and emperors are all buried with enough gold to keep a kingdom running for years. So, if they cannot take it with them, maybe someone else can.

That someone, is me. There is treasure in Fellhollow, even though the villagers claim otherwise. The richest of them bury their gold with flimsy clues so that their descendants may uncover their riches and live as kings.

I'm sure I'm related to one of them, somewhere down the line, so I figure I'm as entitled to that wealth as anyone else.

I don't know where the treasure is buried, but others have gone looking. Four others have sought these riches and between them I'm sure those riches lie.

keysToEntries.WoM_FS_Intro1: Welcome to Ashenfall, sorry about the mess. Had a bit of a dragon problem, that's why we've called for you.
keysToEntries.WoM_FS_Intro2: You've come at a great time. Heroic opportunities await. This long-forgotten place is just screaming to be explored.
keysToEntries.WoM_FS_Intro3: But before all that, how about we take you through some survival basics?
keysToEntries.Player_Choice_FS_SkipTutorial: I can work it out [Skip Tutorial]
keysToEntries.WoM_FS_AcceptedTutorial1: You'll want to get to grips with gathering and crafting first, Doric can help you with that.
keysToEntries.WoM_FS_AcceptedTutorial2: You'll find him outside the temple. Check your map if you get lost, but he should be easy to spot.
keysToEntries.WoM_FS_AcceptedTutorial3: He's the only dwarf on the continent, that we know of anyway, can't miss him.
keysToEntries.WoM_FS_SkippedTutorial1: Fair enough, I do like someone that can show initiative.
keysToEntries.WoM_FS_SkippedTutorial2: But not here. Come find me in Bramblemead Village, and we'll get you started.
keysToEntries.WoM_Generic: Aren't you helping the others out? What are you talking to me for? Check your map if you're not sure what you're meant to be doing.
keysToEntries.WoM_FS_DragonSlayer_Started: Oh, don't mind me here. I'm just enjoying the architecture. I'll meet you at Bramblemead Village if you want to chat.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_Intro1: By Saradomin's big bushy beard! Just our luck, a great ruddy dragon. Think it were him that got the others - may they rest in peace.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_Intro2: And look at ya! Not a knife to yer name, no, no, we can't be doing with that. Come on, let's get you prepared. You need to go and craft a stone club.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_Intro3: It won't kill a dragon, but it's better than nothing and it'll help you live a bit longer, giving you time to make something better.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_Intro4: You'll need to gather stone and ash logs, which you can find laying around, then you'll want to build a crafting table to craft the club.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_Intro5: Oh, I'm Doric by the way. Resident blacksmith and craftsperson on this expedition. Nice to meet you. Now, stop standing around and go make that club!
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_NotMadeClub1: You'll want to build yourself a crafting table. Then once you've done that craft yourself a stone club.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_NotMadeClub2: Both require stone and ash logs, which you'll find scattered all over the ground around here.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_MadeClub1: Aye, that's a fine club you got there, but even the best weapons break over time. When that happens use a crafting bench to repair your gear.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_MadeClub2: Before you go, here, something to help you on your way.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_RC_Intro1: Well met, young pup. Looking to get yourself ready to face off against a dragon? You'll need more than that potato masher to kill one, but you've got to start somewhere.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_RC_Intro2: Look, you may have a weapon, but every great warrior knows wars are won by the stomach, not the blade.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_RC_Intro3: Behind me, up that little hill, is a windmill infested with rats. I want you to kill some of them and cook their meat.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_RC_Intro4: Don't pull that face, you can't be a picky eater in a place like this. Food will make you healthier and heartier, and much more likely to survive.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_RC_Intro5: Properly cook a rat roast and bring them back to me. You'll probably burn a few along the way, don't let that disuade you. No one said survival would be pretty.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_RC_NoProgress1: Well met, young pup. Seems like you haven't managed to cook that rat roast for me yet. You can get them by killing giant rats and cooking their meat. You can find some up by the windmill.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_RC_HasKebabs1: Well met, young pup. Seems your hunt was successful, I see you've gathered the food I asked for.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_RC_HasKebabs2: It may not exactly be gourmet cooking, but a meal like this could make the difference between life and death. You'll not be taking on any dragons if you're starving to death.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_RC_HasKebabs3: You will find rats are annoyingly resilient to clubs. Some enemies can resist certain damage types and be vulnerable to others.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_RC_HasKebabs4: Rats, for example, don't take kindly to getting stabbed. Here, take this little butterknife to make it easier to fight them in future. You can have this as well, it'll help you with what's next.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_RC_HasKebabs5: Right, let's teach you a bit about magic. Zanik's the one to talk to for that, you can find her down the road just before the river.
keysToEntries.Zanik_RM_LinkToWoM2: Better not keep him waiting too long, he gets real cranky.
keysToEntries.Zanik_RM_LinkToWoM1: Right, now that you've learned the basics, it's time to get started. The Wise Old Man is waiting for you just outside Bramblemead Village. Follow your compass to find him.
keysToEntries.Zanik_RM_HasRunes2: You'll unlock new spells as you improve your skills. Here, this book should help you toward that. Have a play around with them, you'll find they can be incredibly useful.
keysToEntries.Zanik_RM_HasRunes1: Great work! Runes are the lifeblood of magic, you'll need them to cast spells and use magic staves. A well placed spell can make all the difference.
keysToEntries.Zanik_RM_HasLogs_NoRunes: Not made the runes yet? You'll need to mine essence from the floating geysers and then craft air and astral runes on a rune altar.
keysToEntries.Zanik_RM_HasLogs5: See those geysers around, with the floating rocks? Take this pickaxe and mine some essence from them, build a rune altar and then craft the runes from rune essence.
keysToEntries.Zanik_RM_HasLogs4: But, magic comes at a cost -- runes. You'll need both air and astral runes to cast Axtral projection, so here's how to get them.
keysToEntries.Zanik_RM_HasLogs3: Axtral projection will let you slice down trees with a wave of your fingers. Much less time consuming than the lumberjack gig.
keysToEntries.Zanik_RM_HasLogs2: Fortunately, we've got magic! As you improve in a skill you'll discover spells that you can use, such as my favourite - Axtral Projection.
keysToEntries.Zanik_RM_HasLogs1: Great, you've got them. Now, between you and me, that was a bit repetitive right? I'd be exhausted having to do that all the time.
keysToEntries.Zanik_RM_Intro1: Dragons, it had to be dragons. Oh, hi, sorry, nice to meet you, I'm Zanik. I'm the local survival and goblin expert around here.
keysToEntries.Zanik_RM_SpokenTo_NoLogs: Cut down trees and gather 50 ash logs, then bring them to me.
keysToEntries.Zanik_RM_Intro4: About fifty ash logs should do it, so make sure you've got a well maintained axe. Shouldn't take too long, come back to me when you have them.
keysToEntries.Zanik_RM_Intro3: Here, I'll show you. First, let's look at things without magic to help you. I need you to cut down a couple of trees and bring me some ash logs. Simple, right?
keysToEntries.Zanik_RM_Intro2: The interesting thing about Ashenfall is that it's laced with magic. Anima everywhere, pouring out of the ground. Which we can use to our advantage.
keysToEntries.Doric_NoRoomInInventory: Oh dear, you seem a little overburdened at the moment. Come and see me when you've got some space free in your backpack.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_NoRoomInInventory: Hrmm, seems you're carrying the weight of the world right now. Come see me again when you've got some space free in your backpack.
keysToEntries.Zanik_NoRoomInInventory: Oh wow, bit of a packrat are we? You're carrying too much right now, come back and chat with me when you've cleared some space from your backpack.
keysToEntries.Doric_Generic: Gathering resources and crafting items, that's the basics of survival in this place. Seek out new resources to discover new recipes.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_Generic: Life on Ashenfall is rarely peaceful. Make sure you're well fed and always have a weapon to hand. Keep an eye on enemy weaknesses to dispatch them quickly.
keysToEntries.Doric_SkippedWom: Oh dear, you just walked right past the Wise Old Man. He won't like that. Best go back and say hi, eh?
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_NextStep: Now you've got something to fight with, go chat to Vannaka over to the north east, he can teach you how to keep fighting fit.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_MadeDagger1: Oh, you made a dagger, not a club? Aye, it's a nice piece of work, not what I asked for, but that's fine.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_MadeDagger2: Now, even the best weapons break over time. When that happens use a crafting bench to repair your gear.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_RC_NotDoneRTftJ: Getting ahead of yourself, aren't we, young pup? Check your map to see where you're meant to be headed.
keysToEntries.Zanik_Generic: There are secrets hidden all over Ashenfall, I'm sure of it. Look around, explore, and see what you can find.
keysToEntries.Zanik_NotDoneRTftJ: I think someone is looking for you. Mentioned a task they needed you to complete. Check your map for where to find them.
keysToEntries.Zanik_NotDoneRC: Vannaka was looking for you, mentioned something about fine dining?
keysToEntries.LO_Description: Zanik wants to teach you the basics of woodcutting.
keysToEntries.RTftJ_Description: Doric wants to teach you some survival basics.
keysToEntries.RC_Description: Vannaka wants to teach you the basics of hunting, fighting and cooking.
keysToEntries.RM_Description: Zanik wants you to learn the basics of crafting runes.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_RC_Intro_Dagger: Well met, young pup. Looking to get yourself ready to face off against a dragon? You'll need more than that butter knife to kill one, but you've got to start somewhere.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_Intro_WaterNeeded1: But look at you, all skin and bones. Must be proper parched after a journey like that.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_Intro_WaterNeeded2: Dehydration and starvation are deadlier than dragons, so you're going to need keep an eye on your basic needs, like hunger and thirst.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_Intro_WaterNeeded3: I've marked two ponds on your map, to the northwest and south of us. Grab some water from there and bring it back here. Then I'll teach you what you'll need to survive.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_Intro_WaterNeeded4: If you see a redberry bush along the way, grab yourself a handful. Not much of a meal, but a little food is better than no food at all.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_GotWater1: Ah good, you've got some water. Bit dirty, I know, but better than nothing. Be careful though, the water's tainted and it'll make life difficult if you drink too much.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_GotWater2: Zanik says boiling it sorts it all out. So might want to give that a look later, when you've got a chance. Anyway, time we got you all set up to survive this harsh land.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_Intro_AlreadyGotWater1: Ah, but you've got a good head on yer shoulders. I see you've already got some water, so you won't die of thirst.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_Intro_AlreadyGotWater2: Still, it's going to be tough surviving this place with a dragon about.
keysToEntries.WS_WiseOldMan_Objective1: Speak to the Wise Old Man to learn about Lifewards.
keysToEntries.WS_WiseOldMan_Objective2: Craft a Small Wardstone at a Crafting Table and bring it to the Wise Old Man.
keysToEntries.WS_WiseOldMan_Objective3: Bring your Small Wardstone to the Wise Old Man.
keysToEntries.WS_WiseOldMan_Intro1: Ah, I see you've discovered Ashenfall's little "blacksmith benefit", as I like to call it. Lifeward!
keysToEntries.WS_WiseOldMan_Intro2: Crafting certain equipment intrinsically involves the weaving of small threads of enchantment, granting you greater protection.
keysToEntries.WS_WiseOldMan_Intro3: Handy stuff, especially with dragons about. Now, you'll find that your Lifewards can get depleted by the brutality of the other creatures on Ashenfall.
keysToEntries.WS_WiseOldMan_Intro4: To recharge them, you'll need to craft yourself wardstones. These impressive little doohickies will recharge the Lifeward enchantments on your armour... at a small cost.
keysToEntries.WS_WiseOldMan_Intro5: A bit of damage to your soul. Don't worry, it's not permanent, but perhaps avoid overindulging in Wardstones, eh?
keysToEntries.WS_WiseOldMan_Intro6: Go and craft me one small wardstone and bring it here so I can check your working. You just need to gather some rune essence and stone, and craft it at a crafting table. Nice and simple. One small wardstone will do.
keysToEntries.WS_WiseOldMan_NotMadeStone: Go and craft a wardstone and bring it here so I can check your working. You just need to gather some rune essence and stone, and craft them at a crafting table. Nice and simple. One wardstone will do.
keysToEntries.WS_WiseOldMan_MadeStone1: Ah, you've made one. Lovely. See, that barely hurt at all. Just a little drop of blood, nothing to cry about. But now, when your lifeward is damaged, you can repair it.
keysToEntries.WS_WiseOldMan_MadeStone2: Just use a wardstone and BAM! Instant Lifeward recharge. Sure, it'll tear a bit out of your soul, but so will listening to Doric talk about hammers for three hours.
keysToEntries.WS_WiseOldMan_MadeStone3: Just don't use too many at once, and you'll be fine. Right, go on, get out there and slay that dragon!
keysToEntries.WS_QuestDesc: The Wise Old Man wants to teach you about Lifewards and how to recharge them.
keysToEntries.WS_WiseOldMan_NoInventorySpace: Hrmm, I have something for you, but it appears you've overloaded yourself like a magpie gone mad. Clear some space, then come and see me.
keysToEntries.Playerchoice_TellMeAboutAshenfall: Can you tell me about Ashenfall?
keysToEntries.Playerchoice_IKnowYou: I recognise you from the wanted posters!
keysToEntries.WoM_SkippedTutorial_WhoAreYou_1: Oh, the cruelty of time, erasing even our deeds. I was once a famous and adored hero. Now everyone just calls me "that wise old man". Rude really.
keysToEntries.WoM_SkippedTutorial_IKnowYou_1: A lifetime of heroism, and no recognition. But you rob one bank, and suddenly everyone knows your name!
keysToEntries.WoM_SkippedTutorial_CanYouTellMeAboutAsh: We've not been here that long, but I can tell you that Ashenfall is special. You must feel it? Magic everywhere?
The whole continent feels like its infused with anima. Literally pouring out of the ground in some places. That's just not normal.
Probably goes someway to explaining the enormous dragon that wants to kill us... along with all the other things that want to kill us.
keysToEntries.Doric_SkippedTutorial_Intro_1: Strange place this. Ashenfall, I mean. Could make a real home here, if things stopped trying to kill us.
keysToEntries.Doric_SkippedTutorial_WhoAreYou_1: Me? Name's Doric. I'm just a stupid old blacksmith that got swept up in tales of adventure. Trying to win back me glory days.
keysToEntries.Doric_SkippedTutorial_WhoAreYou_2: Left my son behind to watch the shop. Good lad, gonna do good things. Hope he's not too worried about his dad.
keysToEntries.Doric_SkippedTutorial_WhoAreYou_3: Still, no point lollygagging around. I'll catch my breath and then explore these new lands. Who knows? Might find something.
keysToEntries.Doric_SkippedTutorial_CanYouTellMeAboutA: It's a wild place this. Beasties everywhere, and no civilisation in sight. Just the remnants, ghosts of the past.
But you see that great tower, off in the distance? That's dragonkin that is. Means there's probably stuff worth finding about this place.
keysToEntries.Zanik_SkippedTutorial_Intro1: A new adventure is always exciting, don't you think? We lost some good people... friends... but we need to push forward, in their memory.
keysToEntries.Zanik_SkippedTutorial_WhoAreYou_1: I'm Zanik, I'm a cave goblin. Sort of like the goblins over there in Bramblemead village... only we chose a peace over violence.
keysToEntries.Zanik_SkippedTutorial_WhoAreYou_2: Most of my people stay underground, hiding from the surface. But I couldn't wait to explore this vast and wonderful world.
keysToEntries.Zanik_SkippedTutorial_WhoAreYou_3: I'm regretting it a bit, what with big scary dragons and all, but I'm excited too. Looking forward to learn everything about this place.
keysToEntries.Zanik_SkippedTutorial_CanYouTellMeAboutA: There are goblins here, that's unexpected. I thought I knew about all the tribes, but there's a whole load of them here.
I wonder why they're not in the history books.
As well as goblins, look at the creatures here. So vibrant and full of life. This whole place feels... magical.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_SkippedTutorial_Intro_1: Keep your wits about you, young pup. Ashenfall is home to many a dangerous beast, and I've not yet tested my blade against each of them.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_SkippedTutorial_WhoAreYou_1: My name is Vannaka and back on the mainland I was a Slayer Master. It was my job to send adventurers after dangerous beasts.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_SkippedTutorial_WhoAreYou_2: Cull the population of monsters, to make the world a better place.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_SkippedTutorial_WhoAreYou_3: I was finding things a little stale on the mainland. When I saw the posters for the expedition, I felt a pull... a call to adventure that I could not resist.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_SkippedTutorial_CanYouTellMeAbou: Be ready to fight, that's my first piece of advice. Craft armour, weapons and anything else you need for battle.
There are goblins everywhere in Bramblemead and deadlier things beyond.
Past Bramblemead is Ghornfell, a place of danger and excitement. Something calls to me out there and I intend to seek it out.
keysToEntries.WS_WiseOldMan_Wardstone_Choice_01: Ah, hello. What would you like to discuss?
keysToEntries.WS_Playerchoice_Askabout_DragonSlayer: Ask about the Dragon Slayer quest.
keysToEntries.Doric_RTftJ_GotCleanWater1: Ah, good, you've got some nice clean water there. The water in lakes and rivers tends to be dirty, tainted by something nasty that will make life difficult if you drink too much.

keysToEntries.Quest.Name: Growing Pains
keysToEntries.Quest.Description: Learn the basics of the Farming skill from the Wise Old Man.
keysToEntries.Objective.SpeakToWOM1: Speak to the Wise Old Man, in Bramblemead, to learn about the Farming skill.
keysToEntries.Objective.HarvestCrop: Grow and harvest your first crop from a farming plot.
keysToEntries.Objective.FinishQuest: Return to the Wise Old Man in Bramblemead.
keysToEntries.WoM.QuestionHub.IntroFirstTime: What would you like to know?
keysToEntries.WoM.QuestionHub.IntroReturn: Anything else I can help you with?
keysToEntries.WoM.QuestionHub.PlantingSeeds.1: First, you'll need to build a farming plot. Use a spade on it to clear it of weeds. Once it's completely clear, you're ready to plant.
keysToEntries.WoM.QuestionHub.PlantingSeeds.2: Just grab the seeds in your hand, and then whack em into the soil. No need for grace, or ceremony, just whomp them in and they'll be fine.
keysToEntries.WoM.QuestionHub.Watering.1: Plants are like people, they need water to survive. You'll want to keep your plants nice and hydrated, so that they don't succumb to disease.
keysToEntries.WoM.QuestionHub.Watering.2: Grab yourself a watering can, fill it with water at a water source, and then liberally douse your patch until it's fully soaked through.
keysToEntries.WoM.QuestionHub.Watering.3: Make sure to water your plants every day, ideally first thing in the morning, to keep them nice and healthy.
keysToEntries.WoM.QuestionHub.Compost.1: Compost improves the yield on your crops when you harvest them. They simply love to spread their roots around in the stinky stuff.
keysToEntries.WoM.QuestionHub.Compost.2: To make compost, you'll first need to build a Compost Bin. Then you'll want to fill it with compostible ingredients, like weeds, unwanted logs or irritating exes.
keysToEntries.WoM.QuestionHub.Compost.3: Give it a little time for the newly added stuff to rot down into compost and then take a Compost Bucket and fill it up.
keysToEntries.WoM.QuestionHub.Compost.4: Then just pour the contents of your bucket over the farming plot, making sure it's spread over it as fully as possible.
keysToEntries.WoM.TalkAboutFarming.Intro.1: I see you've started to get a little green around the thumbs! Farming will make survival on Ashenfall a little less horrible.
keysToEntries.WoM.TalkAboutFarming.Intro.2: You'll be able to grow fruits, vegetables, and all manner of plants. Food, potion ingredients and perhaps some crafting resources too!
keysToEntries.WoM.TalkAboutFarming.Intro.3: Why don't you go and give it a proper go right now? Go harvest some crops and then come back and see me. I'll give you a little something for your efforts.
keysToEntries.WoM.TalkAboutFarming.Intro.4: Here, take these to get you started.
keysToEntries.WoM.TalkAboutFarming.NoInventorySpace: Oh? You've overburdened yourself with all the random things you can cram in your pockets. Come back and see me when you've made a bit of room.
keysToEntries.WoM.TalkAboutFarming.Finished.1: Look at you, practically master farmer already. Farming is a wonderful way to keep yourself well fed, and with access to useful materials.
keysToEntries.WoM.TalkAboutFarming.Finished.2: Speaking of useful, here, take this. My little gift to you. Just don't tell the others, they'll get all jealous again.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_FinishedFarmingTutorialPack: A cloth bag with some useful items for farmers.
keysToEntries.FlavourText_FarmingTutorialPack: Contains different types of seeds from brynmoor.

keysToEntries.VaultStatue_Intro1: Your thoughts swirl with images of a statue by the Runecrafting Guild. A word leaps to the front of your mind -- Pthentorok
keysToEntries.Objective_AfterDiscoveringVaultStatue: Locate the statue near a tower and give it the password.
keysToEntries.QuestDescription: Explore the RuneCrafting Guild to discover the means to craft a powerful magical weapon.
keysToEntries.WizardStatue_NoPassword: A statue dedicated to the wizard Ramus Lumen. An unknown dragonkin symbol has been scratched into the plaque.
keysToEntries.VaultHunterJournal:
I've been searching for weeks and yet still I've found nothing. My notes have led me here, to a secret hidden within these ruins, and this statue must be the key.   

The goblins make investigation difficult and I am running out of arrows, but I know there's something here.   

Previous reports suggest there's an ancient vault nearby. A dragonkin structure full of secrets. Perhaps it might go someway to explain this symbol scratched across this statue's plaque. If I can find the entrance and make my way past its defences, perhaps I can find the answers I seek.   

There are so few of us left now, fate owes us this one.
keysToEntries.WizardStatue_Intro1: A statue dedicated to the wizard Ramus Lumen. A dragonkin symbol has been scratched into the plaque. A word summons itself to the tip of your tongue.
keysToEntries.WizardStatue_NotGotPassword_GenericAnswe: Alas, the statue does not respond.
keysToEntries.WizardStatue_GotPassword_WrongAnswer: ...No... nothing happens. Did you expect something to happen? Your ancestors are embarrassed by you.
keysToEntries.WizardStatue_GotPassword_RightAnswer1: A panel in the statue slides aside and you retrieve a crystal orb from within. A voice from nowhere fills your head.
keysToEntries.WizardStatue_GotPassword_RightAnswer2: Ah, hello, this thing on? Apprentice, well done, you've got yourself an orb. This is the first step toward creating your very own magical staff.
keysToEntries.WizardStatue_GotPassword_RightAnswer3: All you need to do is to charge the orb on the pedestals around the guild. You can find them on the towers, but you'll need to work out how to reach them yourself.
keysToEntries.WizardStatue_GotPassword_RightAnswer4: Once you've charged the orb at all three, bring it back here to create your staff.
keysToEntries.WizardStatue_GotPassword_RightAnswer5: That's done, right, how do I turn this thing off? Is it this button he--
keysToEntries.Pedestal_DoesNotHaveOrb: There seems to be space for a sphere of some kind.
keysToEntries.Pedestal_HasOrb_NotUsedYet: You place the orb on the pedestal. Ambient energy is absorbed by the orb, causing it to glow faintly. The pedestal loses its charge.
keysToEntries.Pedestal_HasOrb_AlreadyUsed: The pedestal has no charge left.
keysToEntries.Pedestal_Orb_FullyCharged: The orb hums with power. Time to return to the statue.
keysToEntries.WizardStatue_GotOrb1: Alright, it's recording. Hello apprentice, well done on charging up that orb.
keysToEntries.WizardStatue_GotOrb2: This orb is the heart of a very special staff known as the Staff of Light. Popular with blue robed wizards throughout Saradomin's domain.
keysToEntries.WizardStatue_GotOrb3: Now we just attach the orb to the shaft and voila, one staff of light. Great job, apprentice!
keysToEntries.WizardStatue_GotOrb4: Now Jules, turn this thing off so I can hit you with this broom without it being preserved for future generations.
keysToEntries.WizardStatue_NoInventorySpace: Don't ask me how I know this, I'm just a recording, but your bag seems a bit overburdened. Come back here once you've cleared up some space.
keysToEntries.Objective1: Investigate the area for clues about the statue.
keysToEntries.Objective2: Search the Runecrafting Guild for a statue.
keysToEntries.Objective3: Find a way to charge the  mysterious orb.
keysToEntries.OrbDesc: An orb obtained from a statue in the Magic Institute.
keysToEntries.OrbDescCharged: An orb obtained from a statue in the Magic Institute, charged with magical energy.

keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Intro_Default1: Careful, this whole area is full of goblins and not the nice kind like me. What brings you all the way out here?
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Intro_Default2: Do you feel it too? Prophecy. A god's backup plan, waiting for a host to sink its tendrils into.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Intro_NoProgress1: I feel it up there by the northeast ruin, down there, you can see it from this lookout. Another presence south toward the guild, beyond a broken bridge.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Intro_NoProgress2: Something dangerous is hidden out here. It's sealed for now, probably in an amulet or something but we need to find it. By we, I mean you.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Intro_NoProgress3: I'm vulnerable to prophecy, I was Bandos' puppet for a while and I can't risk that again. Never again.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Intro_NonSwampAmul: I feel it up by the ruin over there, beneath this outlook,.. and now on you? What have you found?
I feel it down south toward the guild... and now on you? What have you found?
Hrmm, It's a prophecy alright, one of Bandos' safeguards. I've felt it before... when he made me his puppet.
It's only half of the magic though. We need to find both pieces.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Intro_FoundOneAmul: Wait... I sense something on you. What's that you've got? Let me see.
Hrmm, It's feels like a prophecy, one of Bandos' safeguards. I've felt it before... when he made me his puppet.
The other's one's missing? Very disconcerting, you should track it down. I would, but I'm vulnerable to prophecy, this is all on you.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Intro_BothAmuletsF: You've got the stink of divine manipulation on you, what have you discovered. May I look?
Wow, yup, definitely a prophecy. Contained in two amulet pieces, some real nasty work.
A prophecy is like a parasite, burrowing into your soul. Twisting your choices to get its own way.
Here, I'll take those from you. I know how to dispose of these.
Take this, a little something for your trouble. A reminder that we're all masters of our own fate.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Intro_FoundSwampAm: Wait... I sense something on you. What's that you've got? Let me see.
Hrmm, It's feels like a prophecy, one of Bandos' safeguards. I've felt it before... when he made me his puppet.
That's only half of it though, you should track down the other piece. I would, but I'm vulnerable to prophecy, this is all on you.
I sensed something up there by the ruin over there, beneath this outlook, and south toward the guild.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Puppet_1: I hate the romantic notion of destiny. Mine was to be the puppet of the goblin god of war, Bandos.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Puppet_2: I died, got resurrected and my soul was marked by his claim. I had no choice in the matter.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Puppet_3: I nearly became something awful, but a friend helped me resist Bandos and put a stop to his scheme.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Puppet_4: But... Bandos' mark still dug deep into my being and when he died... well, so did I.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Puppet_5: I mean, I got better, but still, these things haunt you.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice_AskAboutPuppet: What do you mean you were a puppet?
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice_OtherGoblins: Why aren't you like the other goblins?
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Goblins_1: Truth is, I'm a cave goblin -- a different tribe. We escaped Bandos' efforts in the god wars and forged a life of our own.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Goblins_2: The war god hated anything that wasn't combat. So where the surface goblins stagnated, we cave goblins thrived.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Goblins_3: We chose peace. Peace and understanding, and now cave goblin society is wonderfully advanced.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Goblins_4: I look at goblins and see what we were and sometimes I'd like them to see who they *could* be.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Bandos_1: Bandos was a war god. The Big High War God, in the parlance of my people. He delighted in conflict, battle and bloodshed.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Bandos_2: He came to our world, Gielinor, and ignited war. Laughing all the while at the carnage.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Bandos_3: He was banished, like all the gods, with Guthix's edicts. Then returned after Guthix died to immediately pick a fight.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Bandos_4: He challenged the god of justice, Armadyl, and the two raced to build a god-killing weapon. Armadyl finished his first.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_Bandos_5: Armadyl slew Bandos, leaving the universe in a better state. I do not regret his passing. Gielinor is better for it.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_NoProgress_1: How's the investigation going? Found anything yet?
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_NoProgress_2: No? You'll want to investigate over by the ruin over there, beneath this outlook, and south toward the guild.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_OneAmuletFoundNotI: How's the investigation going? Found anything yet?
Oh, wait, that amulet, that's the source of the uncomfortable feeling I felt.
It's a prophecy alright, one of Bandos' safeguards. Although, since he's dead, who else might use it?
It's only half of the magic though. We need to find both pieces.
I felt the other presence further south around the guild.
I felt the other presence near the ruin to the northeast.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_OneAmuletFoundInve: How's the investigation going? Found anything yet?
The amulet was missing? The reliquary filled with fetid water and mud?
Why would someone take it? Don't they need both pieces for the prophecy... unless...
I've been tracking a strange presence in the swamps, not far from the northeast ruins. You need to find the other piece, and quickly. There might be darker powers at work.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_DarkerPowers_1: There are always darker powers. There are things worse than gods... older than the gods.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_DarkerPowers_2: Let's just stop this nonsense before we meet them.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_BothAmuletsFound_1: You did it! I knew you could!
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_BothAmuletsFound_2: Whatever that druid was trying to do, I'm glad you stopped it. It would definitely not have ended well.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_BothAmuletsFound_3: A prophecy is like a parasite, burrowing into your soul. Twisting your choices to get its own way.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_BothAmuletsFound_4: We should all forge our own paths, don't you think?
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_BothAmuletsFound_5: Here, I'll take those from you. I know how to dispose of prophecies.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_BothAmuletsFound_6: Take this, a little something for your trouble. A reminder that we're all masters of our own fate.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_QuestComplete_1: It's actually quite fascinating being here, without a prophecy to worry about.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_QuestComplete_2: The goblins round here are so different from those back home, they don't even worship Bandos.
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_QuestComplete_3: They follow the dragons instead, which is really interesting. I wonder if it changes how their society lives?
keysToEntries.Zanik_GoblinDiplomacy_QuestComplete_4: Do you think they'd object to me taking notes of their rituals? They probably would, right? Dagnammit.
keysToEntries.Goblin_GoblinDiplomacy_Intro_NoProgress_: What you want? Can't you see I'm busy plotting me escape?
Well if you're gonna interrupt me, why not grab me some food, eh? Cos, like, I'm proper starvin right now.
Go, on, do me a solid and bring me a nice steak? I'll tell ya where they hides their treasure if you do.
Wait, I smell steak on ya? If you hand it over I'll tell you where they hide their secret stuff.
keysToEntries.Goblin_GoblinDiplomacy_GivenSteak_1: Ah, you're alright human. Not as ugly on the inside as ya is on the outside.
keysToEntries.Goblin_GoblinDiplomacy_GivenSteak_Ruin: If you want to get yer hand on this lot's stuff, you'll want to look behind them barrels and crates. Not good at hiding things this lot.
keysToEntries.Goblin_GoblinDiplomacy_GivenSteak_Guild: If you want to get yer hand on this lot's stuff, you'll want to look behind them vines near the old fireplace.
keysToEntries.Goblin_GoblinDiplomacy_RefuseSteak: Well, fine! No steak, no secrets! See if I care.
keysToEntries.Goblin_GoblinDiplomacy_FoundOneAmulet_Th: Yeah, nick their stuff! That'll teach 'em.
keysToEntries.Goblin_GoblinDiplomacy_FoundOneAmulet_No: You lookin to steal stuff? Well's your a bit too late.
How's I know? I smells it on ya mate, you stinky of theivery. Probably nicked something shiny from the other tribe.
If you'd got here sooner, I'd help you find this lot's stuff. But that weird garou-beastmen nutjob got here first. Weird fella, lives in a cave in the swamp.
You can take a look behind all them barrels and crates . But won't be nothing there.
You can take a look behind them vines near the old fireplace . But won't be nothing there.
keysToEntries.Title: Goblin Diplomacy
keysToEntries.Description: Zanik needs you to stop a prophecy before it's too late.
keysToEntries.Objective1: Investigate the ruins to the northeast and the guild to the south of Zanik.
keysToEntries.Objective2a: Investigate the ruin northeast of Zanik.
keysToEntries.Objective2b: Investigate the guild south of Zanik.
keysToEntries.Objective3: Search for the other amulet piece in an area filled with mud and stagnant swampwater.
keysToEntries.Reliquery_FirstOneFound: You find an amulet marked with the symbol of Bandos, the goblin war god.
keysToEntries.Reliquery_FirstOneFound_SecondPass: There is nothing there but dust.
keysToEntries.Reliquery_SecondOneFound: The reliquary has already been opened and the contents removed. There are claw marks along the outside and traces of stagnant water and mud within. Perhaps someone nearby saw something.
keysToEntries.Objective5: Look for the other amulet at either the ruins northeast of Zanik, or the guild to the south.
keysToEntries.Objective6: Find someone that can identify the Bandosian amulet.
keysToEntries.Reliquery_NotEnoughSpace: You discover a strange amulet in the reliquary, but your inventory is too full to pick it up right now.

keysToEntries.MesaMemorial5: I will start at our tree, in the east. Do you see it, down there, framed perfectly in this spot? A single point of brilliant blue, in an ocean of fiery red. An outsider, just like I was, until I met you.
keysToEntries.MesaMemorial4: I wrote a song about you, about the time we spent together. It’s a technique of my people, turning music into craft. I will play the pieces of it, as I take this goodbye walk.
keysToEntries.MesaMemorial3: It’s time for me to leave this place. To close this chapter on our memories and to start a life without you. I need to find myself again, without your shadow smiling out of the corner of my eye.
keysToEntries.MesaMemorial2: This view feels so different without you by my side. The angle is strange, because my shoulder isn’t weighed by your head as you lean on me and sigh softly. A hundred years without you, and yet this view still feels wrong.
keysToEntries.MesaMemorial1: You find a crystal in the shape of a seed. The memorial stone changes as you hold the crystal up, revealing text in elvish.
keysToEntries.QuestDescription: Follow an elven bard's trail of memory, and discover a harmonious weapon.
keysToEntries.FirstMemorial1: I never liked your story. Child of a farmer, given over to the Wild Knights, because of the fire in your eyes. It felt as if you were owed a greater story, one that could deliver even a fraction of your courage.
keysToEntries.FirstMemorial2: So I wrote this song to conjure up the essence of you, rather than the fact. A bard’s prerogative, evoke the feelings you left in others, rather than record your daily toils.
keysToEntries.FirstMemorial3:
Er mwyn tynged Gilinor. Oh Gielinor  Mae’n daith un ffordd i fewn i’r affwys, i Gilinor   

That’s why you left me. Not the ravage of time. Not the cruelty of fire-red hair turning the silver-grey of burnt-out ash. You had a mission, a journey, to take the fight into the abyss.
keysToEntries.FirstMemorial4:
Trefn elitaidd rhyfelwyr affwysol   

Leaving me behind, so that you could save us all.
keysToEntries.FirstMemorial5: My journey continues eastward, following the blue in a sea made from waves of colourful dancing leaves. Saying my farewell, each footstep at a time.
keysToEntries.SecondMemorial1:
Gadael pob peth ar ôl    

Leaving everything behind, I thought only I did that.
keysToEntries.SecondMemorial2: Wanderlust overcame reason and I journeyed across the ocean to find myself. Ashenfall is no place for an elf. There is no crystal, save what I brought with me, and the humans here distrust all outsiders. Not without reason.
keysToEntries.SecondMemorial3: Not you though. I remember sitting alone in the forests, wolves circling me, and then a loud crash, boisterous laughter and a mad-woman in leaf-covered armour falls out of a tree and says hello.
keysToEntries.SecondMemorial4:
Marhog Gwagle! I Gielinor! 
Marchog Gwagle! Marchog! I Gielinor!   

A brave warrior, defender of the world. Defeated by a tree.
keysToEntries.SecondMemorial5: We shared a meal. I told stories and you listened in a way that no one has ever listened before. All of you, every part of your being, focused on my words. I think I loved you even then, though it would be years before I realised it.
keysToEntries.ThirdMemorial1: We found a home here, together, the Wild Knight and her strange elf bard. We took the logs from the forests, and the stone from the mountain’s floor, so that we might craft a little place where we could escape together.
keysToEntries.ThirdMemorial2: I’m still not sure why we chose this land. Brynmoor was much calmer.
keysToEntries.ThirdMemorial3:
Y byd dirdro. Tir llygredig   

Ghornfell is a harsh place, filled with dangers. The land itself is twisted, poison to the east and storms to the west. Overhead the dread Velgar circles, laughing at the destruction he’s wrought.
keysToEntries.ThirdMemorial4:
Creaduriaid. Adfeilion.   

Monsters and desolation, everywhere we looked. Yet somehow we made a home, a ramshackle house of nonsense, that became my paradise.
keysToEntries.ThirdMemorial5:
Y baradwys

Our world of adventure and excitement, held between four small walls.
keysToEntries.FourthMemorial1: When the war turned against your people, you took my hand and led me away. As ancient monsters left the secrecy of their shadows to inflict horror on us all.
keysToEntries.FourthMemorial2:
Y dragonkin    

I had thought them a myth, relegated to the most obscure bardic tales and songs. A shadow-on-the-wall horror story to scare small children into bed.
keysToEntries.FourthMemorial3: “Be good little child, or the dragonkin will get you”. But, no, here they are, with their great dragon army, wiping out everyone we have ever known.
keysToEntries.FourthMemorial4: Then we were alone, just you and me. I was used to the solitude, it’s instilled into my people, but it broke my heart when I saw you were used to it too.
keysToEntries.FifthMemorial1: To an elf time is a fickle thing, we age so much slower that time passes almost without meaning. A year to us is a heartbeat, a decade is nothing but a brief rest, a century can be as easily forgotten as any other time. But not for you.
keysToEntries.FifthMemorial2: Every time you stepped out the door, it was as if time took some of you from me. In barely a handful of years your limbs stiffened, you fire-red hair turned grey and you lost some of the agility you had been so proud of.
keysToEntries.FifthMemorial3:
Y pyrth hynaf,  
ar goll   

Those elder gates, stealing more of you from me.
keysToEntries.FifthMemorial4: Yet, even as time left its wounds in your skin, you were still the most beautiful creature in all of Gielinor.
keysToEntries.SixthMemorial1: You didn’t die, you just went off to battle. That’s how I could brave the years. In the songs I wrote about you, you were off to war, fighting for Guthix against all the evils in creation.
keysToEntries.SixthMemorial2:
Clwych curo’r drymiau rhyfel,  A ffanffer Guthix yn canu   

I imagined your war-song as you lead your Wild Knights into battle.
keysToEntries.SixthMemorial3:
Cleddyf guthix ydym ni  
Ni yw'r golau treiddgar  
Yr ydm yma
I drechu pob cysgod. I Gielinor!
keysToEntries.SixthMemorial4: You were Guthix’s sword, his piercing light against the darkness. That’s why you left me. Not because of time. Not because you were never coming back. But because you needed to save us all from the monsters in the night.
keysToEntries.SixthMemorial5: A shield against a shadowy emptiness. My void knight.
keysToEntries.FinalMemorial1: I laid your bones to rest beneath this tree, far from our home. I felt you deserved one last adventure, stretched out across our little world.
keysToEntries.FinalMemorial2: This song was written for you... In memory of you. Yet the lump in my throat is so great, I cannot sound the words.
keysToEntries.FinalMemorial3: I'm sorry, Meya, even now I cannot say goodbye. So let me just say the words that mean the most.
keysToEntries.FinalMemorial4: Dw i'n dy garu di'n fawr.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice_SingSong: Sing the bard's song of rememberance.
keysToEntries.FinalMemorial5: As the last words leave your lips, the seed shivers and shudders to the song. It twists, stretches and transforms into a bow of glittering crystal.
keysToEntries.NotEnoughInventory: You don't have enough space for the crystal seed to grow into the bow. You will need to make some room before it can do so.

keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_GhostClue_Windmill: Blades that once whirled like a tornado, now lay still among crumbled stone. Find the lost who dwells there, set her free.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_GhostClue_Cave: My brother lies buried in darkness, beneath a southern point rising high in the sky. Find his name, set him free.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_GhostClue_Swamp: Choking on swamp gas. Ruined timber, mouldy and splintered. Sinking moment by moment into bitter, bleak, mud. Find the name of the buried, set them free.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_GhostClue_Farm: Once it was homes, standing together. Now, nothing but ruins, falling apart. The last surviving memories trampled beneath angry hooves. Find the name of the forgotten, set him free.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_GhostClue_Memorial: Names to be remembered. Prayers for the dead. Whisper our memories at the place by the temple, so that we might finally rest.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_FarmGhost_1: I never liked goblins, even though Zanik said they deserved our pity, more than hate.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_FarmGhost_2: She was wrong, of course. They killed me. So I should hate them.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_FarmGhost_3: I came to rebuild a farmstead. Now I'll serve as nothing but compost. Gran would be so upset with me.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_FarmGhost_4: Remember me, adventurer. Help my spirit rest. Warren was my name, don't let it be forgotten.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_WindmillGhost_1: Well this didn't work out for me, did it? I made this great stupid journey, leaving Al Kharid behind, because adventure awaited me. Glory and fame.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_WindmillGhost_2: Then a great bloody dragon messed everything up.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_WindmillGhost_3: Dead within moments. Barely a day in this land. I never did have the best luck.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_WindmillGhost_4: Remember me, adventurer. I was Lazina, blade for hire and swordmaster supreme. Say a prayer for me, so that the gods will look on me fondly.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_CaveGhost_1: I thought I might be safe here, hidden away from the skies. As the dragon circled over me, I thought "maybe I'll be OK".
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_CaveGhost_3: He knew of this place, that dragon. Poked his great scaley head through the entrance and grinned at me as he slowly drew in a breath. I watched my life unfold before me, until the poison put it all to an end. I wish I'd found that shield.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_CaveGhost_4: Remember me, adventurer. Say a farewell to Finn, won't you? Tell ma I'm sorry, and tell my stepda he's an arse.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_Warren_Remembered: <The wind smells briefly of grass and soil. A contented feeling fills your heart>
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_Lazina_Remembered: <You hear the sound of laughter and the clash of blade against blade. You cannot help but smile>
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_Finn_Remembered: <You hear the sound of children playing and a woman laughing with them>
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_Kay_Remembered: <You smell sausages, bacon and the sizzling of eggs on the pan. Your stomach rumbles in response>
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_Everyone_Remembered: <The lost, the dead and the once forgotten, are finally laid to rest. A gentle peace washes over you and you are left with a gift from the dead.>
keysToEntries.QuestDescription: Lay to rest the spirits of the lost expedition members.
keysToEntries.WellFoundAmulet: The well contains cold, stagnant water.
keysToEntries.WellNotFoundAmulet: You see something glinting in the bucket. Reaching into the cold water, you pull out a strange looking amulet that's even colder to the touch.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_SwampGhost_1: I'm impressed it got this far, really, this boat. Because I never sailed it here.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_SwampGhost_2: I tried to row out by the lake near the temple, when that dragon whacked me with his tail. What had I ever done to him?
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_SwampGhost_3: Hurtling through the air, knowing everything was over. All I could think about was how I'd missed breakfast. Most important meal of the day, and I forgot it on my last one.
keysToEntries.MessageInABottle_SwampGhost_4: Names Kay, adventurer. Remember me, won't you? Give me a proper goodbye.
keysToEntries.Altar_Warren_Remembered: The wind smells briefly of grass and soil. A contented feeling fills your heart.
keysToEntries.Altar_Lazina_Remembered: You hear the sound of laughter and the clash of blade against blade. You cannot help but smile.
keysToEntries.Altar_Finn_Remembered: You hear the sound of children playing and a woman laughing with them.
keysToEntries.Altar_Kay_Remembered: You smell sausages, bacon and the sizzling of eggs on the pan. Your stomach rumbles in response.
keysToEntries.Altar_All_Remembered: The lost, the dead and the once forgotten, are finally laid to rest. A gentle peace washes over you and you are left with a gift from the dead.
keysToEntries.NotEnoughInventory: You do not have the space to receive your reward. You will need to make room in your backpack.
keysToEntries.MoreGhostsLeft: The dead do not yet feel fully at peace. There may be more out that need help moving on.
keysToEntries.Objective_FindUseForAmulet: Search for a purpose for the Amulet of Ghostspeak.
keysToEntries.Objective_LayGhostsToRest: Find the restless spirits and help lay them to rest. They only appear at night.
keysToEntries.AltarDescription: This shrine is a place where the dead are remembered. So that they may move on to the afterlife they deserve.
keysToEntries.Amulet_ExtraHint: [You'll need to find some way to understand what they're saying.]

keysToEntries.Endurance.Desc: A measure of how well rested you are. As you get more tired your stamina, carrying capacity and even your spellcasting will suffer.
keysToEntries.Endurance.Title: Rest
keysToEntries.Sustenance.Desc: Sustenance affects health regeneration. The higher it is, the better your health regenerates.
keysToEntries.Sustenance.Title: Sustenance
keysToEntries.Hydration.Desc: Hydration affects stamina regeneration. The higher it is, the better your stamina recovers.
keysToEntries.Hydration.Title: Hydration

keysToEntries.RepairModeDescription: Repair damaged structures and add materials to incomplete structures.
keysToEntries.GhostModeDescription: Place blueprints of structures that you can complete later by adding materials. Blueprints do not cost any materials.

keysToEntries.SpamWarning: You are spamming the chat. Please wait a moment.
keysToEntries.EnemiesScalingDown: Ashenfall's bloodlust diminishes as your allies depart.
keysToEntries.EnemiesScalingUp: As your numbers grow, so grows Ashenfall's bloodlust. Enemies grow more powerful.

keysToEntries.CraftingTable.Description: A simple crafting station equipped with an array of primitive tools. It is used to craft equipment and other important resources.
keysToEntries.PotteryWheel.Description: Used for fashioning clay items. Bowls, pots, and jewellery moulds can be crafted at this station and then fired in a furnace.
keysToEntries.RuneAltar.Description: Used for crafting Runes, the magical stones needed to cast magic.
keysToEntries.BrewingCauldron.Description: This cauldron is used to brew potions.
keysToEntries.Kiln.Description: A heated kiln, used to fire pottery.
keysToEntries.Grindstone.Description: This tool can crush stone, bones, and other things, into fine dust.
keysToEntries.Loom.Description: The device processes threads into textiles and cloth.
keysToEntries.Furnace.Description: By burning charcoal to create a blazing hot flame, the furnace is able to fire clay, and smelt ores into metal bars.
keysToEntries.SpinningWheel.Description: A spinning wheel can spin fibres such as flax or fleece into threads for crafting with.
keysToEntries.Tannery.Description: A rack that slowly processes animal hide into leather.
keysToEntries.LoomHarness.Description: A loom improvement. The loom harness grants an additional slot to the loom, allowing the loom to weave more complex textiles.
keysToEntries.TannersKit.Description: A tannery improvement. The tanner's kit adds another build slot to the tanning station, allowing you to create more complex materials.
keysToEntries.CookingRange.Description: A workstation dedicated to cooking food. A cooking range can be used to cook hearty meals as well as individual raw foods.
keysToEntries.CookingPot.Description: A sturdy pot over a campfire that can be used to combine ingredients to make better meals and infuse water with berries or other ingredients.
keysToEntries.Campfire.Description: A nice hot fire on which to cook food or purify dirty water.
keysToEntries.Grill.Description: A much improved home cooking fire with no need for fuel
keysToEntries.AdvancedFurnace.Description: The blast furnace uses intense heat to smelt high tier metal ores into ingots.
keysToEntries.SmithAnvil.Description: A Blacksmith's Anvil. Required for working with metals.
keysToEntries.JewelersBench.Description: Used to craft magical items from precious metals and gems.
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.Title: Runecrafting
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.Description.AttunementPhase: Find an attunement frequency to infuse the runes with magical energy. Higher attunement levels grant additional bonus runes.
keysToEntries.Runecrafting.Description.InfusionPhase: As the attuned rune fills with power, mend it's cracks to contain the magic within and safeguard you bonus runes.

keysToEntries.AbyssalWhipDesc: A brutal weapon crafted from the spine of a terrifying Abyssal Demon.
keysToEntries.GraniteMaulDesc: The power of this warhammer should not be taken for granite.
keysToEntries.CrystalBowDesc: A powerful Shortbow fashioned from crystal of Elven origin.

keysToEntries.EnterDedicatedServerIp.Body: Use this option if you fail to connect to a locally hosted dedicated server. If the dedicated server is running on the same computer as the game, please enter 127.0.0.1. Otherwise, find the IP address of your dedicated server, such as 192.168.0.1. If you altered the Dedicated Server port, your IP address will need to be followed :12345, where 12345 is the selected port. For instance, 192.168.0.10:12345.

keysToEntries.TelemetryBody: With your consent, we would like to automatically collect information from this device via a new player activity plugin. This information allows our team to review how players use systems and play the game to better address issues, expand on old systems, or add new features. With both activity and player feedback we are able to make better decisions about the future of the game. If you choose, we can also use this information to offer a better online advertising experience.

keysToEntries.NarrativeIntroTextPart1: Dragons, always trouble, and Ashenfall has more than its share.
keysToEntries.NarrativeIntroTextPart2: When the continent was suddenly discovered, explorers poured in to claim its secrets.
keysToEntries.NarrativeIntroTextPart3: The dragons didn't like that, not one little bit.
keysToEntries.NarrativeIntroTextPart4: So we called the best - yes, that's you - to hunt them down and end their threat.
keysToEntries.NarrativeIntroTextPart5: Get ready, hero! We're all counting on you.

keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Dragonwolves.1.Title: Laboratory Note
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Dragonwolves.1.Text:
I wonder, sometimes, whether we are too limited in our focus. Is the cold-blooded nature of the reptile, more useful than the mammalian warm-blood approach? I watch the creatures of this region, the kebbits, the cows, the sheep and I see how they multiply and spread at a pace that far exceeds our own.

By contrast, the dragons I birth are a slow and laborious effort. Each one must be carefully managed and maintained with focused precision. Even those capable of spawning their own young, do so slowly. Often brooding no more than a single viable progeny in a century of practice.

Even my prize specimen produced only a single heir, a red-scaled whelp, no less, unlike his mother’s multicoloured hues. Perhaps the fault lies in the father. I should dissect him and see where the problem begins.

But I lose my train of thought. Mammals. Noisy, smelly, but they are everywhere, breeding like rats – which are also mammals, now I think about it. Perhaps I should turn my sights on them instead.

There are a pack of dire wolves that wander near my laboratory, it would be simple enough to procure a few of their number. Some adults, a few children, I’ll need a large and diverse sample size for my experiments.

Perhaps there’s a place where reptile and mammal can meet, to create a superior creature. I shall investigate further.
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Dragonwolves.2.Title: Experiment Log
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Dragonwolves.2.Text:
Dragon-Hybridisation Experiment Day 7

It is not easy splicing reptile and mammal together, their very nature fights against their biology and they would rather perish upon emergence than live with such a hybrid nature. It’s a shame really, the little pups are quite cute. I think Viika would like one as a pet, it might keep my sibling grounded and anchored a little more to our frame of reference. Wishful thinking perhaps.

The wolves have proved remarkably durable, considering. While they all die seconds after birth, they do hold on longer than expected. I attempted to combine kebbit and dragon and… well… that didn’t go well. Chinchompa and dragon, that was a mistake that will take some time to repair – I liked that lab.  Yet wolf, there’s something here, I can feel it. They’re resilient, dangerous and there’s something in the pack mentality that fascinates me.

Dragon-Hybridisation Experiment Day 56

I have decided that a rapid hybridisation approach will not work. Mammals, while fast breeders, are weak and lack the resilience needed to survive the experiment if I go too quickly. Instead I shall engineer a slow breeding process, spreading the development out over the generations.

Tedious, yes, but at least it will allow me to focus on other project while this progresses.

Dragon-Hybridisation Experiment Day 219

The changes are gradual, yet viable. Scales here and there, some improved musculature and reflexes, yet nothing approaching the power of a dragon.

Is this all they can be?

Dragon-Hybridisation Experiment Day 2307

I’d forgotten about this failure of an experiment. The dragon-wolves have proved inadequate for any useful purpose. I was going to incinerate the remaining members, but Viika in a fit of strangeness released them all.

So, now we have it. Dragon-wolves running all over the region, lovely. Honestly my sibling can be so vexing sometimes.
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Dragonwolves.3.Title: The Tale of the Ghost Wolves
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Dragonwolves.3.Text:
There once was a girl with a pet she adored.
He had fur like silver, eyes like ice.
The gentlest of natures.
He was ever so nice.
But there were things in the forest,
Full of shadow and hate.
When the girl found her friend,
It was sadly too late.
Eyes like glass.
Skin cold as stone.
The girl wept for days.
She’d never felt more alone.
Smoke rose through the woods,
Clawing and thick.
When the girl breathed it in,
It made her feel sick.
Yet in the choking and coughing,
There whispered a voice
Beautiful and terrible,
And it gave her a choice.
She could live as she was,
Alone in the night-black,
Or make a pact with the smoke,
And get her friend back.
She didn’t wait for a second.
She agreed with glee.
So the smoke told her to wait.
Where her best friend could see.
Eyes like embers.
Fur like fog.
Her last words uttered.
“You’re a very bad dog.”
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Necromancer.1.Title: Ravanna's First Journal
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Necromancer.1.Text:
The odd thing about life is it’s surprisingly easy to maintain. For all the work of alchemists and scheming sorcerers, continued existence is child’s play really. All it takes is hate.

Hate is a fuel that can burn forever. Pure rage is a force that can drive anyone forward. Grief is a power source that can last until the end of time. You just need to know how to use them.

There’s a morality in vengeance that society shuns. They say it won’t solve anything. That it never makes things better. Yet, I picture the suffering of those monsters and that’s all I need. Knowing they suffer, die and weep for their loved ones, that’s enough to drive my bones forward and keep the skin stitched in place.

Unreasonable, yes, but at least hate is honest in it’s hypocrisy. I choose to turn the wheel of the vicious cycle round and round, grinding garou bones beneath it.

I will never forgive them for the death of my friend. Never. And as long as I curse them, my soul will not fade to the world below.

Someone will tell me that that’s not what Ulv would want, but I knew my friend and, if he could speak, he’d tell me to make sure they hurt.

So, I do.

Those same people tell me that grief fades, but that’s a lie. It’s been so long now, so many years, yet that pain and that hate still burn inside me more ferociously than the sun, and I wake each day smiling because I know they suffer in return.

Perhaps I need a hobby?
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Necromancer.2.Title: Ravanna's Second Journal
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Necromancer.2.Text:
So this is what the people who left Bramblemead are up to. Honestly, it seems like a pretty nice life. There’s amalgamated and, for lack of a better word, normal people working side by side. Shops are well-stocked, and fields are filled with delicious looking vegetables. The entire landscape is a dazzling display of vibrant colours and … oh, yeah, they have dragons.

Not dragons like we had – the ones that burn villages to the ground and stomp on people for fun. No, these dragons watch over the villagers, help them with construction, farming, all sorts of stuff. They even give them this weird little thing they call a Blessing, which somehow binds them together in a way I’m seriously going to need to study later.

The people don’t worship the dragons either. There’s nothing like the goblin shenanigans down south. No, the traditional religions line up in a multifaith community with one another. Saradominists greet Zamorakians and no one starts a fight. I assume, anyway, no one really wears little badges or anything, but there are shrines to more than just Saradomin here, so I like to daydream.

I set up a little shop. Can you imagine? Me, with a shop! Just selling little potions and salves, and services for those that need to speak with the dead. Simple stuff, nothing too taxing, but enough to put a roof over my head.

I think I could be happy here. Well, as happy as I get.
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Necromancer.3.Title: Ravanna's Third Journal
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Necromancer.3.Text:
I have a new customer, and she won’t fit through the door.

Odd to empathise with a dragon, but I do. We all saw what happened. I watched as the great multi-headed dragon queen pulled Xikotal’s heart from his chest and ate it in front of his mate.

You don’t just get over something like that. It’s visceral and cuts you deeper than you ever thought possible. It’s not just grief. It’s not just rage. The two forces combine into something new, something stronger than the sum of its components. That sort of feeling is powerful – strong enough to change you into something other than what you were.

I have lived for longer than I can say on that feeling alone. It’s a fuel that rages within you, burning you from the inside to keep you warm. I can only imagine what it’s doing to a creature like her.

She asks me to bring her beloved back, but that’s beyond my power, always has been. I’ve never been great at animating the dead, I can do a few bones, sure, but nothing stronger.

Curses, though, that I’m good with those.

Imaru’s rage makes her the perfect focus. The beating heart around the darkest magics. She can be a weapon against the Godeater, if I hone her right. The rage of a dragon, combined with the power of a grief-curse, such a thing could be the blade needed to slit Kuldra’s throat.

I will forge her carefully.
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Necromancer.4.Title: Ravanna's Fourth Journal
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Necromancer.4.Text:
I am guilty of mistakes, I admit this. I let things get to me and I don’t think them through as well as I should. When I lost Ulv I tried to bring him back and… well… the result of that is better buried, I confess.

I visit him from time to time. There’s nothing left of my friend there, hasn’t been for some time, but I don’t want him getting lonely.

She was my next mistake. Imaru. After the death of her mate she came to me seeking a way to bring him back. Necromancy doesn’t work like that, at least not yet and certainly not within my skill set. Yet, still, I thought I could offer her something almost as good.

I offered her revenge.

I created a curse within her, using her grief and rage as a focus, so that it might strengthen her against Kuldra. She was to become a devouring force that would feast on the Godeater’s very soul.

It did not work.

Kuldra is stronger than I imagined. Her essence more powerful than either of us could have guessed. As Imaru raged at her Kuldra simply laughed and rebuffed her, barely even registering the unholy force hurled against her. She is a monster, and I use that word very rarely.

So Imaru died. Fell to the ground in a crumbled heap… and I wish that were the end of it.
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Necromancer.5.Title: Ravanna's Fifth Journal
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Necromancer.5.Text:
Things are so much worse than I thought. The River Noumenon, the way to the Afterlife, is blocked. Imaru has somehow wedged herself in there, using the curse I gave her and her own, sheer, bloody-minded rage to stick her in place.

She claws at the edge of the Underworld, creating rifts that bleed the Noumenon’s waters into our world like some deadly poison that rips out the soul. She uses her “Blessing” as an anchor to her people, dragging their souls to her so that she might feast and grow stronger.

So focused on strength and power, on beating Kuldra, that she doesn’t think about what happens to everyone else. My soul is protected, warded by the curse that rages inside me, but the other villagers aren’t so lucky.

I see tendrils of their spirits being dragged from their chests like a ragged cloak billowing in the wind. They don’t see it, of course, but they can feel it. They know something is going on and it’s leading to blind panic. The once peaceful chatter of Fellhollow is now turning to stifled screams.

This is all my fault. I need to try and find a way to fix it.
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Necromancer.6.Title: Ravanna's Sixth Journal
keysToEntries.Fellhollow.Necromancer.6.Text:
