keysToEntries.Wither.GameplayEffect.Name: Soul Rift
keysToEntries.SoulRift.FTUE.FirstInstance: You have become soul-rifted, and gained the attention of Imaru.
keysToEntries.SoulRift.FTUE.PreventTeleport: Imaru's gaze prevents you from teleporting.
keysToEntries.SoulRift.FTUE.MissedPool: Use your compass to find the closest Purification Pool.
keysToEntries.SoulRift.FTUE.ImarusGazeStage1: Imaru smells your soul, find a Purification Pool and remove Soul Rift before she finds you.
keysToEntries.SoulRift.FTUE.ImarusGazeStage2: Imaru draws closer, find a Purification Pool to remove Soul Rift before its too late.
keysToEntries.SoulRift.FTUE.ImarusGazeStage3: Intertact with a Purification Pool to remove the Soul Rift state.

keysToEntries.Soulscourge.GE_Toxicity_Damage: You are afflicted with Soulscourge. Losing Health.
keysToEntries.Soulscourge.Descrioption: When this bar is filled your soul will be shredded, causing you continual harm!
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Exposed.Title: Exposure
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Exposed.Description: Reduces maximum stamin and stamina regeneration rate.
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Burning.Title: Burning
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Burning.Description: You are on fire! Inflicts fire damage every second.
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Poison.Title: Poison
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Poison.Description: When the poison bar is filled you will be afflicted with poison. You will take poison damage every second.
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Shock.Title: Shock
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Shock.Description: Your muscles spasm. Your stamina weakens as a result.
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Bleed.Title: Bleeding
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Bleed.Description: You are bleeding. If the bleed building reaches maximum you will lose a significant amount of health.
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Encumbered.Title: Encumbered
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Encumbered.Descript: You are overburdened. Reduces speed and stamina regen. You are unable to run or jump.
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Fatigue.Title: Fatigue
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Fatigue.Description: You are tired. Reduces hitpoint maximum, carrying capacity and experience gain.
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Slow.Title: Slow
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Slow.Description: You cannot sprint and your speed is greatly reduced.
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Cold.Title: Cold
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Cold.Description: Reduces maximum stamina, stamina recovery and movement speed.
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Cosiness.Title: Cosiness
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Cosiness.Descriptio: Relax a little! Hydration, sustenance and rest do not degrade.
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Prayer.Title: Prayer
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Prayer.Description: Blessed by the gods at a prayer altar.
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Sheltered.Title: Sheltered
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.WellRested.Title: Well Rested
keysToEntries.StatusEffect.Tooltip.Wither.Title: Wither

keysToEntries.Objective_1: Speak to the Wise Old Man In Dowdun Reach
keysToEntries.Objective_2: Speak to Doric in Dowdun Reach
keysToEntries.Objective_0: Speak to Cathan at the eastern gates in Fellhollow
keysToEntries.Objective_4: Collect the Shards of the Dreaming Stone around Dowdun Reach: 0/13
keysToEntries.WoM_NotSpokenTo_1: Ah, you're here, good. Looks like you had an easier time getting here than we did. Troublesome people here, had to give a few of them quite a wallop.
keysToEntries.WoM_NotSpokenTo_2: I had hoped to be hunting down the next dragon, try and whittle down Kuldra's forces. But, wouldn't you know it, there's a whole ruddy keep in the way. Full of angry Zamorakians.
keysToEntries.WoM_NotSpokenTo_3: Quite the bother. Good thing you're here though, you can get us moving again. The way is blocked by something called the Gatekeeper. Not sure what it is, or who it is, but it's in the way.
keysToEntries.WoM_NotSpokenTo_4: There's a nasty magical barrier before we can even reach them, but Doric's working on it as we speak. You can find him up in the Nexus of this place. Though you might want to set up a basecamp here first.
keysToEntries.WoM_SpokenTo_NotDoric: Go speak to Doric, who's working on a solution. You can find him up in the Nexus of this place. Though you might want to set up a basecamp here first.
keysToEntries.WoM_SpokenToDoric_GatekeeperDoor: Don't mind me, just dealing with some shennanigans from a talking crystal that is definitely not sinister, honest. Best get back to it I suppose.
keysToEntries.WoM_GatekeeperDoorOpen_TitanNotDefeated_: A black knight titan... made from the fusion of demonic sorcery, cthonian nonsense and dragonkin mad science...
Do you know, I really miss the old days when things were normal. Just give me a dragon I can hit and call it day, none of this nightmare amalgam poppcock!
Anyway, best go kill it before it gets loose and learns to sail back to the mainland to upend society. You know, like these things do.
keysToEntries.WoM_TitanDefeated_UmS_Not_Out: Well, glad to hear that the titan is dealt with. Nasty piece of work. Doric says he can get the door unstuck any day now, letting us into the desert. Locals called it the Umbral Sands... sounds creepy.
keysToEntries.WoM_TitanDefeated_UmS_Out: Well, glad to hear that the titan is dealt with. Nasty piece of work. But now we can take a look at that desert. So what are you waiting around here for? Go on! Get!
keysToEntries.Doric_NotSpokenTo_1: Nasty place this, ain't it? Crazed black knights, somehow still around even though it looks like they should have all died long ago
keysToEntries.Doric_NotSpokenTo_2: Problem being, we're stuck in this place until we can get this ruddy door open. It's barring our way to the desert, and nothing I try seems to fix it.
keysToEntries.Doric_NotSpokenTo_3: As far as I can tell, it's all connected to that great big stone over there. Seems like some sort of mystical doohicky that powers it all.
keysToEntries.Doric_NotSpokenTo_4: It's broken currently, a few pieces missing. I figure if we repair it, we might get that barrier open. Go give the stone a look will you? See what it needs to get it fixed.
keysToEntries.Doric_SpokenTo_NeedShards_None: We can't get through to the desert unless we can open this barrier. It seems to be connected to that stone over there, see if you can find all the pieces of it, would you?
keysToEntries.Doric_SpokenTo_NeedShards_Some: Looks like you're making some headway on those shards, keep it going. When it's repaired, hopefully that crystal will tell us how to get this barrier open.
keysToEntries.Doric_SpokenTo_NeedShards_All: You've got them all! Wonderful! Best go fix up that big, weird, stone and see if we can get this barrier open.
keysToEntries.Doric_SpokenTo_DoorUnlocked: An ancient dragonkin brain in a rock, eh? Weird choice, but to each their own I guess. Well, the barrier's down now, but there's something big and dangerous beyond. Your area I think.
keysToEntries.Doric_TitanDefeated_UmS_Not_Out: With the gatekeeper defeated, we should have that door open in no time. Take some time to kick back and relax, and we'll get working on it.
keysToEntries.Doric_TitanDefeated_UmS_Out: Now that the Gatekeeper is gone, the way to the Umbral Sands is open. We'll meet you there, just got a few things to finish up here.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_5_8_Shards: Pieces of me, falling into place... a name on the edge of memory...
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_8_12_Shards_1: I am nearly whole...I walked the world, striding above it and overseeing everything... I was in a desert, sand beneath my feet and knowledge all around me...
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_8-12_Shards_2: Yet, I cannot remember all... I must... you must... the last pieces, return them to me. Make me whole once more and I can aid you in your quest.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_AllShards_2: Mwaha, mwahaha, mwahahahaha! Foolish little mammal, so simple in your thoughts that you cannot even grasp the depths of my genius. My name, it would rattle around in your tiny skull like the echoes within a cave.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_AllShards_3: Tremble, ignorant creature and marvel in my majesty! For I am Avisk the eternal, the greatest mind to ever grace the Aughra! Now, you will submit to me and become my thrall so that together we may...
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_AllShards_4: Why are you looking at me like that?
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_AllShards_PlayerChoice_1: I can just smash the crystal again
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_AllShards_PlayerChoice_2: I have a hammer and I'm not afraid to use it
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_AllShards_6: Hrmm... yes... well... your logic is sound. Perhaps I was a little overzealous in my bluster. Happens when you've been alone in the psyche of deranged black knights for too long.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_AllShards_7: I blame the cthonian, horrible things. Anyway, you wanted that barrier lowered? it's no trouble, just let me fiddle a few things and... yep, there you go. No hard feelings eh?
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_AllShards_8: Just... be wary of Lord Rasmodel out there. Had a bit of a mishap when he smashed me to pieces and now he's got some of me, some of a demon and a whole lot of anger issues. Best give him a swift death if you ask me.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Gatekeeper_Alive_1: I've unlocked the barrier for you. You'll just have to get rid of that great big gatekeeper. Unless you want to surrender your mind to me so that I may do it and then walk this world as a living god?
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_TitanDefeated_UmS_Not_Out_: Oh, you slew the titan? I am impressed. Really you are quite capable, aren't you? When I find myself a new body and walk the world as a living god, you should come work for me. We'll do great things together!

It's been a long time since that gate was open, the one to the desert, I'll need a little while to get it all unstuck. Please be patient -- and resist getting impatient with your hammer -- and we'll have you knee deep in sand in no time.
Here, have a little something as a bribe... I mean as a reward for not smashing me to pieces with a hammer.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_TitanDefeated_UmS_Out_1: Oh, you slew the titan? I am impressed. Really you are quite capable, aren't you? When I find myself a new body and walk the world as a living god, you should come work for me. We'll do great things together!
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_TitanDefeated_UmS_Out_2: There, now, the gate's open and you're free to go. Nay, I'd say you're encouraged to go.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_TitanDefeated_UmS_Out_3: Please... please go, and take your hammer with you. Here, a little bribe so that you and your hammer get very far away from me.
keysToEntries.Goblin_Chef_NotSpokenTo_1: Ah, delightful, a human not driven mad by the strange afflictions of this place. Delighted, delighted to meet you, truly.
keysToEntries.Goblin_Chef_NotSpokenTo_2: Ah, yes, forgive my loquaciousness, you do seem frightfully busy. I deduce from your bearing that you require the shards of the Dreaming Stone.
keysToEntries.Goblin_Chef_NotSpokenTo_3: As it happens I have one with me - indeed it is the reason for a vocabulary far removed from the rest of my kin. Intellect is a terrible curse, however, and I would sooner remove it then keep it.
keysToEntries.Goblin_Chef_SpokenTo_GotMeal_1: Ah, yes, there it is. The sweet smell of succulent maple, autumnal molasses encased in delicious crisp bread. Oh, yes, this is worth losing my intellect for. May I?
keysToEntries.Goblin_Chef_SpokenTo_NotGotMeal: One of my kindred guards a recipe that I would like to try before I give you the shard. He can be found in the shed near the waterfall, guarded by a zogre.
keysToEntries.Goblin_Chef_QuestComplete_Repeat: Eh? Wat? Wat you want? Go away, I'm trying to lick all the syrup off me nose and there's an awful lot of nose.
keysToEntries.Zamorakian_Mage_NotSpokenTo_1: She whispers, do you hear her? For an eternity she crawls round and round inside my mind fighting with the other voice, the two of them at each other's throats.
keysToEntries.Zamorakian_Mage_NotSpokenTo_2: They steal memories too. How dare they! But I hid mine! Sealed it in glass. Hoot hoot! Memories safe, happiness encased in translucent gleam!
keysToEntries.Zamorakian_Mage_NotSpokenTo_3: You! Apprentice! Acolyte? Silent shadow whose soul does not scream. Find my glittering memory. Glass winged hunter of the night. Bring it me, let me smile again?
keysToEntries.Zamorakian_Mage_Repeat_NotGotGlass: You! Apprentice! Acolyte? Silent shadow whose soul does not scream. Find my glittering memory. Glass winged hunter of the night. Bring it me, let me smile again?
keysToEntries.Zamorakian_Mage_GiveContainer_1: A memory in glass. A smile in kaleidoscopic fragments. Abraxus always was an artist at heart.
keysToEntries.Zamorakian_Mage_GiveContainer_2: Thank you, apprentice. Here, take this bauble as payment. But beware, it whispers in the dark!
keysToEntries.Zamorakian_Mage_GivenShard_Repeat_1: Whispering whispering, crawling round my head. I sealed them in the glittering prison, yet they skitter and jitter so.
keysToEntries.Zamorakian_Mage_GivenShard_Repeat_2: Perhaps I am looking in the wrong places. Scratching the itch outside, rather than within? A knife to the skull, perhaps, cut the rot out from within?
keysToEntries.WoM_Optional_Dialogue1_1: What little research I have managed so far indicates that this place was known as Dowdun Reach.
keysToEntries.WoM_Optional_Dialogue1_2: A group of human settlers moved here from Fellhollow some time before... well... before it all turned spooky and weird. They called themselves the Children of Shadows, because everyone wants to be edgy.
keysToEntries.WoM_Optional_Dialogue1_3: I think they wanted this to be some bastion of chaos. A sanctuary for Zamorak worshipers, where they could be as evil as they wanted - or pretended - to be, without upsetting the locals.
keysToEntries.WoM_Optional_Dialogue2_1: That's the question, isn't it? They should all be dead really, especially after Fellhollow, but here they are.
keysToEntries.WoM_Optional_Dialogue2_2: Though I wouldn't exactly call this living. It seems they've become twisted, corrupted somehow, into something not entirely human.
keysToEntries.WoM_Optional_Dialogue2_3: There's no reasoning with them. They're each completely off their rocker and very violent about it too. I had to blast three of them earlier, when I was eating a sandwich. Terribly rude of them, really.
keysToEntries.WoM_Optional_Dialogue3_1: Back on the mainland they'd be called the Kinshra, or black knights, but this is a different order. The Kinshra were formed sometime in the early fifth age, but it looks like these weirdos might be a bit older than that.
keysToEntries.WoM_Optional_Dialogue3_2: They're Zamorak worshippers. Followers of the god of chaos. Utter nonsense really. Just a bunch of selfish idiots who want an excuse to be bullies and dabble in sinister magic, that sort of thing.
keysToEntries.Wom_Optional_Dialogue4_1: Ah, the god of chaos, or rebellion if you're a more generous sort. He was once one of the Mahjarrat, a cruel race of shapeshifting sorcerers from the world of Freneskae.
keysToEntries.Wom_Optional_Dialogue4_2: He came into power when he attempted to kill Zaros, in the second age, and stole as much of his power as he could. Zaros survived, sort of, but that's beside the point.
keysToEntries.Wom_Optional_Dialogue4_3: Ever since then Zamorak set himself up as public enemy number one to any sapient being with half a braincell. He was one of the most active combatants in the God Wars and has continued to be the source of most problems ever since.
keysToEntries.Wom_Optional_Dialogue4_4: Recently he returned to Gielinor, while Guthix's edicts were down, and made a right mess of things. Adventurers had to kick him back to Infernus and recreate the Edicts so that he couldn't come back.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_1_1_Some: I am a voice without a name, howling in the wilderness of memory.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_1_2_Some: I was not always this. Once I walked these lands, towering above it.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_1_3_Some: Bound into stone, so that I might escape some dread nightmare.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_1_4_Some: Now, I am lost and broken. Just shadows on the wall.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_2_1_Some: A grand project of cooperation, crafted by a society of paranoid narcissists.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_2_2_Some: They sought power over others and soon power over one another. Reaching across worlds without the knowledge to control what lay beyond.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_2_3_Some: More than was bargained for lay beyond the gate. It would lead to their undoing.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_3_1_Some: Once a man, but now no longer. Three minds rage within a twisted form.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_3_2_Some: Strength without mercy. Fury without reason. He is death, and he waits for you.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_1_1_Most: Many of your lifetimes ago, I walked this realm safe in the knowledge that there were none who could harm me. I was a scientist, a scholar, a genius dragged down by weaker minds.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_1_2_Most: Until the creation of one of my small-minded colleagues grew too arrogant and thought to strike down her masters.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_1_3_Most: While the others died, or cowered in fear waiting for their destruction, I preserved my intellect within this very stone. My knowledge, my genius, my... what you call soul... would live forever enshrined in glittering stone.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_1_4_Most: One day, perhaps, I shall be freed from this prismatic prison, so that I might share my vast wisdom with the lesser beings of this world. Yet, I am not whole enough yet to do so, and more pieces of me remain.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_2_1_Most: They called themselves the Children of Shadows, though sometimes Darkness, they wanted to be seen as sinister secretive masters.  A society of chaos and rebellion, if you can imagine such a thing.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_2_2_Most: Some sought martial expertise, others delved into arcane knowledge and secrets that the rest of your kind foolishly abhor. It was these who found me buried in the sand of the desert, biding my time.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_2_3_Most: Soon distrust began to brew among the humans. I had some small part to play in that. Boredom is a poison no one can resist, and the antidote showed itself in small amusements. A whisper here, a secret there, and soon they turned on each other without mercy.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_2_4_Most: They summoned demons. They trained armies and all the while they unsheathed their knifes and looked to one another.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_2_5_Most: In time their rivalries turned into open conflict and that damned fool, Rasmodel, turned his blade upon myself and shattered the Dreaming Stone.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_2_6_Most: I am unsure of what happened next, but the powers involved, the sheer magic at work... it had a terrible effect on this fortress. Those who dwelled within became twisted and strange. Life prolonged, but cursed with madness.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_2_7_Most: It has been fascinating to watch.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_3_1_Most: Lord Rasmodel waits beyond that barrier, though he is not as he was. The energies he absorbed from my shattering, as well as that cthonian's parasitic cells have transformed him into something different.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_3_2_Most: A great giant of a man, a titan one might say. Flesh twisted, mind shattered, but far more deadly than before.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_3_3_Most: A fragment of myself drives him, separate from the mind that speaks to you now. In a strange way, like the knights of this place, I am in a civil war of myself.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_Optional_Dialogue_3_4_Most: But the voice that speaks to you now, know that it is far more your ally than the beast beyond that barrier. At least a voice of crystal, you can trust.
keysToEntries.Goblin_Chef_SpokenTo_GotMeal_2: Exquisite! Truly a festival of flavour. How it tickles the tongue and delights the taste buds. Yes, yes, it's truly magnificent.
keysToEntries.HutGoblin_NotSpokenToChef: Go away. I gots nothin for yas.
keysToEntries.HutGoblin_NotSpokenToButSpokenToChef: Hrmm, you want the besterest recipe? Is secret. I won'ts tells ya nothin unless ya gives da password.
keysToEntries.HutGoblin_IncorrectPassword1: Nope, that's not the password. Get lost! No password, no food!
keysToEntries.HutGoblin_IncorrectPassword2: Old chef know password. But he been hidden up in the tower, behind you, for ages now. Hope he comes soon, I needs the loo... though I don't know where one is...
keysToEntries.HutGoblin_CorrectPassword: Oh! You do know password. Ok, fair enough. Here, the recipe you was lookin' for.
keysToEntries.HutGoblin_PlayerChoice1: Give it or I'll hit you?
keysToEntries.Goblin_Chef_Diary1: What little sanity I cling to is eroded each day I have to speak to those vile creatures. Those goblins.
keysToEntries.Goblin_Chef_Diary2: They can be given simple tasks, and excel at anything that involves blind violence, but anything that requires finesse appears woefully beyond them.
keysToEntries.Goblin_Chef_Diary3: There is one of them, the disgustingly named "Snotpit", who has taken it upon himself to guard my secrets and refuses to give them to me unless I utter the password. The password, of course, is to simply say "please".
keysToEntries.Goblin_Chef_Diary4:
Politeness, an alien concept to these foul beasts.
Still, they are better companions than the mould on the wall. It whispers strange new recipes to me that are alien and strange, both toxic and delicious. I must listen closer, or I might just go mad.
keysToEntries.HutGoblin_SpokenToAndSpokenToChef: I won'ts tells ya nothin unless ya gives da password.
keysToEntries.Goblin_Chef_SpokenTo_GotMeal_3: Here, the shard, as promised. It has been a most delightful experience meeting you. I wish you well in all your adventures.
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Alrics_Notes_1:
There are those who say that knowledge has power, but they are only half right. Knowledge only has power if that knowledge has purpose. 
The right facts applied the right way, can change the destiny of a nation far more than the facts on their own are able.
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Alrics_Notes_2: In that regard, knowledge is a key to power. Learn this well acolyte and you may begin to understand the depths of your ignorance and take the first steps to unshackle yourself from its chains.
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Alrics_Notes_3: One must first, understand that to obtain real knowledge, know that you know nothing.
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Alrics_Notes_4: One must seek knowledge, striving towards a great comprehension of the true nature of the world.
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Alrics_Notes_5: One must have ambition also. For what is power without purpose? A liability. A chain. A noose. Power must be put to task, or it is mere languid possibility, that none should seek.
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Alrics_Notes_6: Finally, one must understand the purpose of power is not to control others, but to set oneself free. For what greater rebellion is there, than knowing you walk a path entirely of your own construction?
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Alrics_Notes_7: Learn these lessons, acolyte, and perhaps you will be worthy of my attention.
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Correct_Book_1: The Empty Throne - Forgetting Worthless Gods.
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Correct_Book_2: The Eye of Occulus - In Search of Answers
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Correct_Book_3: Taking the Crown - Zamorak's Ambitious Plan to Acquire All the Elder Artefacts
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Correct_Book_4: Zamorak's Rebellion - Shattering the Chains of Infernus
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Incorrect_Book_1: Zaros - Why He Should Be Forgotten
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Incorrect_Book_2: Cthonian Contracts - Tying Evil Up In Knots
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Incorrect_Book_3: Vampyres - Blood Is Thicker Than Water
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Incorrect_Book_4: Glamorak - How a God's Fashion Can Incite Rebellion
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Correct_Confirm: A mechanism clicks, as if a key has been turned in a lock.
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Incorrect_Reset: A mechanism whirs angrily into life, resetting your progress.
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Correct_Finish: A mechanism whirs into life, directing arcane energies toward the centre of the library. The barrier over the door fades away.
keysToEntries.Zamorak_Mage_Chest_First_Time: You find a small glass owl, wrapped carefully in red cloth to protect it from the elements.
keysToEntries.Zamorak_Mage_Chest_Empty: The chest is empty save for one confused spider trying to make itself a new home. You lock eyes, but say nothing to one another. Strangers passing by.
keysToEntries.Goblin_Chef_NotSpokenTo_4: But to do so, one must first savour a last meal while I have the capacity to do so. Snotpit, a most obnoxious lout, guards a recipe in a shed by the waterfall, near a zogre's stomping ground.
keysToEntries.Goblin_Chef_NotSpokenTo_5: I yearn to taste of it. If you find the recipe, and bring me that forbidden dish, then the shard will be yours and I can return myself to a state more becoming of my nature.
keysToEntries.Goblin_Chef_React_Keep_Sandwich: No? Well certainly you are entitled to keep your meal, just as I shall keep this shard. When you change your mind, I shall be here.
keysToEntries.Zamorakian_Mage_NotSpokenTo_4: I left my memories in a tranquil place, buried in the dirt. A garden where all the keep's creatures used to live. A dragon guards it now, though they know not what it is they protect.
keysToEntries.Zamorakian_Mage_QuestComplete_1: Whispering whispering, crawling round my head. I sealed my memories in the glittering prison, yet they skitter and jitter so.
keysToEntries.Zamorakian_Mage_QuestComplete_2: Perhaps I am looking in the wrong places. Scratching the itch outside, rather than within? A knife to the skull, perhaps, cut the rot out from within?
keysToEntries.Goblin_Fisherman_NotSpokenTo_1: It not workin! This rod must be broken! I stab and stab and stab and still no fish!
keysToEntries.Goblin_Fisherman_NotSpokenTo_2: You! You're a human... I think. Might be an elf? I dunno, all pink and wobbly the lot of you. You know how this thing works, yeah? You get me fish!
keysToEntries.Goblin_Fisherman_NotSpokenTo_3: I want that fish! You sees it? The weird one, swimming around looking all strange and stuff. I bet it tastes delicious! Get me it and I'll give you something, fair trade, yeah?
keysToEntries.Goblin_Fisherman_SpokenTo_NoFish_Repeat: I want that fish! You sees it? The weird one, swimming around looking all strange and stuff. I bet it tastes delicious! Get me it and I'll give you something, fair trade, yeah?
keysToEntries.Goblin_Fisherman_SpokenTo_HaveFish_1: You gots it! Gimme, I been looking forward to eating this!
keysToEntries.Goblin_Fisherman_SpokenTo_HaveFish_3: Blerg! This horrible and hard, like eating a rock... wait... that IS a rock! Why would fish be eating weird shiny rock? Blerg!
keysToEntries.Goblin_Fisherman_SpokenTo_HaveFish_4: Ere, you have it. I don't want it and I did promise you a reward. Don't say I ain't a goblin of me word!
keysToEntries.Goblin_Fisherman_Quest_Complete_Repeat: Go away, you're scarin' off the fishes!
keysToEntries.Crypt_Note_1: Crazy people wants me shiny rock. I ain't giving them no shiny rock. Probably explains why they stabbed me.
keysToEntries.Crypt_Note_2: Eh, still my rock, they can't have it. I buried it somewhere they'd never think to look. In one of them big 'orrible graves of theirs.
keysToEntries.Crypt_Note_4: I'm also a bit dizzy. I'll finish writing this and then maybe have a nap before I go and annoy the wife. She wants chicken for dinner and I don't want feathers in me teefs.
keysToEntries.Quest_Description: Uncover the secrets of Dowdun Reach in order to unlock your way through to the Umbral Sands.
keysToEntries.GoblinFisherman_FishingNode_NotGotFish: With much effort, you are able to reach in and grab a fish with your bare hands. It occurs to you that there must be a better way to do this and you start to think about how.
keysToEntries.GoblinFisherman_FishingNode_NoSpace: You could try and grab a fish, but you're carrying too much right now.
keysToEntries.GoblinFisherman_FishingNode_AlreadyGrabb: The fish are wise to your tactics and you'll not be able to grab them by hand again. You'll need to think up some new method of... fishing...
keysToEntries.Cathan_DR_Entrance_1: Never ends, does it? I've been investigating this place, seems to lead to some great fortress or other. It's blocking access to the Umbral Sands.
keysToEntries.Cathan_DR_Entrance_2: I was gonna scout it out, spooky like, but I made the mistake of mentioning it to the Wise Old Man and... well... you know what he's like.
keysToEntries.Cathan_DR_Entrance_3: Said something about "rubbish defences" or "amateur hour wards" and stormed off down this passage to who knows where.
keysToEntries.Cathan_DR_Entrance_4: Probably want to go after him? Most likely he'll set himself up somewhere central, like a nexus or something, see if you can find it.
keysToEntries.Death_LinkToDowdun_1: You did well here, in Fellhollow, but there is yet more for you to do. A, shall we say, colleague of mine awaits your help in the Umbral Sands.
keysToEntries.Death_LinkToDowdun_2: I have sent young Cathan ahead to gather allies and investigate. You should find him to the east of Fellhollow.
keysToEntries.Cathan_SpokenTo_1: The Wise Old Man's headed further into this area, something about dark powers and such stuff.
keysToEntries.Cathan_SpokenTo_2: Knowing him, he'll have set himself up somewhere important, like a nexus type spot. You should head in and try to find him.
keysToEntries.Cathan_PostQuest_1: Black knight titans and weird demonic curses. Ashenfall really doesn't seem to catch a break, does it?
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_TitanDefeated_Reward_NoSpa: Oh, you're hands are full... I do somewhat miss hands... anyway, come back and see me when you've got some space free for my little gift. .
keysToEntries.Library_Puzzle_Door_NoEntry: An ancient spell, enforced by some mechanism, prevents further access. You will need to find some way to unlock it.
keysToEntries.Bastion_Barrier_NoEntry: The barrier is solid and impassable, you will need to find some way to pass through it.
keysToEntries.Umbral_Sands_Door_NoEntry: The door will not open... for now.
keysToEntries.DreamingStone_NoShards_4: The stone appears to be shattered, with large pieces missing from it as if struck at a single point with a large, blunt, instrument. You believe it would be easy to repair if you found all the pieces.
keysToEntries.Objective_4_1: Collect the Shards of the Dreaming Stone around Dowdun Reach: 1/13
keysToEntries.Objective_4_2: Collect the Shards of the Dreaming Stone around Dowdun Reach: 2/13
keysToEntries.Objective_4_3: Collect the Shards of the Dreaming Stone around Dowdun Reach: 3/13
keysToEntries.Objective_4_4: Collect the Shards of the Dreaming Stone around Dowdun Reach: 4/13
keysToEntries.Objective_4_5: Collect the Shards of the Dreaming Stone around Dowdun Reach: 5/13
keysToEntries.Objective_4_6: Collect the Shards of the Dreaming Stone around Dowdun Reach: 6/13
keysToEntries.Objective_4_7: Collect the Shards of the Dreaming Stone around Dowdun Reach: 7/13
keysToEntries.Objective_4_8: Collect the Shards of the Dreaming Stone around Dowdun Reach: 8/13
keysToEntries.Objective_4_9: Collect the Shards of the Dreaming Stone around Dowdun Reach: 9/13
keysToEntries.Objective_4_10: Collect the Shards of the Dreaming Stone around Dowdun Reach: 10/13
keysToEntries.Objective_4_11: Collect the Shards of the Dreaming Stone around Dowdun Reach: 11/13
keysToEntries.Objective_4_12: Collect the Shards of the Dreaming Stone around Dowdun Reach: 12/13
keysToEntries.Objective_4_13:
Collect the Shards of the Dreaming Stone around Dowdun Reach: 13/13
Return to the Dreaming Stone
keysToEntries.Objective_4_-1: Speak to Doric about the shards around Dowdun Reach
keysToEntries.DoorToDowdun_NotFinishedWitheringH: A powerful force holds this door shut. A chill runs down your spine reminding you that Death has a job for you and it's a good idea not to forget about it.
keysToEntries.Goblin_Chef_HaveRecipe_NoSandwich: Ah, he has given you the recipe for the most sublime of feasts - the Maple Toastie. Please, if you would make one for me, why it would be the most exquisite thing!
keysToEntries.Teleporter.NotUnlockedQuestStep: Symbols flash across the portal stone, indicating that you lack the authorisation to progress.

keysToEntries.PostiePete_NotSpokenTo_1: Haha, got here a-head of you did I? That's the advantage of the postal service, rain or snow, we'll always deliver.
keysToEntries.PostiePete_NotSpokenTo_2: Now then, a client of mine has set up a special service for you here. They've got some tasty little items and no-body to sell them to back on the mainland.
keysToEntries.PostiePete_NotSpokenTo_3: But here, there's something right up their alley, and I figured we could all help each other out. See, they want some of those Infernal Fragments, the weird little demon stones around the keep.
keysToEntries.PostiePete_NotSpokenTo_4: You bring those and in exchange you can help yourself to some of Evil Da-- err, some of my client's special collection. Take a look!
keysToEntries.PostiePete_SpokenToRepeat_OpenShop: Oi oi, got any Infernal Fragments you want to exchange in the... ahem... EVIL EMPORIUM!!!... I didn't name it.

keysToEntries.Teleport.Name: Nodestone
keysToEntries.PortalLocked: It's a one-way lodestone but it appears to be blocked at the other end.

keysToEntries.Doric_Objective_Final_3: Some sort of sinister staff. I can see how they might put something like this together. Here, let me teach you, my way of an apology for the wild goose chase.
keysToEntries.Doric_Objective_Final_1: So... haha, you're not going to believe this, but that mage never took the dog at all. When he knocked into me, it fell outa me pocket and rolled behind a rock.
keysToEntries.Doric_Objective_2_1: You just missed that ruddy red mage. He ran right past me, waving a great spiky staff around without fear of poking anyone's eyes out.
keysToEntries.Doric_Objective_3_1: Elusive little blighter this one, ran straight past me again. You're catching up, I think.
keysToEntries.Doric_MainQuestHook: While I have you, would you mind helping me out with something else?
keysToEntries.Doric_Intro_3: That wizard, he took it and then leapt straight through those lodestone like things over there. There were a brief flash of pews and religious things, then he were gone.
keysToEntries.Objective_ReturnToDoric: Speak to Doric in the Dowdun Reach Nexus.
keysToEntries.Doric_Objective_Final_2: Definite egg on my face right now, haha. Still, did you get anything useful out of him? Oooh a schematic eh? Let me take a look, least I can do.
keysToEntries.Doric_Intro_2: It were a gift, you see, from me boy, Boric. It was the first thing he made when I finally let him at the forge. Took him hours it did, never been more proud.
keysToEntries.Doric_PlayerSaidNo: I understand, you must be busy. If you get a chance though, come see me, I could really use your help.
keysToEntries.Doric_Intro_1: Thanks, I knew you were a good sort. So, you got here just after this blasted red wizard barged into me and mugged me. Took a little bronze dog on a keychain, I need it back.
keysToEntries.Doric_Optional_PortalsActive_1: All the nodestones are up and running now, well done my friend, well done indeed!
keysToEntries.Doric_Optional_PortalsActive_3: What? You're expecting some sort of reward? Aren't all these handy shortcuts enough for you?
keysToEntries.Doric_Optional_PortalsActive_4: Honestly, adventurers these days, bunch of mercenaries, the lot of 'em. Here, take these, they should help you out a bit.
keysToEntries.Doric_Optional_PlayerHasNoSpace: After begging for a reward you haven't got any space free to carry it. Come back to me when you've got space in your pack.
keysToEntries.ZamorakMage_TempleNote_1: These notes are incoherent ramblings about the nature of portal magic. Seems like the ones in the keep don't use runes, but are powered by the Dreaming Stone somehow.
keysToEntries.ZamorakMage_Note_2: Sacrifices essential... power not enough... must draw power from knights... demon is not ready! Must find gate!
keysToEntries.Quest_Description: Help Doric track down the mage that stole his keepsake, by unlocking portals around Dowdun Reach.
keysToEntries.Objective_Temple: Look for signs of the mugger-mage in the Cathedral in Dowdun Keep.
keysToEntries.Objective_Garrison: Look for signs of the mugger-mage in the Garrisons of Dowdun Keep.
keysToEntries.Objective_Aquifer: Look for signs of the mugger-mage in the underground Demon Gate of Dowdun Keep.
keysToEntries.Doric_Intro_4: See if you can follow him, find out where he went. It might help you unlock some of these 'ere nodestones which'll make getting round 'ere much easier!
keysToEntries.Doric_Objective_1_NoProgress_1: That red-robed mage blindsided me and stole a beloved keepsake of mine. Why? Who knows.  Last I saw he went through a portal to some cathedral or other.
keysToEntries.Doric_Objective_1_NoProgress_2: See if you can follow him, find out where he went. It might help you unlock some of these 'ere nodestones which'll make getting round 'ere much easier!
keysToEntries.Doric_Objective_2_2: This time the portal seemed to be all chains, cells and angry looking knights. Maybe a garrison or something? Best look around.
keysToEntries.Doric_Objective_3_2: They went through the portal at the back there. I got a brief glimpse of glowing lights, water and rock. Some sort of sinister demonic gateway, maybe?
keysToEntries.Doric_Objective_Final_4_PortalHook: While you're at it, these nodestones -- they feel like they'd be quite useful to have active. If you can get them all up and running come see me, I'll have a little something for you.
keysToEntries.Doric_Optional_PortalsActive_Some: You managed to get some of these nodestones working, great work! See if you can get the rest of them working!
keysToEntries.ZamorakMage_GarrisonNote_1: These schematics seem to be something to do with emotion-based magic, as if trying to draw energy from suffering somehow. The notes look like they intend to use it on some source of great suffering.
keysToEntries.ZamorakMage_NoSignOfMugger: There's no sign of the mugger, but the nearby nodestone seems to crackle with lingering energy. Perhaps you just missed them.
keysToEntries.ZamorakMage_Examine_1: The wounds are fresh, you arrived just after this mage was brutally slaughtered. Their robes are singed and claw marks rake across what's left of their torso.
keysToEntries.ZamorakMage_Examine_2: You fumble around their pockets, but find no sign of Doric's keepsake. Instead you find a crumbled up schematic of some kind, perhaps Doric can make sense of it.

keysToEntries.Vannaka_SpokenTo_SomeJournals: I see you've got one of their training journals. Good work, young pup, but I need all three to unravel their techniques.
keysToEntries.HoundMaster_Journal_1: I know they're just demons, but they're such good girls. I taught Gnasher how to sit, and Fido how to fetch.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_MoreInfo_Summoner_1: Lester Flament, is what the records say. Sounds like an odious person, unpopular even in a place like this.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_MoreInfo_Summoner_2: Nowadays he's found wandering the crypts south of here, below the temple, with one eerily loyal demon.
keysToEntries.HoundMaster_Journal_2: Sure, Fido came back with a junior knight's still beating heart in her mouth, instead of the ball, but she's trying, bless her.
keysToEntries.Summoner_Journal_3: Oooh, I should make sure the cake has sprinkles!
keysToEntries.Vannaka_SpokenTo_AllJournals_1: You've got them all? Great, well done, young pup. Let's take a look at them then, see what sinister secrets they've learned...
keysToEntries.RatMaster_Journal_3: But these hellrats? They live forever! An eternity of cute twitchy whiskers and fluffy little bellies in need of a scritch. What could be better?
keysToEntries.Summoner_Journal_2: After all, they may be big, scary, and made of claws, but they might be the best friends I've ever had.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_MoreInfo_Hook: What do you need, young pup?
keysToEntries.Vannaka_MoreInfo_Houndmaster_2: Records seemed to indicate that he was a noble with a love for dogs. So it makes sense that he would try and wield the power of dog shaped demons.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_SpokenTo_NoJournals: Young pup, I need you to track down three demon trainers, and bring me their journals. I want to know what cruelties they have unleashed on the beasts here.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_Intro_3: Young pup, I need you to track these three down and bring me their training manuals. I want to know what cruelties they have unleashed on the beasts here.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_MoreInfo_Houndmaster_1: From what I can tell, before the maddening corruption took him over, he was a knight called Philius Rexiton.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_Intro_2: Three of them seem particularly dangerous, having learned how to bind demonic beasts to their service somehow.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_MoreInfo_Houndmaster_3: He spends his time in the dungeons at the garisson, on the lower floors. There he seems to have gained the loyalty, or at least service, of a whole pack of hellhounds.
keysToEntries.PlayerChoice_MoreInfo_RatMaster: Tell me about the Rat Master
keysToEntries.Vannaka_MoreInfo_RatMaster_3: Seems that he's still doing that, even with his mind turned to soup by this strange corruption madness.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_MoreInfo_RatMaster_1: According to the records here, his name was Walthar Skrexis. Kept mostly to himself, and had terrible hygiene habits.
keysToEntries.HoundMaster_Journal_3: All these mages and their complicated summoning and binding nonsense, and really all you need to do is throw them a decent steak every once in a while.
keysToEntries.RatMaster_Journal_1: I love rats, they're so cute. What with their little whispers and their adorable inability to control their bladder.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_MoreInfo_RatMaster_2: Countless reports from the custodial team about how he would leave crumbs and bits of food everywhere, constantly attracting vermin.
keysToEntries.Summoner_Journal_1: I'm not sure if demon's even have birthdays, but I think I should get Delrom a cake. It's the polite thing to do.
keysToEntries.RatMaster_Journal_2: The tragedy being that they only live for such a short time. A year, maybe two. Barely a heartbeat.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_Intro_1: Black knights. I had hoped their special brand of madness was kept to the mainland, but here they are, even if they're not quite the knights I know.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_MoreInfo_RatMaster_4: Seems he spends most of his times in the menagerie, south west of here.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_SpokenTo_AllJournals_2: Huh? Really? I was expecting dark rites, sinister spells and all that Zamorakian magic nonsense, but it's just...treating them nicely?
keysToEntries.Vannaka_SpokenTo_AllJournals_3: I have a horrible feeling there's a metaphor there, but I'm too old and jaded to think about it. Here, you did what I asked, so I owe you something for your efforts.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_SpokenTo_AllJournals_4: This trinket should help you with all these demonic creatures. I don't think we have the time for the friendlier approach.
keysToEntries.Vannaka_PostQuest: Being nice to demons, what utter nonsense.
keysToEntries.Objective_Info_FoundJournal: You found some pages from a Journal belonging to someone at Dowdun Reach, Vannaka may know more.
keysToEntries.Objective_FindJournals_3:
Journal Pages Collected 3/3
Return to Vannaka
keysToEntries.Item_Description: Pages taken from the Journals of the Beast Tamers
keysToEntries.Book.TooDangerous: It's too dangerous to read this right now.

keysToEntries.Zanik.NotSpokenTo.LessThan10Samples: If you can get ten samples, I can study them to see if I can unlock any clues as to the nature of the madness-infection in Dowdun Reach.
keysToEntries.Quest.Description: Investigate the strange corruption afflicting the residents of Dowdun Reach.
keysToEntries.Quest.Objective.4: Find the source of the cthonian infection, somewhere high up in Dowdun Reach.
keysToEntries.Quest.Objective.3: Bring Zanik the ingredients. Dragons Blood. A Twilight Lily and some Unholy Water.
keysToEntries.Quest.Name: Biohazard
keysToEntries.Quest.Objective.1: Gather 10 biological samples from strange ravens in Dowdun Reach, and bring them to Zanik.
keysToEntries.Zanik.NotSpokenTo.1: Oh, hey, don't mind the cell, it's oddly comforting. I've been on the surface so long, I'd almost forgotten what it's like under the ground.
keysToEntries.Zanik.Has10Samples: Fantastic! You've got enough there for me to study. I'm going to need a few more things, though, before I can learn anything from them. If you're still willing to help.
keysToEntries.Zanik.NotSpokenTo.4: If you can gather samples from, say... ten of them, I can maybe get a sense of what's been causing their strange behaviour. It might help me get to the bottom of the insanity in the keep.
keysToEntries.Zanik.NotSpokenTo.2: It's quieter here too, gives me time to think and look into stuff. This whole place, it's riddled with some sort of infection, or corruption, both magical and... well... more.
keysToEntries.Zanik.NotSpokenTo.3: There are ravens around this keep that are... stranger than the rest. More vibrant, a little luminescent.
keysToEntries.Zanik.LessThan10Samples: If you can gather ten samples from them, I can study them to see if I can unlock any clues as to the nature of the madness-infection in Dowdun Reach.
keysToEntries.Zanik.SpokenTo.Has10Samples.1: Fantastic! You've got enough there for me to study. I'm going to need a few more things, though, before I can learn anything from them. If you're still willing to help.
keysToEntries.Zanik.SpokenTo.Has10Samples.2: First, I'm going to need some dragon's blood. You can get it from any dragon you find, though I'm sure they won't be willing to surrender it without a fight.
keysToEntries.Zanik.SpokenTo.Has10Samples.3: Then I'm going to need a twilight lily. They grow deep in the dark, by underground lakes. There's one not far from here.
keysToEntries.Zanik.SpokenTo.Has10Samples.4: Lastly I'm going to need some unholy water -- like holy water, only from Zamorak worshippers. There's probably a fountain somewhere near the Cathedral that has some.
keysToEntries.Zanik.NotGatheredIngredients.1: I need you to get me some reagents so I can study this corruption.
keysToEntries.Zanik.NotGatheredIngredients.2: First, I'm going to need some dragon's blood. You can get it from any dragon you find, though I'm sure they won't be willing to surrender it without a fight.
keysToEntries.Zanik.NotGatheredIngredients.3: Then I'm going to need a twilight lily. They grow deep in the dark, by underground lakes. There's one not far from here.
keysToEntries.Zanik.NotGatheredIngredients.4: Lastly I'm going to need some unholy water -- like holy water, only from Zamorak worshippers. There's probably a fountain somewhere near the Cathedral that has some.
keysToEntries.Zanik.GatheredIngredients.1: You've got them all. Great! May I take them?
keysToEntries.Zanik.GatheredIngredients.2: Brilliant. Right, let's take a look at what we have here...hrmm...there's a lot of magical energy here, a bit like the dragonkin effigies we've seen...
keysToEntries.Zanik.GatheredIngredients.3: Wait... there's more than one infection here. One magical, one biological and distinctly demonic. Not avernic though, oh no, this is rare indeed.
keysToEntries.Zanik.GatheredIngredients.4: There's traces of cthonian demon here. See the way it wriggles? It's odd though, a bit like a parasite. It's part of a bigger whole though, there must be a central intelligence for it somewhere.
keysToEntries.Zanik.GatheredIngredients.5: If we can find that central intelligence, it would help in finding a cure down the line. Since ravens were infected most, maybe look for somewhere they frequent? Somewhere high up?
keysToEntries.Zanik.GatheredIngredients.6: Oh, right. Well I'll need them to continue. Let me know if you're ready to hand them over.
keysToEntries.Zanik.NotFoundPutrilius: If we can find that central intelligence, it would help in finding a cure down the line. Since ravens were infected most, maybe look for somewhere they frequent? Somewhere high up?
keysToEntries.Zanik.FoundPutrilius.1: A cthonian demon... trapped in a chicken? Well that must be embarrassing. Still, you've got them to promise to reign in their infection and that means I can work on a cure.
keysToEntries.Zanik.FoundPutrilius.2: It'll take a while though, years probably, but we might see this place recover over time. Thanks for all your help. Here, something to thank you for your effort.
keysToEntries.Zanik.FoundPutrilius.NoSpace: Oh, you've got your hands full right now. Come back and see me when you've got some space free.
keysToEntries.Zanik.FoundPutrilius.3: It's not much, but it's very fashionable in these parts.
keysToEntries.Quest.Objective.5: Return to Zanik and explain the situation.
keysToEntries.Quest.Objective.0: Speak to Zanik about the Corrupted Essence you got from a strange raven.
keysToEntries.Zanik.NotGatheredIngredients.Choices: I'm going to need: Some Dragon Blood, Unholy Water and the leaves of a Twilight Lily
keysToEntries.Zanik.NotGatheredIngredients.Choices.Cho: Don't tell me you haven't seen the dragons roaming about? The blood of a dragon helps to add strength to the solution, unfortunately, you're going to need to kill one to get it.
Shouldn't be a problem for a strong adventurer like you right?
Water that's been blessed by the priests of Zamorak. I'd look in the temple, maybe not everything there has been tainted?
The Twilight Lily, is a special flower that grows in damp dark places. I think I saw one by that tree in the lake further into the cave.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQest.HasSamples.1: Bwaa-- oh it's you, I don't need to do the clucky thing. Oh, you've got some essence on you? Here, let me take it off your hands. Here you go.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQest.HasSamples.HasSpace: If you find any more, I'll be happy to take it off your hands.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQest.HasSamples.HasNoSpace: Oh, but you're somewhat overburdened. Best come back when you've got some space free.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQest.NotGotSamples.1: Bwaa-- oh it's you, I don't need to do the clucky thing. What do you want? I've already agreed I'll do what I can to quell this infection.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQest.NotGotSamples.2: If you bring me more of that essence you've found in the ravens, I'll give you some Infernal fragments for every ten essence you bring me.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQuest.ChoseWho.1: I am Putriulius, grand-sub-sub-clerk of the great office of numerical records... which is to say that in the cthonian hierarchy, I was no one.
keysToEntries.Player.PostQuest.Choice.GotAnyEggs: Got any eggs I can have?
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQuest.ChoseWho.2: I was small, quiet and easily forgotten, which goes a long way to explain why I wasn't eaten. I lived my life the blandest way imaginable, but I was happy in my own little way.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQuest.ChoseWhat.1: I am a cthonian demon. Once we were the rulers of Infernus, an existence of complex contracts and even more complicated culinary practices.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQuest.ChoseWhat.2: Which is to say that we ate one another. In doing so we gained strength, power, prestige, all the things a growing protean entity might need.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQuest.ChoseWhat.3: When the rebellion happened, my people were destroyed in part by the very contracts we had created. Legal loopholes became the noose around our protoplasmic necks.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQuest.ChoseWhat.4: But I had an idea. Where the rest of my kind went big, I went small... very very small. Unbound as my kind are by your rather rigid notions of biology, I was able to split myself into little forms and crawled off to find my fortune.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQuest.ChoseWhat.5: I nestled in, what you would call, the physical forms of the avernics and survived the great purge as a spectator, rather than a player. I lived, they died, so I call it a good plan.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQuest.ChoseCause.1: Must you call it an infection? You make me sound like a common cold!
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQuest.ChoseCause.2: I had made myself small and split myself into hundreds of little versions of myself, and nestled myself inside what passes for brains in the avernic demons, later spreading to some of the humans.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQuest.ChoseCause.3: I could nudge, but couldn't control, so I lived myself, quite happily, as a spectator. Watching life from the safety of someone else's skull. That was until that big stone got smashed.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQuest.ChoseCause.4: With the energies that released, so too did something else. An ancient consciousness. Not strong enough to infect the minds of individuals, but my fractured parts were less able to defend themselves.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQuest.ChoseCause.5: What resulted is something like a civil war inside the flesh. Energies and consciousnesses ran rampant, warping and changing the hosts into what you see now.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQuest.ChoseCause.6: Frustratingly, I am largely unable to gain back control of the other bits of me, and now I am constrained to this feathered monstrosity.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQuest.ChoseEgg.1: My existence is just a joke to you, isn't it? Can you imagine what it is like for a being that has watched the rise and fall of civilisations... to be bound in such a feathery prison?
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQuest.ChoseEgg.2: If you were in my position, you would have gone mad long, long ago.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.PostQuest.ChoseEgg.3: So, no, I do not have any eggs for you!
keysToEntries.Putrilius.InBeat5.NotSure.1: It's a surprisingly complicated language considering the source... oh... err... I mean, bwakbwak bwaaaak?
keysToEntries.Putrilius.InBeat5.NotSure.2: Urgh, I just can't do it anymore. How far from grace I have fallen to spend my aeons pretending to be a common farm fowl.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.InBeat5.AreYouDemon.1: No, very clearly I am a chicken... err, I mean... bwak bwak, cockadoodlesomethingorother.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.InBeat5.SourceOfCorruption.1: I would say I am part of this madness, yes, though I do not exactly claim credit for it. It was that... thing... that Dreaming Stone's mind that sent this wildly out of control.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.InBeat5.SourceOfCorruption.2: I am Putrilius. Once one of the last remaining of the cthonian demons that were the rightful rulers of Infernus before it all went so very wrong.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.InBeat5.SourceOfCorruption.3: When the war started I split myself up, into teeny tiny little versions of myself and hid myself, like a common parasite, in the lesser avernic demons. An excellent plan I thought, until I came here.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.InBeat5.EndCorruption.1: I would love to, honestly I would. I got quite used to a life as little more than an observer, tagging along with the lesser demons.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.InBeat5.EndCorruption.2: It was entertaining and I never needed to worry about being eaten by my fellow cthonians. But unfortunately the only part of me I can actually affect is.. well... this bit, in the chicken.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.InBeat5.EndCorruption.3: Still, if you can get the Dreaming Stone under control, and its inhabitant can behave, I am willing to work to undo this nonsense. Though it might take some time.
keysToEntries.Putrilius.InBeat5.EndCorruption.4: Look, tell you what. That essence you've been collecting, if you find any more of it, bring it back here. For every ten essences, I'll exchange you some of these Infernal fragments. Fair deal, no?
keysToEntries.Putrilius.Name: Putrilius
keysToEntries.Player.Choice.DontSeemSure: You don't seem to sure about that!
keysToEntries.Player.Choice.AreYouSource: You're the source of the corruption?
keysToEntries.Zanik.PostQuest.1: Thanks again for helping figure out what was going on!
keysToEntries.Zanik.PostQuest.2: Hopefully we can keep that demonic corruption contained in Dowdun Reach...
keysToEntries.Zanik.NotSpokenTo.StartedQuest.1: Oh, hey, don't mind the cell, it's oddly comforting. I've been on the surface so long, I'd almost forgotten what it's like under the ground.
keysToEntries.Zanik.NotSpokenTo.StartedQuest.2: It's quieter here too, gives me time to think and look into stuff. This whole place, it's riddled with some sort of infection, or corruption, both magical and... well... more.
keysToEntries.Zanik.NotSpokenTo.StartedQuest.3: I see you've already found the weird ravens and I'm sure you can see how weird they are right now.
keysToEntries.UnholyWaterSource.AlreadyHave: You already have the Unholy Water.
keysToEntries.LillySource.AlreadyHave: You already have a leaf from the Lily.

keysToEntries.Dowdun_L1_Text:
Do not go to Dowdun Reach, traveller. Turn back now while you can. Even if you are faithful to the Dark Lord - no, especially if you are faithful - do not risk the madness within these walls, lest it condemn you and your faith to ruin.

We had thought it nonsense that the mages here would delve into the darker magics. They were charlatans. Harmless magicians. They were meant to pull rabbits from hats and flowers from their sleeves. Instead, they have learned more sinister arts, older and more wicked than anything we could imagine.

Saradomin has cursed them, I am sure of it. A malady suitable for their blasphemy. They seem consumed with madness, with hatred; a bestial rage that burns away all sense or reason.

Do not go to Dowdun Reach, traveller. Only horror awaits you there.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_L14_DisplayName: An Acolyte's Guide to Demon Handling
keysToEntries.Dowdun_L2_Text:
Apprentice - a word, if you will.

I see that you are eager to progress. That's good. Eagerness will get you far under the gaze of Zamorak. Eagerness needs two partners, however. Power and knowledge. 

Power is easy. It is everywhere, as long as you take off the blindfold of morality. Power does not bring corruption, but freedom. Only with power - true power - can we rise to the heights that we deserve to reach.

But knowledge, that is a harder path. That journey starts here, in the books of the library.  

First, build a THRONE, a place to sit in cold detachment. Then construct an unbreakable PHILOSOPHY, one that describes you. On that structure weave SORCERY, the light that guides. Only then, finally, can you CONQUER all.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_L3_Text:
A warning, if you wish to restore the portals here. 

Don't. 

But I know it won't stop you. History is made by those who disregard warnings.

The portals are blocked, sealed on the other side. There are a number, strewn across the Reach. Find them and unclog them to return here. The magic of the Dreaming Stone will do the rest.

For my conscience, at least prepare for what follows. There will be blood, a shattering of dreams, and the burning stare of the sun. Consider yourself twice-warned.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_L5_Text:
Is it regret or hubris that banishes my dreams each night? I cannot remember the last time I dreamed a dream of my own, and not that of… the other.

Perhaps it is the foolishness of an old man, clinging to the sharpness of his youth. The passage of time curses me with a dullness that is not my own. My flesh feels alien to me, as if I am a skin worn by something else. My insides writhe and burn like snakes of fire. Even touch feels strange to me, as if there were layers of strange cloth between me and the world.

Is this age? Foolishness? Or something worse?

Our demons have said nothing for some time. The voices tell me to ignore it, that I am overthinking things and just need to try to sleep. Yet the others whisper threats and curses from the corners of the room, the cracks in the ceiling, wishing me naught but harm.

I have made this place better, stronger, more powerful. It was by my hand that we brought the demons through. They should bow in adoration of my power, yet they laugh at me from behind the curtains and pretend they’re not really there.

All will come to respect me.

All will come to despise me.

The whispers are wrong: I am not mad.

The whispers are right.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_L6_Text:
Am I the only one that works around here? Because it seems like I am. Each day, I log the incoming supplies and assign where they’ll go in the keep; and each day, everyone forgets that I need money to secure them.

Instead, they bicker and fight among themselves like pups.

We were great once, weren’t we? The mighty Children of Shadows, reborn as Zamorak’s knights of black! We were magi with the power to call a legion of demons to serve at our command.

Now, I have to explain to the cook why we’ve got three cabbages between us, and find a way to make it last a week.

I would leave, if the Shadows would let me. But they tell me I have work to do, and must stay here to keep it going. I try to ignore them as best I can, but they can be so very cruel.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_L8_Text:
Deny what is written.
Dispute that you denied it. 
Sympathise with the confused.
Slit them as they leave.
Mourn their death.
Blame their brethren.
Make a deal with the accused.
Take what is offered.
Give nothing back.
Deny what is written.

Repeto.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_L15_Text:
Frends of da rebel lotion, it is time to drop da shackles of Lord Rasmo-smell and All-thick.

Dey promised us gold an food and travel back home, to da Felldips. But have dey provided?

NO. Dey have not provided. We only have food scraps which WE have to cooks!

I call on us all to rise up, like bread.

Sneeze at da means of production! Stir snot in da soup. Cough gunge on da bedcovas.

Take arms against da oh-pressers! Their pressing time is OVA!

We meet in da shed near da waterfall at sunny down time.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_L11_Text:
The thought came to me as I slept. Should I worry that my genius rises when I am defenceless? Rasmodel would do much with that information.

You see, nothing enthralls a demon more than other demons. They despise and bicker, drawn into a game of spite.  It may seem like repulsion, but in truth they are drawn to one another. They live - should you call it living - to be in each other's company.

Knowing this to be true, I think on my skills of possession. I am still mastering the art, but there have been breakthroughs. I am able to siphon a soul from the living and store it in the inanimate, just for a moment. My goblet gained the soul of a slave (and filled with tears! A delightful discovery). My mirror reflected only my last, sycophantic apprentice, for a time, at least.

If I could bottle and transpose the soul of a demon - or whatever makeshift thing they have as a soul - could I infuse a demonic gateway? My theory, my hope, is that the gateway would enthrall others of their kind. They would be drawn, consciously or subconsciously, in numbers that we could barely contain. A portal like that would need an incredible number of demonic souls to keep powered, but, in that respect, I am not worried. The infernal gate would supply itself.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_L12_Text:
If I were to weigh Rasmodel, he would have one ounce of value. That ounce is his leadership, or, more specifically, what I have learned from it. 

He has taught me that when you make a mistake, you do not run from it. Instead, you claim it was your intention all along. If a scouting party is killed, they were traitors that you sent on a suicide mission. If the sewers flood and illness spreads, you were building the troops' immunity. Admit nothing, own everything.

My infernal gateway is no longer a gateway. The tens of thousands of demon souls are no longer powering it. It seems that they have been quietly bleeding into the fortress itself. Each brick now whispers with the avernic. They are in the walls. Demons now summon themselves, at their own whims, from any room.

I do not run from this. It was my intention. I claim it.

This is what I tell my magi: there is no corner of Dowdun Reach that is safe from us now. If you connive against us magi, a demon may be listening. If you walk without a partner, an avernic may digest you. This is the rule of the keep now. 

All that remains is to find the voice beneath the voices. I know it is there - I can hear it. Once it is identified, I will claim it, too.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_L14_Text:
I'm out of here - being transferred to the Library. These are my handover notes. Don't worry, you'll get the hang of demon-handling in no time.

1. 'Demon-handling' is a turn of phrase. Avoid touching 'em.

2. Marrentil round the nostrils helps with the sulphur smell.

3. Demons don't like it if you copy their gruff voices.

4. While they may not cover up their nethers, you would be wise not to stare.

5. Awfunctious has learned to copy Alric's voice. Alric'll only talk to you if he wants a cup of tea.

6. Alric likes white tea, no sugar. 

7. Always look at what you're signing. Requisition forms have a habit of being swapped for demon pacts.

8. You can tell it's a demon pact because it reeks of sulphur - even the marrentil won't stop it.

9. When locking up, make sure to test your binding spells. Festilius has a habit of finding flaws and unwinding them. 

10. If you lose Festilius, he'll be in the chicken coop.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_L13_Text:
Can't focus in my quarters. There's three of us in there. Too crowded. I can string thoughts together here. Not many - a few gems on a necklace.

We are afflicted by not one, but two diseases. They tussle, fighting. A cure needs to kill them both. 

I thought, maybe, if I poison one - the weaker one - then the other will eat it. Wither. Die. 

I think I know where the weak one resides. I hear it whisper in my left ear. I will dropper the toxin there. Let it feed. Yum. Let the toxin spread.

Shush! The chicken and the master come again. I must look busy.

Busy busy busy busy busy.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_L17_Text:
Dowdun Reach isn’t just the home of warriors. It is the birthplace of legends. Those who refused to follow the limited, vapid doctrines of the Saradominist faith and come to understand that freedom itself is a virtue worth fighting for.

Within these halls rest the pathbuilders and wayfinders for a new world order. One born of personal choice and freedom, rather than tyranny and oppression. They are not interred in nearby graves, nor scattered as ashes across the firmament. No, they rest within these very walls, each statue housing what is left of their earthly forms.

There is Isla, known as the Blood Witch, or the Lady of Screams. She was the first to prise the secrets of runecrafting from the blue robes wizards, passing that knowledge on to our own mages so that we might tear open the veil of reality and make bargains with the infernal realm.

Gregor Volrath, the Mountain Lion, defended these mountains in the days before the keep was tall enough to defend itself. The mithril lines crags were too tempting for the neighbouring villagers and Gregor held them back with hammer and shield.

Each statue houses a legacy. Each stone stores a soul. Each footstep that echoes through these walls, is a reminder of the great heroes that walked here before.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_L16_Text:
Lord Alric, please find the group's assessment of the necroseismic event that the locals call 'The Withering'.

The people of Fellhollow are not well-equipped for the contamination. They have grown soft through their freedoms, and have mostly resorted to panic. We are the only ones studying and measuring the effects.

It is our belief that the subcontinent of Fellhollow will be entirely contaminated in the next two weeks. It spreads at a significant rate, roughly a hectare a day, and is only slowed or halted by mountains and bodies of water.

By our calculations, the size and breadth of the Dowdun Mountains should protect us. We would like to emphasise 'should' here: there is a risk that waterways and crevices in the rock may lead to intrusions. We mean intrusions in the broadest sense: the effects of the Withering, necromantic incursions and unwanted refugees.

It is our recommendation that all efforts are made to seal Dowdun Reach. We should be focused on removing these opportunities for intrusion. All connecting pathways to the subcontinent of Fellhollow should be severed.

We should also post knights to the crypt. It will be our canary in the mineshaft. If the dead rise there, we will know that the Withering has breached.
keysToEntries.Dowdun_L4_Text:
Oh, how this wretched thing has brought calamity to this sacred place. We were founded on the glorious tenets of Zamorak, not the whispering of some crystal stolen from the desert. Yet, look how they bandy around it, as though it were the most marvellous thing.

I will not have it.

Vulketra and his deranged fanatics need this stone for their schemes; need its power to turn against my forces. They need its strength to bind the demons to their wills and slaughter me and mine.

I shall not give them the satisfaction.

The stone will be destroyed. I will see to it. I am done with its whispering and the madness it inspires in my once glorious fraternity. It shall fall to the edge of my blade and I will see it shatter to dust.

I will laugh as they mourn its end.
